## 5. Federal Court Immigration Case Backlog (Western District of Texas, El Paso Division)

- From 2021-2024, El Paso federal judges handled an average of **26 habeas corpus petitions per year**.
- In **2025**, that jumped to nearly **300** in El Paso alone (759 across the entire Western District, a record).
- In **January 2026**, habeas filings came in at a pace that would yield over **2,800 such filings for the year** -- a 100x increase over pre-2025 levels.
- In January 2026, habeas corpus petitions accounted for **75% of all new civil cases** in the Western District of Texas.
- Only **two district judges** (Kathleen Cardone and Leon Schydlower) handle full caseloads in El Paso.
- Over **1,300 habeas petitions** filed by ICE detainees in the Western District since mass deportations began.

Sources:
- [Border Report: El Paso courts deluged with detention challenges](https://www.borderreport.com/hot-topics/immigration/el-paso-west-texas-federal-courts-deluged-with-challenges-to-immigration-detention/)
- [El Paso Matters: Habeas corpus filings surge](https://elpasomatters.org/2026/02/01/el-paso-habeas-corpus-filings-surge-ice-detainees-released/)
- [El Paso Matters: Judge Briones on Trump-supported immigration rulings](https://elpasomatters.org/2026/02/03/el-paso-judge-david-briones-trump-immigration-bond-cases/)

---

## 6. Cross-Border Impacts and Port of Entry Changes

- **Bridge of the Americas (BOTA) modernization**: GSA posted the RFP on **January 26, 2026**; construction begins late 2026. GSA proposes **permanently banning commercial truck traffic** from BOTA (effective after 2028). New pedestrian processing lanes, primary building, and passenger vehicle lanes planned.
- Commercial traffic will be rerouted to the **Santa Teresa Port of Entry** (NM), with a **7.5-mile connector highway** (Phase 1 contract letting in November 2026).
- **Bishop Mark Seitz** (El Paso Diocese, chair of USCCB Committee on Migration) led "**Aqui Estamos: March and Vigil to Stand with Migrants**" at Sacred Heart Church on **March 24, 2026**, drawing hundreds. Seitz called mass deportations a "war on the poor."
- **February 24, 2026**: 20 border-state Catholic bishops issued a joint statement hours before Trump's State of the Union address urging humane immigration reforms.

Sources:
- [GSA: BOTA modernization](https://www.gsa.gov/real-estate/ongoing-construction-projects/bridge-of-the-americas-land-port-of-entry)
- [Border Report: Permanent truck ban at BOTA](https://www.borderreport.com/news/trade/feds-propose-permanent-truck-ban-at-el-paso-port-of-entry/)
- [NCR: Hundreds join bishop's protest](https://www.ncronline.org/news/hundreds-join-el-paso-bishops-protest-against-migrant-mass-deportation-asylum-bans)
- [El Paso Matters: Border bishops call for humane policies](https://elpasomatters.org/2026/02/27/border-catholic-bishops-el-paso-immigration-humane-enforcement-call-to-action/)

---

## 7. Additional Named El Paso Incidents (Timeline)

| Jan 3, 2026 | Death of Geraldo Lunas Campos (ruled homicide Jan 21) | Camp East Montana, Fort Bliss |
| Jan 2026 | TB (2 cases) and COVID-19 cases reported at Camp East Montana | Camp East Montana |
| Jan 10-19, 2026 | Nine-day operation: 38 immigrants arrested at 3 construction sites | El Paso + Santa Teresa, NM |
| Jan 17, 2026 | DHS purchases warehouses near Clint for $123M mega-facility | Far East El Paso County |
| Jan 29, 2026 | City Council considers ban on ICE detention centers | El Paso |
| Jan 30, 2026 | National Shutdown: hundreds participate, student walkouts | El Paso (Eastwood, Bel Air HS) |
| Feb 2, 2026 | City Council moves to reject ICE mega-facility | El Paso |
| Feb 6, 2026 | DHS warehouse purchase for mega-facility reported | Clint area |
| Feb 26, 2026 | 17 measles cases reported (13 at East Montana) | El Paso |
| Mar 3, 2026 | Measles count at 14 confirmed at camp, 112 in isolation | Camp East Montana |
| Mar 4, 2026 | Reports: ICE moving toward closing Camp East Montana ($1.2B contract termination draft) | Camp East Montana |
| Mar 6-7, 2026 | 911 call records reveal attempted suicides, fights, medical emergencies | Camp East Montana |</result>

US hub page is created. Good news — all 4 regional research agents have completed with excellent data. Let me check on the Texas statewide agent, then address your question about images.

**Re: images** — great idea. For free, attribution-required photography:
- **Unsplash** — free, no API key needed, attribution appreciated but not required
- **Pexels** — free, no API key needed, attribution not required
- **Wikimedia Commons** — CC-licensed, attribution required, excellent for news/documentary photos
- **Flickr Creative Commons** — CC-BY/CC-BY-SA licensed, attribution required, great for protest/community photos

For ICE-related imagery specifically, Flickr CC and Wikimedia will have more relevant documentary photography (protests, enforcement, community responses). Unsplash/Pexels tend toward generic stock.

For now, let me continue building the Texas pages with the research data. I'll add placeholder image sections and we can source specific photos per region. Let me check the Texas statewide research agent and start building the Texas hub.

task.prompt:
Research ICE enforcement activity in Texas statewide for January-March 2026. I need:

1. **SB 8 (Texas immigration law)** — what it requires, enforcement deadline, which counties have complied
2. **Governor Abbott actions** — Operation Lone Star updates, any executive orders, DPS immigration enforcement
3. **DPS 287(g) program** — how many agencies signed, which counties, what it means
4. **Statewide enforcement stats** — total ICE arrests in Texas Jan-Mar 2026, deportation numbers
5. **School/church impacts** — any reports of ICE near schools or churches in Texas
6. **Construction/agricultural industry impact** — worksite raids, economic effects
7. **Deportation flights** — flights from Texas airports (San Antonio, El Paso, etc.)
8. **Legal challenges** — any federal court rulings affecting Texas immigration enforcement
9. **Sanctuary city status** — which Texas cities still resist ICE cooperation

Search for recent news from January-March 2026. Provide specific dates, numbers, and source URLs for everything. This will be used to create a sourced Texas statewide hub page for an ICE activity tracker.

<result>Now I have comprehensive data across all nine topics. Here is the full research summary:

---

# Texas Statewide ICE Enforcement Activity: January-March 2026

## 1. SB 8 (Texas Immigration Law)

**What it requires:** Senate Bill 8, passed during the 89th Texas Legislature, mandates that all Texas sheriffs who operate or contract with county jails must request and enter into federal immigration law enforcement agreements with ICE under Section 287(g) of the Immigration and Nationality Act. Sheriffs must choose an operational model (jail-based or task force) that defines their scope of authority.

**Effective date:** January 1, 2026, with a **full compliance deadline of December 1, 2026**.

**Financial provisions:** A tiered grant program provides funding based on county population:
- Counties under 100,000: $80,000
- Counties over 1 million: $140,000
- Grants cover officer compensation, training, equipment, reporting, and inmate confinement costs
- County commissioners are prohibited from reducing sheriff budgets in response to these grants

**Large county mandate:** Counties with populations over 100,000 **must** implement 287(g) agreements. Counties over 1 million (Harris, Dallas, Tarrant, Bexar, Travis, Collin) must pay their own way and are not eligible for the state grant. Smaller counties may participate voluntarily.

**Enforcement mechanism:** The Texas Attorney General is empowered to sue sheriffs who fail to comply.

**Current compliance status:** As of early 2026, Texas has **299 active 287(g) agreements across 241 agencies in 186 counties** (approximately 21% of all active 287(g) agreements nationwide). Harris and Dallas Counties do not yet have 287(g) programs but still cooperate with ICE through the older Secure Communities program. They must comply by December 2026.

Sources:
- [Texas Policy Research: SB 8 and 287(g) Agreements](https://www.texaspolicyresearch.com/senate-bill-8-and-287g-agreements-where-texas-stands-today/)
- [VisaVerge: Texas SB 8 Expands 287(g)](https://www.visaverge.com/news/texas-sb-8-expands-287g-task-force-model-to-large-counties/)
- [KERA News: Immigration advocates worry as SB 8 takes effect](https://www.keranews.org/news/2025-12-29/immigration-advocates-new-law-texas-sheriffs-ice-287g-senate-bill-8)
- [Attorney Daniels: Major Changes to Texas Immigration Law in 2026](https://www.attorneydaniels.com/post/understanding-the-major-changes-to-texas-immigration-law-in-2026)
- [ACLU of Texas: Status of 287(g) Agreements](https://www.aclutx.org/news/status-287g-agreements-texas/)

---

## 2. Governor Abbott Actions

**Five Executive Orders:** Governor Abbott issued five Executive Orders directing state agencies to partner with the Trump Administration on border and homeland security operations.

**Memorandum of Understanding (February 2025):** Abbott signed an MOU authorizing Texas National Guard soldiers to make immigration arrests and conduct operations with federal immigration officers. The agreement requires soldiers to be accompanied by a U.S. immigration officer or Border Patrol agent (ratio of 1 federal officer per 4 soldiers). This added at least **3,000 Texas Army National Guard soldiers** to immigration enforcement ranks. Texas is not reimbursed for costs.

**DPS Tactical Strike Teams:** Abbott directed DPS tactical strike teams (troopers, special agents, and Texas Rangers) to coordinate with Homeland Security agencies to track down **5,400 illegal immigrants with active warrants** across Texas. Teams are supported by DPS aircraft and intelligence divisions.

**Texas Tactical Border Force:** Deployed to work alongside U.S. Border Patrol agents at the border.

**Operation Lone Star cumulative totals (as of early 2026):**
- Over 531,300 illegal immigrant apprehensions
- More than 50,800 criminal arrests
- More than 43,500 felony charges
- Over 624 million lethal doses of fentanyl seized

Sources:
- [Governor's Office: Texas Partners With Trump Administration](https://gov.texas.gov/news/post/texas-partners-with-trump-administration-on-arrests-deportations)
- [Texas Tribune: Texas National Guard immigration arrests MOU](https://www.texastribune.org/2025/02/03/texas-national-guard-immigration-arrests/)
- [Newsweek: Abbott Gives Texas National Guard Sweeping New Powers](https://www.newsweek.com/greg-abott-texas-national-guard-immigration-arrest-2025059)
- [Fox News: Abbott orders DPS tactical strike teams](https://www.foxnews.com/us/texas-gov-abbott-orders-dps-tactical-strike-teams-assist-federal-illegal-immigration-enforcement-efforts)
- [Governor's Office: Operation Lone Star](https://gov.texas.gov/operationlonestar)

---

## 3. DPS 287(g) Program

**DPS-ICE agreements signed:** Between mid-October and early November 2025, DPS and ICE signed **two agreements** authorizing state police within DPS's Criminal Investigations and Highway Patrol divisions to effectively operate as ICE agents.

**Scope:** Traffic stops in Texas can now include immigration checks. DPS troopers on state highways can conduct immigration enforcement during routine stops.

**Statewide 287(g) footprint:** 299 active agreements across 241 agencies in 186 counties.

**SB 8 mandate:** All sheriffs operating jails in counties over 100,000 population must have agreements by December 1, 2026.

Sources:
- [Texas Observer: Texas Turns Its Sprawling State Police Force Into Immigration Agents](https://www.texasobserver.org/texas-dps-287g-ice-trump-abbott/)
- [News 4 San Antonio: Traffic stops could include immigration checks](https://news4sanantonio.com/news/local/traffic-stops-in-texas-could-soon-include-immigration-checks-under-new-program-san-antonio-texas-troopers-bexar-county-sheriff-deputies-investigation-state-highways)
- [News 4 San Antonio: Texas troopers to enforce immigration laws](https://news4sanantonio.com/news/investigations/texas-troopers-to-enforce-immigration-laws-under-new-federal-partnership-with-ice)
- [The Center Square: Texas law enforcement agencies with 287(g)](https://www.thecentersquare.com/texas/article_84221fad-efb6-422e-855a-993bbe9ceffe.html)

---

## 4. Statewide Enforcement Stats

**Daily arrest rate:** ICE arrests in Texas jumped from an average of **85 per day** (final 18 months of Biden administration) to **176 per day** (first six months under Trump).

**Texas share of national arrests:** Texas accounts for approximately **24% of all ICE arrests nationwide**. From Trump's inauguration through July 29, 2025, ICE made 138,068 arrests nationally, roughly 33,000 in Texas.

**ICE arrest rate:** Nearly doubled from ~58 per 100,000 state residents to **110 per 100,000**.

**January 2026 national detention bookings:** 39,694 people booked into ICE detention (36,099 by ICE, 3,595 by CBP).

**Texas detention population (February 2026):** More than **18,700 people** detained in Texas ICE facilities.

**National detention population:** ~73,000 individuals in ICE custody (a record high, 84% increase from same period in 2025).

**North Texas raids (January 2026):** ICE arrested **84 individuals** across Dallas, Irving, Arlington, Fort Worth, Garland, and Collin County.

**New arrest tactics:** ICE agents arresting people at federal buildings (El Paso), immigration courts (San Antonio), and probation offices (Dallas) -- described as unprecedented by attorneys and advocates.

**Detainee deaths:** Six people died in Texas ICE custody in a six-week span (mid-December 2025 through January 2026), three at Camp East Montana. One death (Geraldo Lunas Campos, 55, Cuban national) was **ruled a homicide** -- death by asphyxia while being physically restrained by law enforcement. This was the first homicide ruling for an ICE detainee death linked to staff in at least 15 years.

Sources:
- [Texas Tribune: What to know about ICE operations in Texas (Jan 19, 2026)](https://www.texastribune.org/2026/01/19/texas-immigration-ice-arrests-raids-police/)
- [KERA News: Immigration arrests quotas North Texas (Jan 28, 2026)](https://www.keranews.org/immigration/2026-01-28/immigration-arrests-quotas-north-texas-dallas-ice-tactics)
- [WFAA: ICE arrests 84 in North Texas](https://www.wfaa.com/article/news/local/ice-arrests-84-people-in-north-texas-during-immigration-enforcement-operations-agency-says/287-1b720e88-51a1-4060-9abc-fa6fd297233a)
- [CBS News: ICE detainee population record 73,000](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/ices-detainee-population-record-high-of-73000/)
- [Texas Tribune: ICE detention deaths Texas (Feb 19, 2026)](https://www.texastribune.org/2026/02/19/ice-detention-deaths-texas-east-montana-dilley-campos/)
- [NPR: ICE detainee death ruled homicide (Jan 22, 2026)](https://www.npr.org/2026/01/22/nx-s1-5685017/death-of-a-detainee-at-an-ice-detention-center-in-texas-is-ruled-a-homicide)
- [TPR: Six deaths in six weeks (Feb 20, 2026)](https://www.tpr.org/news/2026-02-20/six-deaths-in-six-weeks-what-to-know-about-ice-detentions-in-texas)

---

## 5. School/Church Impacts

**Policy change:** The Trump administration rescinded the 2011 "sensitive locations" policy in January 2025, removing protections that limited immigration enforcement at schools, churches, and hospitals.

**School impacts in Texas:**
- Agents seen circling school campuses, stalking school drop-off lines, and stopping school buses
- Significant decline in school attendance reported in immigrant communities

**Church impacts in Texas:**
- Faith leaders across Texas report declining congregation attendance and rising anxiety
- Immigrant congregations particularly affected

**Legal protection (February 14, 2026):** U.S. District Judge F. Dennis Saylor IV issued an injunction protecting certain churches, including the **Southwest Texas Synod** of the Evangelical Lutheran Church in America. The injunction blocks warrantless immigration enforcement not just inside houses of worship and social-service facilities (like daycare centers) but also on adjacent church property (parking lots) and **within 100 feet of a church's entrance**. Protected denominations include American Baptist Churches USA, Alliance of Baptists, Metropolitan Community Churches, and several ELCA regional synods.

Sources:
- [Reporting Texas: Faith Under Fire: How ICE Raids Affect Religious Practice in Texas](https://www.reportingtexas.com/faith-under-fire-how-ice-raids-affect-religious-practice-in-texas/)
- [Word&Way: Judge Freezes ICE Raids at Some Baptist, Lutheran, & MCC Churches (Feb 14, 2026)](https://wordandway.org/2026/02/14/judge-freezes-ice-raids-at-some-baptist-lutheran-mcc-churches/)
- [Texas AFT: Trump Eliminates Sensitive Location Protections](https://www.texasaft.org/policy/immigration/trump-administration-eliminates-sensitive-location-protections-for-public-schools-and-churches-in-immigration-orders/)
- [EdTrust: Separating Families, Terrorizing Communities, Upending Schools](https://edtrust.org/blog/ice-raids-upending-schools-communities/)

---

## 6. Construction/Agricultural Industry Impact

**Construction labor shortage:** At least **30% labor shortage** in the North Texas construction industry (per Dallas Builders' Association). Foreign-born noncitizens make up **38.6% of the Houston-area construction workforce** (U.S. Census Bureau).

**Project delays:** Basic 2,100-sq-ft homes that took 3-4 months now take **9-11 months**. Starter homes that took 4-5 months now take 8-10 months.

**Financial impacts:**
- Construction loans in South Texas down **30%** over the past year
- Residential construction in Hidalgo County dropped an estimated **30%**
- Supply chain companies reporting sharp revenue declines; some filing for bankruptcy
- Delays spreading into commercial developments, municipal projects, and new housing starts

**Agriculture:** Farm owners report up to **70% of workers** stopped reporting to work following ICE actions. Crops left rotting in fields.

**Disaster recovery impact:** A February 2026 survey found Texas disaster recovery efforts hampered by construction worker shortages tied to immigration enforcement.

**Political response:** Rep. Henry Cuellar (D-TX) asking federal officials to back off construction sites, citing economic harm.

Sources:
- [NewsNation: ICE raids at Texas construction sites hurting economy (Feb 2026)](https://www.newsnationnow.com/us-news/immigration/ice-raids-texas-construction-sites-economy/)
- [Texas Standard: Construction site ICE raids hurting economy (Feb 4, 2026)](https://www.texasstandard.org/stories/construction-site-ice-raids-hurting-economy-and-building-industry/)
- [CBS Texas: North Texas construction faces labor shortages](https://www.cbsnews.com/texas/news/immigration-tariff-policies-north-texas-construction-business/)
- [KVUE: Texas construction leaders say enforcement shrinking workforce](https://www.kvue.com/article/news/politics/immigration-news/texas-construction-leaders-immigration-enforcement-shrinking-workforce-slowing-projects/269-99015947-32db-4265-bb86-32c0d125246d)
- [Texas Tribune: South Texas homebuilders say ICE arrests slowed work (Dec 24, 2025)](https://www.texastribune.org/2025/12/24/south-texas-ice-arrests-home-construction/)
- [Newsweek: ICE Raids on US Farms Leaves Crops Rotting](https://www.newsweek.com/ice-immigration-raids-farms-crops-rotting-2092749)
- [Houston Public Media: Disaster recovery survey, construction worker shortage (Feb 2, 2026)](https://www.houstonpublicmedia.org/articles/news/business/2026/02/02/542355/texas-disaster-recovery-survey-construction-worker-shortage/)

---

## 7. Deportation Flights

**ICE Air Operations hubs in Texas:** San Antonio, Brownsville/Harlingen (Valley International Airport). Two of the five national ICE Air hubs are in Texas.

**Daily flight volume:** An average of **four deportation flights per day** depart Texas -- the highest of any state.

**National flight totals:** ICE carried out **2,138 deportation flights** between inauguration day (Jan 20, 2025) and end of 2025 (224 in December alone), a **44% increase** from 2024.

**Notable flight (January 2026):** 91 Colombian nationals boarded a Colombian military aircraft sent from Bogota at **Biggs Army Airfield** in El Paso.

**Planned facilities:** At least two new ICE processing/detention facilities planned in **McAllen** and **San Antonio**.

**Camp East Montana (El Paso):** The nation's largest ICE detention facility (averaging **2,954 detainees/day**), constructed in two months under a $1.2 billion contract with Acquisition Logistics (a company with no prior detention experience). Plagued by medical neglect, spoiled food, unsanitary conditions. **14 measles cases** confirmed March 3, 2026; 112 in isolation. Prior outbreaks of tuberculosis and COVID-19 in January. ICE moving toward **closing the facility** and terminating the contract.

Sources:
- [Border Report: ICE Air deportation flights from South Texas](https://www.borderreport.com/hot-topics/immigration/video-ice-deportation-flights-continue-from-south-texas/)
- [NOTUS: ICE deportation flights increased 44%](https://www.notus.org/immigration/ice-customs-enforcement-deportation-flights-increased-trump-white-house)
- [Border Report: Deportation flight to Colombia from El Paso](https://www.borderreport.com/news/politics/deportation-flight-from-the-u-s-to-colombia-departs-from-el-paso/)
- [Texas Tribune: Camp East Montana possible closure (Mar 4, 2026)](https://www.texastribune.org/2026/03/04/texas-camp-east-montana-ice-detention-possible-closure/)
- [Texas Tribune: 14 measles cases at Camp East Montana (Mar 3, 2026)](https://www.texastribune.org/2026/03/03/texas-ice-detention-measles-east-montana-dilley-el-paso/)
- [CNN: Camp East Montana measles outbreak (Mar 4, 2026)](https://www.cnn.com/2026/03/04/health/texas-immigration-camp-measles)

---

## 8. Legal Challenges

**SB 4 (state criminal law for illegal border crossing):**
- July 2025: Fifth Circuit three-judge panel upheld injunction blocking SB 4, ruling it unconstitutional (federal preemption)
- August 29, 2025: Fifth Circuit vacated the panel decision and granted rare en banc rehearing
- **January 22, 2026:** Full en banc hearing held before 17 judges (12 Republican-appointed, 6 by Trump)
- SB 4 remains **NOT in effect** as of March 2026 pending the en banc ruling
- Key legal question: whether remaining challengers have standing now that the federal government no longer opposes the law
- A ruling could come in months, likely heading to the Supreme Court

**Habeas corpus surge:**
- El Paso federal courts received **759 habeas corpus petitions** in 2025 (a record)
- That annual record was broken **in the first month of 2026 alone**
- Federal judges increasingly ordering bond hearings and releases for ICE detainees
- Over **300 federal judges** have ruled against the Trump administration's detention policies; only 14 have upheld them

**Church protection injunction (February 14, 2026):** Federal judge blocked warrantless ICE enforcement at certain Baptist, Lutheran, and MCC churches (including Southwest Texas Synod), extending protection to parking lots and within 100 feet of entrances.

Sources:
- [Texas Tribune: Appeals court reconsiders SB 4 (Jan 22, 2026)](https://www.texastribune.org/2026/01/22/texas-immigration-law-senate-bill-4-5th-circuit-court-of-appeals/)
- [Houston Public Media: Federal appeals court rehears SB 4 (Jan 22, 2026)](https://www.houstonpublicmedia.org/articles/news/politics/immigration/2026/01/22/541298/texas-immigration-law-senate-bill-4-5th-circuit-court-of-appeals/)
- [El Paso Matters: Habeas corpus filings surge (Feb 1, 2026)](https://elpasomatters.org/2026/02/01/el-paso-habeas-corpus-filings-surge-ice-detainees-released/)
- [El Paso Matters: Trump immigration bond cases (Feb 3, 2026)](https://elpasomatters.org/2026/02/03/el-paso-judge-david-briones-trump-immigration-bond-cases/)
- [KSAT: Texas court district leads US in detention challenges (Feb 10, 2026)](https://www.ksat.com/news/texas/2026/02/10/texas-court-district-leads-us-in-immigrants-challenging-legality-of-federal-detention/)

---

## 9. Sanctuary City Status

**Texas has no formally declared sanctuary cities.** SB 4 (2017, separate from the current SB 4 being litigated) already banned sanctuary policies in Texas. SB 8 (2026) further eliminates any remaining non-cooperation by mandating 287(g) agreements.

**Current holdouts (not yet in 287(g) but cooperating through Secure Communities):**
- **Harris County** (Houston) -- no 287(g) agreement yet; cooperates via Secure Communities
- **Dallas County** -- no 287(g) agreement yet; cooperates via Secure Communities
- Both must comply with SB 8 by December 1, 2026

**Big-city police departments face a "difficult choice":** NBC News reported that Texas's big-city police departments (Houston, Dallas, San Antonio, Austin) face tension between the state mandate and community policing concerns, but the legal framework under SB 8 leaves little room for resistance.

**State posture:** Texas is among the top states nationally for ICE cooperation, alongside Florida, Tennessee, Pennsylvania, and Alabama.

Sources:
- [NBC News: Texas big-city police departments face difficult choice](https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/texas-vowed-cooperate-ice-big-city-police-departments-face-difficult-c-rcna245937)
- [Breitbart: No More Sanctuary Texas Sheriffs (Jan 1, 2026)](https://www.breitbart.com/border/2026/01/01/no-more-sanctuary-texas-sheriffs-mandatory-ice-partnership-in-sweeping-state-law-begins-today/amp/)
- [Migration Policy Institute: State and Local Authorities Central to Deportation Strategy](https://www.migrationpolicy.org/article/state-local-authorities-ice-immigration-enforcement)

---

## Summary of Key Numbers

| Metric | Value | Period |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Daily ICE arrests in Texas | 176/day (up from 85) | First 6 months under Trump |
| Texas share of national ICE arrests | ~24% | Jan 2025 - Jul 2025 |
| Texas ICE detainees | 18,700+ | February 2026 |
| National ICE detainees | ~73,000 (record) | February 2026 |
| 287(g) agreements in Texas | 299 across 186 counties | Early 2026 |
| Deportation flights from Texas | ~4/day (highest in US) | 2026 |
| Camp East Montana avg. population | 2,954/day | FY 2026 |
| Construction labor shortage (North TX) | 30% | 2026 |
| Construction loan decline (South TX) | 30% | Past year |
| Habeas corpus petitions (El Paso) | Record broken in Jan 2026 alone | January 2026 |
| ICE detainee deaths in Texas | 6 in 6 weeks | Dec 2025 - Jan 2026 |
| Measles cases at Camp East Montana | 14 confirmed, 112 isolated | March 3, 2026 |</result>

Community & Policy March 8, 2026

ICE Activity in Texas

Texas has become the epicenter of federal immigration enforcement under the Trump administration’s second term. With SB 8 mandating sheriff participation in 287(g), daily ICE arrests jumping from 85 to 176, and mega-detention facilities under construction, the state is experiencing enforcement at a scale not seen in modern history. This hub tracks statewide developments and links to detailed regional pages.

Report ICE Activity in Texas

If you witness or experience ICE enforcement, submit an anonymous report. See our Know Your Rights guide.

Regional Coverage

Austin / Central Texas

Travis, Williamson, Hays counties · APD policy changes · Student walkouts

Dallas–Fort Worth

100 arrests/day · Hutchins facility blocked · Dallas PD rejected 287(g)

Houston / Harris County

646 arrests in one week · HPD calls to ICE up 1,000% · East End raids

San Antonio / Bexar County

7-hour council meeting · $66M warehouse · Vehicle ramming · Lackland

Rio Grande Valley

Mariachi family detained · Dilley conditions · Worksite raids · LUPE

El Paso / West Texas

Camp East Montana homicide · Measles · $123M mega-facility · Court backlog

Senate Bill 8: Mandatory ICE Cooperation

Texas Senate Bill 8 took effect January 1, 2026, requiring every county sheriff in a county with a population over 100,000 to apply for a 287(g) agreement with ICE by December 1, 2026. Under 287(g), trained local officers can perform federal immigration enforcement functions — from checking immigration status of jail inmates to assisting with field arrests, depending on the agreement type.[1]

SB 8 provides three models of cooperation:

  • Jail Enforcement Model (least aggressive) — Deputies question inmates about immigration status inside the jail
  • Warrant Service Officer — Deputies serve ICE warrants on inmates already in custody
  • Task Force Model (most aggressive) — Officers assist with street-level immigration enforcement

Counties that fail to apply face potential lawsuits from the Texas Attorney General. The law effectively ends the ability of Texas counties to function as sanctuary jurisdictions.[2]

Agency 287(g) Status Model
Tarrant County SheriffActive since 2017, expanded Feb 2026Full
Bexar County SheriffActive (Oct 2025)Warrant Service Officer
El Paso County SheriffActive (Jan 2026)Jail Enforcement
Dallas County SheriffPartial (notifies ICE)No formal 287(g)
Harris County SheriffRequired under SB 8Pending (Dec 2026 deadline)
Travis County SheriffRequired under SB 8Pending
Dallas PDRejected $25M offerN/A (city police)
Arlington PDRejectedN/A
TX DPS + Pct 3 Constable (SA)ActiveTask Force (field)

Enforcement by the Numbers

Texas accounts for roughly one quarter of all ICE arrests nationwide. Key statistics from January 2025 through March 2026:[3][4]

Metric Number Context
Daily ICE arrests in Texas176/dayUp from 85/day under Biden
Dallas field office arrests (Jan–Oct 2025)12,100+108% increase year-over-year
Houston field office arrests (Jan–Oct 2025)17,500+Southeast TX to Waco
Houston week-long operation (Feb 23–Mar 2)646 arrested543 criminal, 7 gang members
Dallas arrestees without criminal convictions62%Up from 44% in 2024
Western District of TX immigration cases12,000+ in 2025240+ new cases first week of Feb 2026 alone
El Paso habeas filings~300 in 2025Up from avg. 26/year (2021–24)
DFW Dallas field office daily average~100/day2nd highest nationally

Mega-Detention Facilities

Texas is at the center of a $38 billion national plan for expanded immigration detention.[5] Three major facility developments are underway:

Camp East Montana (El Paso)

The nation’s largest ICE detention facility, a tent structure at Fort Bliss holding ~2,954 detainees per day. A detainee died in January 2026 — the El Paso medical examiner ruled it a homicide (asphyxia due to compression). A measles outbreak infected 14+ detainees. As of March 4, ICE is reportedly drafting termination of the $1.2 billion contract.[6]

San Antonio East Side Warehouse

ICE purchased a 640,000-sq-ft warehouse at 542 SE Loop 410 for $66 million (appraised at $37M). Planned as a 1,500-bed processing facility, operational by November 2026. City Council voted 8–2 to explore moratorium on detention facilities but acknowledged ICE doesn’t need local approval.[7]

El Paso / Clint Mega-Facility

DHS purchased warehouses near Clint for $123 million, planning an 8,500-capacity facility. El Paso City Council moved to reject it, and Socorro is also blocking. The three reinforced concrete warehouses total ~888,000 sq ft.[8]

Hutchins (Dallas) — Blocked

DHS identified a 1-million-sq-ft warehouse in Hutchins (pop. 6,000) for 9,500–10,000 beds. After intense community opposition, property owner Majestic Realty Co. rejected the sale on February 16.[9]

Economic Impact

ICE enforcement is having measurable economic consequences across Texas, particularly in the construction industry:[10]

  • 38.6% of Houston-area construction workers are foreign-born noncitizens (Census Bureau)
  • Construction loans in affected regions down ~30% year-over-year
  • 92% of construction businesses struggling to fill positions (AGC survey)
  • 28% of construction firms directly affected by ICE raids
  • Supply chain companies reporting sharp revenue declines; some filing bankruptcy
  • Arbor Realty Trust reports ICE activity directly impacting apartment occupancy in Houston, SA, and DFW
  • 1 in 7 Houston-area residents personally know someone detained or deported (Rice/Kinder Institute survey)
  • Among Hispanic Houstonians: 25% know someone detained
“There has to be some sort of warrant that needs to be presented when these federal agents show up to construction sites. And there’s no warrants being presented.” — Mario Guerrero, South Texas Builders Association

Community Response

Student Walkouts (January–February 2026)

Thousands of Texas students walked out of schools across the state to protest ICE operations. Walkouts occurred at campuses in Dallas, Fort Worth, Houston, San Antonio, Austin, and El Paso. The protests were triggered in part by the January 7 fatal shooting of Renee Nicole Good by an ICE agent in Minneapolis. Texas AG Paxton launched investigations into three school districts, and TEA threatened penalties including possible state takeover.[11]

January 30 National Shutdown

Texas saw some of the largest participation in the January 30 National Shutdown. Hundreds marched in San Antonio (Travis Park), Houston (Galleria), El Paso, Dallas, and Austin. The nationwide strike called on supporters to stay home from work and school.[12]

Faith-Based Response

In DFW, 40 multi-faith clergy formed CLEAR (Clergy League for Emergency Action and Response), operating a respite center behind the Dallas ICE field office serving 50–100 people daily. In El Paso, Bishop Mark Seitz led hundreds in a march calling mass deportations a “war on the poor.” Dallas faith leaders were central to blocking the Hutchins detention facility.[13]

Bexar County Judge Sakai

On January 24, Bexar County Judge Peter Sakai compared ICE raids to Japanese American internment camps, drawing national attention.[14]

Key Incidents (Statewide)

  2. NBC News: Texas vowed to cooperate with ICE. Big-city police face difficult choices. ↑
  3. KERA News: As immigration officials ramp up arrests in North Texas ↑
  4. NBC 5 DFW: Dallas ICE ERO says it makes the second highest number of arrests in the country ↑
  5. Axios: ICE reveals $38B plan for immigrant mega-jails ↑
  6. Texas Tribune: ICE moving toward closing Camp East Montana ↑
  7. KSAT: ICE confirms purchase of East Side warehouse ↑
  8. El Paso Matters: DHS buys warehouses for $123M mega-detention center near Clint ↑
  9. FOX 4: Texans block ICE detention center sale in Hutchins ↑
  10. Texas Standard: Construction site ICE raids hurting economy ↑
  11. Texas Tribune: AG Paxton probes three school districts over ICE walkout protests ↑
  12. KSAT: Hundreds march in San Antonio as part of nationwide protest against ICE ↑
  13. Presbyterian Outlook: Faith leaders form rapid response network in Dallas ↑
  14. KSAT: Bexar County Judge Sakai compares ICE raids to Japanese American internment ↑

Tracking Tools & Data

Structured Data

Deportation Data ProjectDownloadable enforcement datasets TRAC ImmigrationQueryable court & enforcement data CourtListenerFederal court opinions & filings API FlightAware ICE FleetLive deportation flight tracking (fleet code RPN)

Maps & Alerts

DetentionReports.comFacility-level detention data ResistMapCrowdsourced ICE sighting map DeFlock.meALPR camera location map ICE in My AreaSMS alerts for ICE sightings

Texas-Specific

Legal & Policy

Immigration Policy TrackingStanford/Yale executive action tracker ACLU Immigrants’ RightsLegal challenges & FOIA documents

Know Your Rights

  • •You have the right to remain silent
  • •You do not have to open your door
  • •Ask if you are free to leave
  • •Do not sign anything you don’t understand
  • •You have the right to an attorney
Full KYR Training Guide →

Support Our Community

All donations go 100% to mutual aid through Gifted Dreamers, Inc., an Austin, Texas-based 501(c)(3).

Donate Now

Share a Resource or Send a Message

Only message is required. All other fields are optional.

About justNICE

justNICE provides free, community-sourced immigrant resources and Know Your Rights training guides. We believe preparation is power and that every person deserves access to information that protects their rights.

Thousands of Texas students walked out of schools across the state to protest ICE operations. Walkouts occurred at campuses in Dallas, Fort Worth, Houston, San Antonio, Austin, and El Paso. The protests were triggered in part by the January 7 fatal shooting of Renee Nicole Good by an ICE agent in Minneapolis. Texas AG Paxton launched investigations into three school districts, and TEA threatened penalties including possible state takeover.[11]

January 30 National Shutdown

Texas saw some of the largest participation in the January 30 National Shutdown. Hundreds marched in San Antonio (Travis Park), Houston (Galleria), El Paso, Dallas, and Austin. The nationwide strike called on supporters to stay home from work and school.[12]

Faith-Based Response

In DFW, 40 multi-faith clergy formed CLEAR (Clergy League for Emergency Action and Response), operating a respite center behind the Dallas ICE field office serving 50–100 people daily. In El Paso, Bishop Mark Seitz led hundreds in a march calling mass deportations a “war on the poor.” Dallas faith leaders were central to blocking the Hutchins detention facility.[13]

Bexar County Judge Sakai

On January 24, Bexar County Judge Peter Sakai compared ICE raids to Japanese American internment camps, drawing national attention.[14]

Know Your Rights

  • •You have the right to remain silent
  • •You do not have to open your door
  • •Ask if you are free to leave
  • •Do not sign anything you don’t understand
  • •You have the right to an attorney

This is a regional ICE activity page for Dallas-Fort Worth as part of the justNICE.us ICE Activity Tracker.

## Template Pattern (MUST follow exactly)
Use the same HTML structure as the existing site:
- Tailwind CDN 3.4.17 with colors maroon: '#8B1A2B', gold: '#F5A623', font Inter
- Sticky maroon header with justNICE logo, same nav links as other pages
- Breadcrumb: Home / Blog / ICE Activity Tracker / Texas / Dallas-Fort Worth
- Article with category badge "Community & Policy", date March 8, 2026
- Content area with class "content-area prose prose-lg max-w-none text-gray-800 leading-relaxed"
- h2 for major sections (text-2xl md:text-3xl font-bold text-gray-900)
- h3 for incidents (text-xl font-semibold text-maroon) with date + location
- Footnotes section with numbered sources
- Standard justNICE footer with contact form (webhook: https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact)
- Mobile menu toggle script, contact form script, translations.js

## Content (from research)
Write these sections with the research data below. Each entry should have date, what happened, and footnote references to sources.

### Key Data Points:
- **84 arrests in one day** (Jan 26) across Dallas, Fort Worth, Arlington, Irving, Garland, Collin County
- **75 daily arrest target** set by internal ICE call
- **100 arrests/day** average by late 2025, 2nd highest nationally. Since Oct 2025, over 9,644 arrested.
- **108% year-over-year increase**: 12,100 arrests Jan-Oct 2025. 62% without criminal convictions (up from 44%)
- **Probation office arrests**: ICE entering Dallas County CSCD offices. Commissioner Elba Garcia raised alarm Nov 2025.
- **Courthouse arrests**: ICE arresting at Dallas courthouse. Sensitive locations policy revoked.
- **Construction site raids**: 38.6% foreign-born construction workforce. Loans down 30%. Companies filing bankruptcy.
- **Student walkouts**: Townview magnet (Feb 10), Booker T Washington, Fort Worth ISD (Jan 31). AG Paxton investigating 3 districts.
- **CLEAR**: 40 multi-faith clergy operating respite center behind Dallas ICE field office, 50-100 people daily, Monday prayer vigils
- **Hutchins detention center BLOCKED**: 1M sq-ft warehouse, 9,500-10,000 beds. Majestic Realty rejected sale Feb 16.
- **Dallas PD rejected $25M 287(g) offer**: Chief Comeaux, City Council backed him.
- **Tarrant County**: 287(g) since 2017, expanded Feb 10, 2026 (3-2 vote).
- **Arlington**: Mayor Jim Ross rejected ICE partnership.
- **Leqaa Kordia**: Palestinian woman, Prairieland Detention Center, hospitalized Feb 6 after seizure.
- **Dallas ICE Facility Shooting** (Sept 2025): 2 detainees killed.
- **Code complaints** against Dallas ICE field office at 8101 N Stemmons Fwy for overcrowding.

### Sources (use as footnotes):
1. WFAA: ICE arrests 84 in North Texas - https://www.wfaa.com/article/news/local/ice-arrests-84-people-in-north-texas-during-immigration-enforcement-operations-agency-says/287-1b720e88-51a1-4060-9abc-fa6fd297233a
2. NBC 5 DFW: Dallas ICE 2nd highest arrests - https://www.nbcdfw.com/news/local/dallas-ice-ero-says-it-makes-the-second-highest-number-of-arrests-in-the-country/3969068/
3. KERA: Immigration arrests quotas North Texas - https://www.keranews.org/immigration/2026-01-28/immigration-arrests-quotas-north-texas-dallas-ice-tactics
4. Hoodline: Dallas ICE Arrests Soar 108% - https://hoodline.com/2026/01/dallas-ice-arrests-soar-108-as-majority-nabbed-had-no-criminal-convictions/
5. KERA: Dallas County commissioner raises alarm over probation arrests - https://www.keranews.org/news/2025-11-14/dallas-county-commissioner-elba-garcia-raises-alarm-over-ice-arrests-in-probation-offices
6. Texas Tribune: AG probes school districts - https://www.texastribune.org/2026/02/16/texas-ken-paxton-investigation-isd-schools-immigration-protests-ice/
7. Presbyterian Outlook: CLEAR faith network - https://pres-outlook.org/2026/02/faith-leaders-form-rapid-response-network-for-migrants-facing-ice-detention-in-dallas/
8. FOX 4: Hutchins facility blocked - https://www.fox4news.com/news/texans-block-ice-detention-center-sale-hutchins-after-property-owner-rejects-offer
9. CBS Texas: Dallas PD rejected $25M - https://www.cbsnews.com/texas/news/dallas-police-department-ice-immigration-enforcement-offer-texas/
10. Fort Worth Report: Tarrant County grant - https://fortworthreport.org/2026/02/10/tarrant-county-seeks-state-grant-to-support-ice-partnership/
11. KERA: Arlington won't partner with ICE - https://www.keranews.org/news/2026-02-12/mayor-council-member-wont-consider-partnering-arlington-police-with-ice-for-enforcement-operations
12. KERA: Palestinian woman hospitalized - https://www.keranews.org/immigration/2026-02-09/palestinian-woman-in-ice-custody-near-dallas-hospitalized-legal-team-fears-for-her-health
13. D Magazine: Activists vs Dallas ICE field office - https://www.dmagazine.com/frontburner/2026/02/activists-want-the-city-to-enforce-code-against-the-dallas-ice-field-office-can-it-be-done/
14. Texas Standard: Construction ICE raids - https://www.texasstandard.org/stories/construction-site-ice-raids-hurting-economy-and-building-industry/

Two-column layout with resources sidebar on the right (same as texas.html). Include "Report ICE Activity" callout box. Include "Know Your Rights" quick card in sidebar.

## Template Pattern (MUST follow exactly)
Use the same HTML structure as the existing site:
- Tailwind CDN 3.4.17 with colors maroon: '#8B1A2B', gold: '#F5A623', font Inter

- Footnotes section with numbered sources
- Standard justNICE footer with contact form (webhook: https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact)
- Mobile menu toggle script, contact form script, translations.js

## Content (from research)

### Key Data Points:
- **646 arrests Feb 23-Mar 2**: 543 criminal, 7 gang members, 71 criminal warrants, 140 aggravated felony/violent, 34 sex offenses
- **17,500+ arrests** Houston field office region Jan-Oct 2025
- **HPD calls to ICE surged 1,000%**: 154 calls in 2025. 12 officers made 1/3 of calls. Officers driving detainees to ICE. Mayor said "violation of policy, will be corrected"
- **SB 8 mandates** Harris County 287(g) (ended 2017, now required again by Dec 2026)
- **East End raids**: Several men detained early morning. ~50 detained in Jan 2026
- **Greenspoint Park Immigration Court** arrests: 3 consecutive days Jun 2025, targeting people with admin-closed cases
- **Project Homecoming**: First self-deportation flight from Houston (64 people, May 2025). $1,000 stipend.
- **Avelo Airlines**: Signed ICE contract, then stopped after backlash
- **Student walkouts**: 14 campuses Jan 30. Cy Falls HS, HAIS, Conroe, Elkins. Abbott: "About time students like this were arrested"
- **Curtis Wright**: Legal permanent resident from Canada, detained Nov 6 at Bush IAH, trigger: single Xanax misdemeanor from 2003
- **Construction impact**: 38.6% foreign-born workforce, loans down 30%, bankruptcies
- **Rice/Kinder survey**: 1 in 7 Houston residents know someone detained. 25% of Hispanic residents.
- **Arbor Realty Trust**: ICE impacting apartment occupancy in Houston

### Sources (use as footnotes):
1. ICE press release: 543 criminal aliens arrested - https://www.ice.gov/news/releases/ice-arrests-543-criminal-aliens-7-gang-members-houston-area-during-week-long-multi
2. CW39: 646 arrests in week-long raid - https://cw39.com/news/local/ice-arrests-646-undocumented-houston-area-immigrants-in-week-long-raid/
3. Yahoo/Houston Chronicle: HPD ICE calls surged 1,000% - https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/houston-police-calls-ice-surged-120021632.html
4. ABC13: HPD violates policy - https://abc13.com/post/houston-police-department-relationship-ice-violates-policy-chronicle-report-says/18686247/
5. Click2Houston: 50 detained in Houston raids - https://www.click2houston.com/news/local/2026/01/08/50-people-detained-in-houston-area-immigration-raids-this-week/
6. Houston Public Media: ICE detains at Greenspoint court - https://www.houstonpublicmedia.org/articles/news/politics/immigration/2025/06/10/523600/ice-detains-multiple-men-at-houston-immigration-courthouse/
7. Texas Tribune: First self-deportation flight - https://www.texastribune.org/2025/05/19/texas-immigrants-self-deportation-flights-houston/
8. Houston Public Media: Hundreds protest ICE shooting - https://www.houstonpublicmedia.org/articles/news/politics/immigration/2026/01/09/540309/ice-shooting-minnesota-protest-houston-immigration/
9. Houston Public Media: HISD walkouts - https://www.houstonpublicmedia.org/articles/education/2026/02/10/543091/houston-isd-students-join-statewide-walkouts-protesting-ice-amid-threat-of-school-penalties/
10. Click2Houston: Curtis Wright detained - https://www.click2houston.com/news/local/2026/02/28/houston-family-fighting-to-free-legal-permanent-resident-held-in-ice-detention-for-months/
11. Texas Standard: Construction raids - https://www.texasstandard.org/stories/construction-site-ice-raids-hurting-economy-and-building-industry/
12. Rice/Kinder Institute survey - https://news.rice.edu/news/2026/mass-deportation-policies-personally-felt-1-7-houston-area-residents-according-kinder
13. The Real Deal: Arbor Realty ICE impact - https://therealdeal.com/texas/2026/03/03/ice-raids-rising-delinquencies-batter-arbor-realty-trust/
14. HILSC Houston Immigration Legal Services - https://houstonimmigration.org/

## Layout
Two-column with resources sidebar. Include report callout and KYR card. Write FULL HTML, no placeholders.

This is a regional ICE activity page for San Antonio / Bexar County as part of the justNICE.us ICE Activity Tracker.

## Template Pattern (MUST follow exactly)
- Tailwind CDN 3.4.17 with colors maroon: '#8B1A2B', gold: '#F5A623', font Inter
- Sticky maroon header with justNICE logo, same nav links
- Breadcrumb: Home / Blog / ICE Activity Tracker / Texas / San Antonio
- Article with category badge "Community & Policy", date March 8, 2026
- Content area with class "content-area prose prose-lg max-w-none text-gray-800 leading-relaxed"
- h2 for major sections, h3 for incidents (text-xl font-semibold text-maroon) with date + location
- Footnotes section with numbered sources
- Standard justNICE footer with contact form (webhook: https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact)
- Mobile menu toggle script, contact form script, translations.js

## Content

### Key Data Points:
- **7-hour City Council meeting (Jan 22)**: 180 speakers, 5+ hrs public comment. Chief McManus: 51,000 arrests, only 111 ICE detainers. Castillo called it "textbook fascism." Feb 12: resolution to fight facility. Mar 5: 8-2 vote on moratorium.
- **$66M warehouse (542 SE Loop 410)**: 640K sq-ft, 1,500 beds, operational Nov 2026. Appraised at $37M. Cuellar criticized.
- **Vehicle ramming (Jan 13)**: Robyn Argote Brooks, Walmart Blanco Road. Rammed 2 ICE vehicles, injured officer. $4,847 damage. Father says legal. DHS: 66 vehicular attacks since Jan 21 2025, rising to 180+ by Feb.
- **Viral home raid (early Feb)**: 3M views. Marcos Joel Contreras-Max and Edwin Chinchilla-Lopez arrested. ICE agents smashed cameras. Castro investigation. 92 detained charged with nothing.
- **Food truck raid (Nov 2025)**: 140+ detained, 51 TdA members. San Pedro/Basse. Agent destroyed camera at Las Pollitas.
- **Courthouse arrests**: ICE arrested people outside immigration court. Senator criticized.
- **Lackland AFB**: National Defense Area for migrant detention. 250 miles along Rio Grande. 3,000 migrants daily capacity. Echoes 2014.
- **ICE Air Operations**: SA is 1 of 5 national hubs. DHS bought 6 Boeing 737s.
- **SAPD policy**: Doesn't enforce immigration law, but SB 4 requires reporting. Only 111 of 51,000 arrests involved ICE.
- **Bexar County Sheriff**: 287(g) Warrant Service Officer since Oct 2025.
- **DPS + Pct 3 Constable**: Task Force Model (field enforcement).
- **Protests**: Jan 11, Jan 21, Jan 30 (hundreds at Travis Park), AFL-CIO resolutions.
- **Judge Sakai**: Compared ICE raids to Japanese American internment (Jan 24).
- **SA Rodeo/Fiesta**: ICE refused to say whether targeting. Chilling effect.
- **Construction raids**: South Texas Builders Association accusing ICE of warrantless raids.

### Sources:
1. TPR: 7-hour council meeting - https://www.tpr.org/news/2026-01-22/san-antonio-city-council-meeting-over-ice-cooperation-grows-contentious
2. KSAT: $66M warehouse - https://www.ksat.com/news/local/2026/02/05/ice-purchases-east-side-facility-to-hold-immigrant-detainees-agency-confirms/
3. DHS: Vehicle ramming - https://www.dhs.gov/news/2026/01/15/ice-arrests-illegal-alien-who-weaponized-his-vehicle-ramming-attack-law-enforcement
4. KSAT: Home raid viral video - https://www.ksat.com/news/local/2026/02/07/dhs-says-ice-was-attempting-to-arrest-immigrant-assailant-in-viral-san-antonio-home-raid-video/
5. SA Current: Detention moratorium vote - https://www.sacurrent.com/news/texas-news/san-antonio-city-council-votes-8-2-to-explore-moratorium-on-new-detention-centers/
6. SA Current: Lackland detention - https://www.sacurrent.com/news/san-antonios-joint-base-lackland-to-detain-immigrants-awaiting-deportation-37880506/
7. KSAT: Travis Park protest - https://www.ksat.com/news/local/2026/01/30/hundreds-marching-in-downtown-san-antonio-as-part-of-nationwide-protest-against-ice/
8. KSAT: Judge Sakai internment comparison - https://www.ksat.com/news/local/2026/01/24/bexar-county-judge-peter-sakai-compares-recent-ice-raids-to-japanese-american-internment-camps/
9. San Antonio Report: SAPD cooperation - https://sanantonioreport.org/san-antonio-police-enforce-immigration-laws-ice-city-council/
10. San Antonio Report: Bexar County 287(g) - https://sanantonioreport.org/bexar-county-precinct-balcones-heights-police-opt-into-immigration-enforcement/
11. Hoodline: ICE Rodeo/Fiesta silence - https://hoodline.com/2026/02/ice-silence-spurs-jitters-over-possible-raids-at-san-antonio-rodeo-and-fiesta/
12. SA Current: Warrantless construction raids - https://www.sacurrent.com/news/texas-news/south-texas-construction-group-accuses-ice-of-warrantless-raids-endangering-neighborhoods/
13. KENS5: Food truck raid - https://www.kens5.com/article/news/community/texas-video-san-antonio-ice-raid-food-trucks-agents-breaking-security-camera/273-11150acd-7a3d-4e4d-8120-b512ae835c21

This is a regional ICE activity page for the Rio Grande Valley as part of the justNICE.us ICE Activity Tracker.

## Template Pattern (MUST follow exactly)
- Tailwind CDN 3.4.17 with colors maroon: '#8B1A2B', gold: '#F5A623', font Inter
- Sticky maroon header with justNICE logo, nav links: Immigrant Resources, Know Your Rights, Blog (active with border-b-2 border-gold), Resource Map, NICE Gear, Contact, Español toggle, Donate button
- Breadcrumb: Home / Blog / ICE Activity Tracker / Texas / Rio Grande Valley
- Article with category badge "Community & Policy", date March 8, 2026
- Content area with class "content-area prose prose-lg max-w-none text-gray-800 leading-relaxed"
- h2 for major sections, h3 for incidents with date + location
- Footnotes with numbered sources and back arrows
- Standard justNICE footer with contact form (webhook: https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact), about section, copyright bar
- Mobile menu toggle, contact form JS, translations.js
- Two-column layout: article on left (lg:col-span-2), resources sidebar on right (lg:col-span-1)
- Include "Report ICE Activity" callout box and "Know Your Rights" quick card in sidebar

## Content

### Sections to write:

**1. Mariachi Family Detention (Feb 25, 2026)**
Gamez-Cuellar family detained at McAllen ICE check-in. Parents Luis Antonio and Emma Guadalupe plus sons Antonio (18), Caleb (14), Joshua (12). Caleb and Antonio in McAllen HS Mariachi Oro (8-time state champs). Family entered legally via CBP One app May 2023, passed credible fear, compliant 2 years. Final hearing Sept 2026. Caleb/Joshua/parents to Dilley. Antonio to El Valle Raymondville. Bipartisan condemnation: Rep De La Cruz (R) "heartbroken," Rep Gonzalez.

**2. Worksite Raids**
25 construction workers arrested Brownsville/South Padre. 12 in another operation. 9,000+ arrested RGV past year. STBA symposium Jan 20 requesting pause. 30-40% construction drop. 18 workers picked up at single site.

**3. LUPE Signs and Organizing**
Jan 19: LUPE report "10 Ways ICE Hit the RGV." Valle Fuerte Valle Libre campaign. Monarch butterfly/barbed wire yard signs at raid locations. Called worksite raids "out-and-out racism." Abby's Bakery raid Los Fresnos (Feb 2025): 8 workers, owners convicted, 6M views.

**4. Dilley Detention (South TX Family Residential Center)**
2,400 capacity, CoreCivic $160M/year. RAICES: 700+ medical complaints since Aug 2025. Children ill, metal bunks, worms/mold in food. ProPublica investigation Feb 9. Guards confiscating art, eavesdropping. Jan 24 mass protest "libertad" - triggered by 5-year-old Liam Conejo Ramos. Feb 1: 100-person vigil. Blue Butterfly Zones controversy.

**5. Border Patrol vs ICE**
RGV Sector leads nation in BP arrests FY2026. BP increasingly doing interior enforcement. Feb 24 meeting on enforcement fears.

**6. School and Church Impacts**
Sensitive locations policy rescinded. AG Paxton investigating 3 school districts. Pastor at Iglesia Cristiana Roca de Refugio: 12 members arrested, 7 deported since Jan 2025.

### Sources:
1. KRGV: Mariachi family identities - https://www.krgv.com/news/family-member-confirms-identities-of-mcallen-high-school-mariachi-students-detained-by-ice/
2. MyRGV: Bipartisan rebuke - https://myrgv.com/local-news/2026/03/07/mcallen-mariachi-familys-ice-detainment-sparks-bipartisan-rebuke/
3. MyRGV: RGV builders ask for pause - https://myrgv.com/local-news/2026/01/20/rio-grande-valley-builders-ask-for-immediate-pause-on-worksite-ice-raids/
4. ICE: 25 worksite arrests - https://www.ice.gov/news/releases/ice-rio-grande-valley-conducts-worksite-enforcement-operation-resulting-25-arrests
5. ICE: 12 arrested South TX - https://www.ice.gov/news/releases/12-arrested-south-texas-worksite-enforcement-operation-ice-rio-grande-valley-federal
6. LUPE: 10 ways enforcement hit RGV - https://lupenet.org/2026/01/19/10-ways-ices-aggressive-anti-immigration-enforcement-has-hit-the-rgv/
7. KRGV: Valle Fuerte campaign - https://www.krgv.com/news/valle-fuerte-new-lupe-campaign-focusing-on-immigration-arrests-in-the-valley
8. Texas Observer: Abby's Bakery - https://www.texasobserver.org/ice-raids-abbys-bakery-valley-los-fresnos/
9. ProPublica: Children describe Dilley - https://www.propublica.org/article/life-inside-ice-dilley-children
10. Democracy Now: Dilley protests - https://www.democracynow.org/2026/1/29/dilley_tx_ice_jail_family_detention
11. NBC News: Russian family Dilley nightmare - https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/russian-family-ice-detention-dilley-texas-nightmare-immigration-rcna258377
12. Border Report: RGV leads BP arrests - https://www.borderreport.com/hot-topics/immigration/rgv-sector-leads-nation-in-border-patrol-arrests-so-far-in-fiscal-year-2026/
13. NBC News: South Texas Trump voters seeing red - https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/texas-trump-vote-immigration-raids-rio-grande-valley-workers-rcna259790
14. Border Report: Pastor says ICE arresting congregants - https://www.borderreport.com/hot-topics/immigration/pastor-says-ice-arresting-congregants-at-check-ins/

**1. Camp East Montana (Fort Bliss) — Nation's Largest ICE Detention Facility**
Avg 2,954 detainees/day. Soft-sided tent structure. $1.2B contract with Acquisition Logistics (no prior detention experience).

Homicide of Geraldo Lunas Campos (Jan 3): 55-year-old Cuban. Medical examiner ruled homicide Jan 21: asphyxia compression of neck/torso. DHS claimed suicide. Witness: 5 guards held him down, one squeezed neck. Autopsy: abrasions, hemorrhage in neck, petechial hemorrhaging. Third death at facility.

Measles outbreak: Feb 26: 17 cases, 13 at camp. Mar 3: 14 confirmed, 112 isolated. Prior TB (2) and COVID in Jan.

911 calls (Mar 6-7): Attempted suicides, fights, medical distress. Weight loss, food shortages, private security force.

Possible closure (Mar 4): ICE drafting termination of $1.2B contract.

**2. Construction Raids**
Jan 10-19 nine-day operation: 38 arrested at 3 sites (El Paso + Santa Teresa NM). Videos circulating. 30-40% regional construction drop.

**3. $123M Mega-Facility near Clint**
Jan 17: DHS bought warehouses Far East El Paso County. 8,500-capacity planned. Three 296K sq-ft concrete warehouses. Part of $38B national plan. City Council moved to reject. Socorro blocking.

**4. National Shutdown (Jan 30)**
Hundreds participated. Student walkouts: Eastwood HS, Bel Air HS. Triggered by Renee Nicole Good shooting (Jan 7 Minneapolis). TEA threatened penalties.

**5. City/County Cooperation**
EPCSO: Jail Enforcement Model (least aggressive) under SB 8. City Council: considered ban on detention centers (Jan 29), moved to reject facility (Feb 2), voted to hold ICE accountable.

**6. Federal Court Backlog**
El Paso judges: 26 habeas/year avg 2021-24. 300 in 2025. Pace in Jan 2026 = 2,800/year. Habeas = 75% of new civil cases. Only 2 full-time judges. 1,300+ petitions filed.

**7. Cross-Border / Port of Entry**
BOTA modernization: permanent truck ban planned. Santa Teresa connector highway. Bishop Mark Seitz march Mar 24 "war on the poor." 20 border bishops joint statement Feb 24.

### Sources:

This is a regional ICE activity page for Austin / Central Texas as part of the justNICE.us ICE Activity Tracker. Note: the existing detailed report at /blog/atx-ice-activity.html covers Jan 10-Feb 10 2026. This page should LINK to that existing report and add context + any Feb-Mar updates.

## Template Pattern (MUST follow exactly)
- Tailwind CDN 3.4.17 with colors maroon: '#8B1A2B', gold: '#F5A623', font Inter
- Sticky maroon header with justNICE logo, nav links: Immigrant Resources, Know Your Rights, Blog (active), Resource Map, NICE Gear, Contact, Español toggle, Donate button
- Breadcrumb: Home / Blog / ICE Activity Tracker / Texas / Austin
- Article badge "Community & Policy", date March 8, 2026
- Content area class "content-area prose prose-lg max-w-none text-gray-800 leading-relaxed"
- h2 for sections, h3 for incidents
- Footnotes with numbered sources
- Standard justNICE footer with contact form (webhook: https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact)
- Mobile menu, contact form JS, translations.js
- Two-column: article lg:col-span-2, sidebar lg:col-span-1
- Report callout + KYR card in sidebar

## Content

Start with a prominent link to the existing detailed report:
"For the full Jan 10-Feb 10 timeline with 34+ sourced incidents, see our original report: ICE Activity Within a 2-Hour Radius of Austin, TX" linking to /blog/atx-ice-activity.html

Then add these sections:

**1. Overview**
Austin/Travis County has seen significant ICE enforcement escalation. APD changed its general orders on ICE cooperation. Travis County faces SB 8 287(g) mandate. Student walkouts, community organizing.

**2. Key Incidents (from the original ATX report)**
Summarize the major ones (don't duplicate the full report):
- Jan 5: Karen Gutiérrez-Castellanos + 5-yr-old US citizen deported after 911 call
- Jan 12-13: ICE chase on SH 130, TCSO confusion
- Jan 14: APD policy change on ICE cooperation (Chief Lisa Davis)
- Jan 18: ICE on SH 195 Williamson County
- Late Jan: Wrongful deportation of Austin teen (govt apologized)
- Jan 30: Student walkouts, East Austin rally (thousands)
- Feb 2026: 240+ new immigration cases first week of Feb (Western District TX)

**3. Policy Context**
- APD's new general orders: officers can cooperate with ICE warrants, must notify duty commander first
- Travis County Sheriff: required to enter 287(g) under SB 8 by Dec 2026
- Williamson County: already cooperating
- Hays County: SB 8 compliance pending

**4. Community Response**
- Thousands at East Austin rally led by TX Democrats
- Student walkouts across Austin ISD
- Austin immigration rights groups join national protests

**5. Ongoing Coverage**
This page will be updated as new incidents are reported. Link to the main report and back to TX hub.

### Sources:
1. Original ATX report - /blog/atx-ice-activity.html (internal link)
2. Texas Tribune: Students walk out - https://www.texastribune.org/2026/01/30/texas-students-protest-ice-capitol/
3. KUT: East Austin rally - https://www.kut.org/austin/2026-02-01/austin-tx-democrats-ice-rally-protest-texas-immigration-talarico-casar
4. CBS Austin: Wrongful deportation apology - https://cbsaustin.com/news/local/trump-administrations-apologizes-for-wrongful-deportation-of-austin-teen
5. KXAN: ICE on SH 195 - (reference from original report)
6. DOJ: 240+ new cases Western District - https://www.justice.gov/usao-wdtx/pr/first-week-february-brings-over-240-new-immigration-cases-western-district-texas
7. Texas Tribune: What to know about ICE in TX - https://www.texastribune.org/2026/01/19/texas-immigration-ice-arrests-raids-police/

Write FULL HTML file, no placeholders. Keep it concise since the main content is in the existing ATX report.

  1. Home

Blog

Analysis, research, and community resources

Research & Frameworks

You Can't Google What You Can't Name

The hardest part of finding help isn't the search. It's knowing what to search for. Why vocabulary is infrastructure, how humans and AI are missing different pieces of the same puzzle, and what happens when you find the word that turns isolation into connection.

March 8, 2026 Read more →
Research & Frameworks

The Psychology of Authoritarian Control: From Cults to Cognitive Warfare

Sixty years of research on cults, coercive persuasion, and undue influence already mapped the playbook that fifth-generation warfare deploys at scale. The BITE Model is a personal defense framework — and a diagnostic tool for recognizing when it’s happening to you.

March 8, 2026 Read more →
Research & Frameworks

The Tools Already Exist to Make Power Visible

30+ free, open-source tools for investigation, conflict monitoring, democratic oversight, and accountability. Financial crime trackers, real-time conflict maps, congressional transparency dashboards, media bias analyzers, environmental monitors. The list nobody compiled — until now.

March 8, 2026 Read more →
Research & Frameworks

How Isolated People Find Each Other

You don't need a movement. You need two people who didn't know they needed each other. The research on how isolated people find connection, build real community, and make the invisible network visible to itself.

March 7, 2026 Read more →
Research & Frameworks

You're Not Crazy. Here Are the Words for What You're Feeling.

If you know something is deeply wrong but can't name it — if you feel stuck between grief and action — this is for you. A guide to finding the words for what you're carrying, understanding why you feel paralyzed, and discovering you are not alone.

March 7, 2026 Read more →
Technology & Privacy

Your AI Chats Can Be Used Against You: What You Need to Know in 2026

Federal courts have ruled AI chats have no legal privilege. Learn how your conversations with ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini can be subpoenaed, monitored, and used as evidence — and what you can do about it.

March 6, 2026 Read more →
Technology & Privacy

Data Privacy & Sovereignty Best Practices (Feb 2026)

Practical steps for data privacy, minimization, encryption, and sovereignty to protect against surveillance capitalism.

February 11, 2026 Read more →
Research & Frameworks

Comprehensive Emergency Preparedness Guide for 2026

A guide synthesizing federal guidance and expert recommendations for civil unrest, supply chain disruptions, utility failures, communication outages, and financial system freezes. Layered redundancy: never rely on a single system for critical needs.

February 11, 2026 Read more →
Technology & Privacy

Privacy Protection: Actionable Steps for 2026

Based on Nicholas Merrill's privacy-first philosophy: concrete steps for phones (Phreeli, CalyxOS), WiFi, home devices, and proximity-based protection.

February 11, 2026 Read more →
Community & Policy

ICE Activity Within a 2-Hour Radius of Austin, TX

A comprehensive timeline of ICE operations, community protests, policy changes, and legal developments in and around Austin, Texas from January 10 to February 10, 2026.

February 10, 2026 Read more →
Community & Policy

ICE Activity in Minneapolis: Operation Metro Surge

A comprehensive account of Operation Metro Surge — the largest DHS enforcement operation in U.S. history — including fatal shootings, mass protests, legal battles, and community impact in Minneapolis from January to February 2026.

February 10, 2026 Read more →
Research & Frameworks

Rebuilding Resilience: Frameworks for Personal and Community Recovery

An annotated research compilation of academic frameworks, validated metrics, and practical models for understanding and measuring both personal psychological resilience and community-level resilience.

February 10, 2026 Read more →
Research & Frameworks

Fifth-Generation Warfare: The Invisible War Already Underway

A comprehensive guide to how nations are fighting — and losing — wars you can't see. Covering cognitive warfare, deepfakes, cyber sabotage, NATO's response, and the emerging 5GW paradigm.

February 10, 2026 Read more →
Technology & Privacy

Defeating Facial Recognition in an AI Surveillance Era

The proliferation of AI-powered surveillance has created an urgent need for counter-surveillance tools. A comprehensive breakdown of existing countermeasures, innovations, and concepts that don't yet exist but could prove critical.

February 9, 2026 Read more →
Technology & Privacy

Anti-Surveillance Technology & Wearables: Market Analysis

An in-depth look at the surveillance landscape, market sizes, key players, regulatory impact, and strategic opportunities in the growing counter-surveillance industry.

February 9, 2026 Read more →

input.content:
# n8n Workflow Spec: ICE Activity Tracker Daily Digest ## Overview Automated pipeline that monitors RSS feeds, structured APIs, and key pages for ICE enforcement news, generates daily state-classified digests, and formats draft HTML entries for the justNICE ICE Activity Tracker. ## Trigger - **Schedule Trigger**: Daily at 6:00 AM CT (UTC-6) - Optionally: Webhook trigger for manual runs ## Pipeline Stages ### Stage 1: RSS Feed Collection (Parallel HTTP Requests) **Node type**: HTTP Request (10+ parallel branches via SplitInBatches) | Feed | URL | Focus | |------|-----|-------| | Drop Site News | `https://www.dropsitenews.com/feed` | Immigration investigative | | ProPublica Immigration | `https://www.propublica.org/feeds/topics/immigration` | National investigations | | Texas Tribune Immigration | `https://www.texastribune.org/feeds/tag/immigration/` | Texas-specific | | KERA News Immigration | `https://www.keranews.org/immigration/rss` | DFW / North Texas | | El Paso Matters | `https://elpasomatters.org/feed/` | Border region | | Houston Public Media | `https://www.houstonpublicmedia.org/topics/immigration/feed/` | Houston area | | Border Report | `https://www.borderreport.com/feed/` | Border states | | ICE.gov News Releases | `https://www.ice.gov/newsroom/news-releases/rss` | Official ICE | | DOJ WDTX Press Releases | `https://www.justice.gov/usao-wdtx/press-releases/feed` | Western District TX | | ACLU Immigration | `https://www.aclu.org/issues/immigrants-rights/feed` | Legal challenges | **Processing per feed**: 1. HTTP Request node fetches RSS XML 2. XML node parses to JSON 3. Filter node: only items published in last 24 hours 4. Set node: normalize fields (title, link, pubDate, source, description) ### Stage 2: Structured API Checks **Node type**: HTTP Request 1. **CourtListener API** — Check for new immigration-related opinions - `GET https://www.courtlistener.com/api/rest/v4/search/?q=immigration+ICE+deportation&type=o&filed_after=YYYY-MM-DD` - Filter: opinions from last 24 hours 2. **TRAC Reports** — Check for new data releases - Scrape `https://tracreports.org/whatsnew/` for new immigration reports 3. **ICE.gov ERO Stats** — Check for updated enforcement stats - `GET https://www.ice.gov/spotlight/enforcement-and-removal-operations` ### Stage 3: Page Scraping (Biweekly) **Node type**: HTTP Request + HTML Extract 1. **DetentionReports.com** — Extract current detention population numbers 2. **ICE.gov Detention Management** — Current facility populations 3. **immpolicytracking.org** — New executive actions **Schedule**: These run every Tuesday and Friday only (not daily) to be respectful of source servers. ### Stage 4: AI Classification & Summarization **Node type**: AI Agent (Claude via Anthropic API) **Input**: All collected items from Stages 1-3, merged into single array **System prompt**: ``` You are an immigration enforcement news classifier for the justNICE ICE Activity Tracker. For each news item, determine: 1. Which US state(s) it relates to (use 2-letter abbreviation) 2. Category: raid, arrest, detention, deportation, legal, policy, community-response, economic-impact, facility, other 3. Severity: high (deaths, mass raids, new facilities, major policy changes), medium (individual incidents, local policy), low (routine, background) 4. A 2-3 sentence summary focusing on: what happened, where, when, how many people affected 5. Any specific numbers (arrests, detentions, facility capacity, etc.) Output as JSON array. ``` **Output**: Classified items with state, category, severity, summary, and source URL ### Stage 5: HTML Formatting **Node type**: Code (JavaScript) Generate HTML snippets formatted to match the justNICE ICE tracker entry pattern: ```javascript // For each classified item, generate: const entry = `

${item.date} — ${item.title}

${item.summary} [${item.footnoteNum}]

`; ``` Group entries by state, then by date (newest first). ### Stage 6: Digest Assembly **Node type**: Code (JavaScript) Compile entries into a daily digest email/notification: ``` ## ICE Activity Digest — [Date] ### High Priority - [TX] 646 arrested in Houston week-long operation (source: ICE.gov) - [CA] New detention facility proposed in Bakersfield (source: LA Times) ### By State **Texas** (12 items) - [HTML snippet for each entry] **California** (3 items) - [HTML snippet for each entry] ### New Legal Developments - [Federal court rulings] ### Data Updates - [New TRAC or detention data] ``` ### Stage 7: Notification **Node type**: Send Email (or Slack/Discord) Send digest to human reviewer with: - Summary of new items by state - High-priority items flagged - Ready-to-paste HTML snippets - Links to all source URLs ## Workflow Diagram ``` Schedule (6am CT) ├─→ [RSS Feeds x10] ──→ Parse XML ──→ Filter 24h ──→ Normalize ├─→ [CourtListener API] ──→ Filter new ──→ Normalize ├─→ [TRAC Check] ──→ Parse ──→ Normalize └─→ [ICE.gov Stats] ──→ Parse ──→ Normalize │ ▼ Merge All Items │ ▼ AI Agent (Claude) → Classify by state, category, severity → Summarize │ ▼ Code Node → Format HTML snippets per justNICE template │ ▼ Code Node → Assemble daily digest │ ▼ Email/Slack → Send to reviewer ``` ## Implementation Notes 1. **Rate limiting**: Add 1-2s delays between RSS fetches to avoid IP blocks 2. **Deduplication**: Use a Postgres table or n8n's built-in dedup to track seen URLs (prevent re-alerting on the same story) 3. **Error handling**: Each RSS feed should have its own error handler (some feeds may be temporarily down) 4. **Credential**: Use the n8n Anthropic credential for AI Agent node (Claude Sonnet for cost efficiency) 5. **Storage**: Consider saving all classified items to Airtable or Postgres for historical tracking 6. **Activation**: Start with manual testing before enabling the schedule trigger ## n8n Credential Requirements | Service | Credential Type | Notes | |---------|----------------|-------| | Anthropic (Claude) | API Key | For AI classification node | | Email (SMTP) | SMTP | For digest delivery | | CourtListener | API Key | Free tier available | | Postgres (optional) | Connection string | For dedup/history | ## Future Enhancements - Auto-publish low-severity updates directly to state pages (with human approval queue) - Monitor social media (Reddit, Bluesky) for ICE sighting reports - Integrate with ResistMap API for crowdsourced sighting data - Weekly summary post auto-generated for blog - Translation to Spanish using Claude

${item.date} — ${item.title}

${item.summary} [${item.footnoteNum}]

`; ``` Group entries by state, then by date (newest first). ### Stage 6: Digest Assembly **Node type**: Code (JavaScript) Compile entries into a daily digest email/notification: ``` ## ICE Activity Digest — [Date] ### High Priority - [TX] 646 arrested in Houston week-long operation (source: ICE.gov) - [CA] New detention facility proposed in Bakersfield (source: LA Times) ### By State **Texas** (12 items) - [HTML snippet for each entry] **California** (3 items) - [HTML snippet for each entry] ### New Legal Developments - [Federal court rulings] ### Data Updates - [New TRAC or detention data] ``` ### Stage 7: Notification **Node type**: Send Email (or Slack/Discord) Send digest to human reviewer with: - Summary of new items by state - High-priority items flagged - Ready-to-paste HTML snippets - Links to all source URLs ## Workflow Diagram ``` Schedule (6am CT) ├─→ [RSS Feeds x10] ──→ Parse XML ──→ Filter 24h ──→ Normalize ├─→ [CourtListener API] ──→ Filter new ──→ Normalize ├─→ [TRAC Check] ──→ Parse ──→ Normalize └─→ [ICE.gov Stats] ──→ Parse ──→ Normalize │ ▼ Merge All Items │ ▼ AI Agent (Claude) → Classify by state, category, severity → Summarize │ ▼ Code Node → Format HTML snippets per justNICE template │ ▼ Code Node → Assemble daily digest │ ▼ Email/Slack → Send to reviewer ``` ## Implementation Notes 1. **Rate limiting**: Add 1-2s delays between RSS fetches to avoid IP blocks 2. **Deduplication**: Use a Postgres table or n8n's built-in dedup to track seen URLs (prevent re-alerting on the same story) 3. **Error handling**: Each RSS feed should have its own error handler (some feeds may be temporarily down) 4. **Credential**: Use the n8n Anthropic credential for AI Agent node (Claude Sonnet for cost efficiency) 5. **Storage**: Consider saving all classified items to Airtable or Postgres for historical tracking 6. **Activation**: Start with manual testing before enabling the schedule trigger ## n8n Credential Requirements | Service | Credential Type | Notes | |---------|----------------|-------| | Anthropic (Claude) | API Key | For AI classification node | | Email (SMTP) | SMTP | For digest delivery | | CourtListener | API Key | Free tier available | | Postgres (optional) | Connection string | For dedup/history | ## Future Enhancements - Auto-publish low-severity updates directly to state pages (with human approval queue) - Monitor social media (Reddit, Bluesky) for ICE sighting reports - Integrate with ResistMap API for crowdsourced sighting data - Weekly summary post auto-generated for blog - Translation to Spanish using Claude
