A witness account reported that five guards held Lunas Campos down while one squeezed his neck. The autopsy documented abrasions, hemorrhage in the neck, and petechial hemorrhaging—physical findings inconsistent with self-inflicted injury.2

Lunas Campos was the third person to die at Camp East Montana.

Measles Outbreak

On February 26, public health officials confirmed 17 measles cases, with 13 occurring at Camp East Montana.3 By March 3, the count had reached 14 confirmed cases with 112 individuals in isolation. The facility had previously documented two tuberculosis cases and a COVID outbreak in January.3

911 Calls Reveal Conditions (March 6–7)

911 call records from March 6–7 documented attempted suicides, fights, and medical distress inside Camp East Montana. Detainees reported weight loss and food shortages. The facility relies on a private security force rather than federal staff.5

Possible Closure (March 4)

On March 4, the Texas Tribune reported that ICE is drafting a termination of the $1.2 billion contract with ALT, potentially closing Camp East Montana.4

2. Construction-Site Raids

Between January 10 and 19, ICE conducted a nine-day enforcement operation targeting construction sites across the El Paso region. A total of 38 workers were arrested at three sites in El Paso and Santa Teresa, New Mexico. Videos of the raids circulated widely on social media.6

The construction industry in the region experienced an estimated 30–40% drop in activity in the weeks following the raids, as workers stayed home out of fear of further enforcement actions.6

3. $123M Mega-Facility Near Clint

On January 17, DHS purchased warehouses in far East El Paso County for a planned 8,500-capacity detention facility. The $123 million purchase includes three 296,000-square-foot concrete warehouses near Clint, Texas.7

The facility is part of the administration’s $38 billion national detention expansion plan, which envisions converting industrial warehouses into immigration detention centers across the country.8

El Paso City Council moved to reject the facility on February 2, and the city of Socorro has also moved to block it.14

4. National Shutdown Protests (January 30)

Hundreds of El Paso residents participated in the January 30 National Shutdown, a coordinated day of action against federal immigration enforcement. Student walkouts occurred at Eastwood High School and Bel Air High School, among others.9

The protests were triggered in part by the shooting of Renee Nicole Good on January 7 in Minneapolis by ICE agents. The Texas Education Agency (TEA) threatened penalties against districts where students walked out.9

5. City & County Cooperation with ICE

The El Paso County Sheriff’s Office (EPCSO) adopted the Jail Enforcement Model under SB 8—the least aggressive of the three compliance options available to Texas sheriffs. This model limits cooperation to processing individuals already in county custody.10

El Paso City Council has taken several steps to push back against federal enforcement expansion:

  • January 29: Considered a ban on new detention centers within city limits
  • February 2: Moved to reject the planned $123M detention facility near Clint14
  • Voted to hold ICE accountable for conditions at Camp East Montana

6. Federal Court Backlog

The Western District of Texas (El Paso Division) is experiencing an unprecedented surge in habeas corpus petitions from ICE detainees. Between 2021 and 2024, El Paso federal judges averaged 26 habeas petitions per year. In 2025, that number jumped to 300.11

At the pace set in January 2026, filings would reach 2,800 per year. Habeas corpus cases now represent 75% of all new civil filings in the division. The court has only two full-time judges handling over 1,300 petitions already filed.12

7. Cross-Border & Port of Entry Developments

The Bridge of the Americas (BOTA) modernization plan includes a permanent ban on commercial truck traffic at the crossing. A new Santa Teresa connector highway is intended to reroute commercial traffic through southern New Mexico.12

On March 24, El Paso Bishop Mark Seitz led a march to the border, calling federal enforcement a “war on the poor.”13 Twenty border bishops had previously issued a joint statement on February 24 condemning the administration’s enforcement posture.13

Sources

  1. Texas Tribune: Homicide ruling — Immigrant in ICE custody at El Paso’s Camp East Montana died of homicide ↑
  2. PBS NewsHour: Cuban immigrant in ICE custody died of homicide due to asphyxia, autopsy finds ↑
  3. Texas Tribune: 17 measles cases linked to Camp East Montana ICE facility in El Paso ↑
  4. Texas Tribune: ICE moving toward possible closure of Camp East Montana ↑
  5. Texarkana Gazette: Attempted suicides, fights, pain — 911 calls reveal misery at Camp East Montana ↑
  6. The Prospector: ICE raids target El Paso construction sites ↑
  7. El Paso Matters: DHS buys warehouses for $123M mega-detention center near Clint ↑
  8. Axios: ICE reveals $38B plan for immigrant mega-jails ↑
  9. KFOX: Thousands join national strike to protest federal policies in El Paso and beyond ↑
  10. KTSM: EPCSO chooses detention-based model under new Texas immigration law ↑
  11. El Paso Matters: Habeas corpus filings surge as ICE detainees seek release ↑
  12. Border Report: El Paso, West Texas federal courts deluged with challenges to immigration detention ↑
  13. NCR: Hundreds join El Paso bishop’s protest against migrant mass deportation, asylum bans ↑
  14. El Paso Herald Post: El Paso City Council moves to reject ICE detention facility ↑

Report ICE Activity

Witnessed an ICE operation? Submit an anonymous report.

El Paso Resources

Local Reporting

El Paso MattersBorder region investigative reporting The ProspectorUTEP student newspaper KFOX14Local TV news coverage KTSM 9 NewsEl Paso NBC affiliate Border ReportBorder-focused national reporting

Tracking Tools

Deportation Data ProjectDownloadable enforcement datasets TRAC ImmigrationQueryable court & enforcement data ResistMapCrowdsourced ICE sighting map DetentionReports.comFacility-level detention data

Related Pages

Texas Statewide TrackerSB 8, 287(g), all regional breakdowns National ICE Activity TrackerAll states and regions

Know Your Rights

  • •You have the right to remain silent
  • •You do not have to open your door
  • •Ask if you are free to leave
  • •Do not sign anything you don’t understand
  • •You have the right to an attorney
Full KYR Training Guide →

Support Our Community

All donations go 100% to mutual aid through Gifted Dreamers, Inc., an Austin, Texas-based 501(c)(3).

Donate Now

Share a Resource or Send a Message

Only message is required. All other fields are optional.

About justNICE

justNICE provides free, community-sourced immigrant resources and Know Your Rights training guides. We believe preparation is power and that every person deserves access to information that protects their rights.

{"parentUuid":null,"isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall","sessionId":"cc38197f-7723-433e-aac6-d50b4464a5a8","version":"2.1.68","gitBranch":"HEAD","agentId":"aea273c726262477f","slug":"zippy-forging-cloud","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"Create an HTML file at: /Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/justNICE.us/blog/ice-activity/texas/el-paso.html

This is a regional ICE activity page for El Paso / West Texas as part of the justNICE.us ICE Activity Tracker.

## Template Pattern (MUST follow exactly)
- Tailwind CDN 3.4.17 with colors maroon: '#8B1A2B', gold: '#F5A623', font Inter
- Sticky maroon header with justNICE logo, nav links: Immigrant Resources, Know Your Rights, Blog (active), Resource Map, NICE Gear, Contact, Español toggle, Donate button
- Breadcrumb: Home / Blog / ICE Activity Tracker / Texas / El Paso
- Article badge "Community & Policy", date March 8, 2026
- Content area class "content-area prose prose-lg max-w-none text-gray-800 leading-relaxed"
- h2 for sections, h3 for incidents (text-xl font-semibold text-maroon)
- Footnotes with numbered sources
- Standard justNICE footer with contact form (webhook: https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact)
- Mobile menu, contact form JS, translations.js
- Two-column: article lg:col-span-2, sidebar lg:col-span-1
- Report callout + KYR card in sidebar

## Content

**1. Camp East Montana (Fort Bliss) — Nation's Largest ICE Detention Facility**
Avg 2,954 detainees/day. Soft-sided tent structure. $1.2B contract with Acquisition Logistics (no prior detention experience).

Homicide of Geraldo Lunas Campos (Jan 3): 55-year-old Cuban. Medical examiner ruled homicide Jan 21: asphyxia compression of neck/torso. DHS claimed suicide. Witness: 5 guards held him down, one squeezed neck. Autopsy: abrasions, hemorrhage in neck, petechial hemorrhaging. Third death at facility.

Measles outbreak: Feb 26: 17 cases, 13 at camp. Mar 3: 14 confirmed, 112 isolated. Prior TB (2) and COVID in Jan.

911 calls (Mar 6-7): Attempted suicides, fights, medical distress. Weight loss, food shortages, private security force.

Possible closure (Mar 4): ICE drafting termination of $1.2B contract.

**2. Construction Raids**
Jan 10-19 nine-day operation: 38 arrested at 3 sites (El Paso + Santa Teresa NM). Videos circulating. 30-40% regional construction drop.

**3. $123M Mega-Facility near Clint**
Jan 17: DHS bought warehouses Far East El Paso County. 8,500-capacity planned. Three 296K sq-ft concrete warehouses. Part of $38B national plan. City Council moved to reject. Socorro blocking.

**4. National Shutdown (Jan 30)**
Hundreds participated. Student walkouts: Eastwood HS, Bel Air HS. Triggered by Renee Nicole Good shooting (Jan 7 Minneapolis). TEA threatened penalties.

**5. City/County Cooperation**
EPCSO: Jail Enforcement Model (least aggressive) under SB 8. City Council: considered ban on detention centers (Jan 29), moved to reject facility (Feb 2), voted to hold ICE accountable.

**6. Federal Court Backlog**
El Paso judges: 26 habeas/year avg 2021-24. 300 in 2025. Pace in Jan 2026 = 2,800/year. Habeas = 75% of new civil cases. Only 2 full-time judges. 1,300+ petitions filed.

**7. Cross-Border / Port of Entry**
BOTA modernization: permanent truck ban planned. Santa Teresa connector highway. Bishop Mark Seitz march Mar 24 "war on the poor." 20 border bishops joint statement Feb 24.

### Sources:
1. Texas Tribune: Homicide ruling - https://www.texastribune.org/2026/01/21/texas-el-paso-immigrant-death-ice-custody-homicide/
2. PBS: Autopsy asphyxia - https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/cuban-immigrant-in-ice-custody-died-of-homicide-due-to-asphyxia-autopsy-finds
3. Texas Tribune: 17 measles cases - https://www.texastribune.org/2026/02/26/measles-el-paso-camp-east-montana-ice/
4. Texas Tribune: Possible closure - https://www.texastribune.org/2026/03/04/texas-camp-east-montana-ice-detention-possible-closure/
5. Texarkana Gazette: 911 calls misery - https://www.texarkanagazette.com/news/2026/mar/07/attempted-suicides-fights-pain-911-calls-reveal/
6. The Prospector: Construction raids - https://www.theprospectordaily.com/2026/02/04/ice-raids-el-paso-immigration-law-united-states-custom-and-border-enforcement-immigration-and-customs-enforcement/
7. El Paso Matters: $123M warehouses - https://elpasomatters.org/2026/02/06/el-paso-texas-dhs-buys-warehouses-ice-mega-immigration-detention-center-clint/
8. Axios: $38B mega-jail plan - https://www.axios.com/2026/02/13/ice-immigrant-detention-warehouses-spending
9. KFOX: National Shutdown El Paso - https://kfoxtv.com/news/local/thousands-join-national-strike-to-protest-federal-policies-in-el-paso-and-beyond

   146→          <h1 class="text-3xl md:text-4xl lg:text-5xl font-extrabold text-gray-900 leading-tight mb-6">
   147→            ICE Activity in Texas
   148→          </h1>
   149→          <p class="text-lg md:text-xl text-gray-600 leading-relaxed">
   150→            Texas has become the epicenter of federal immigration enforcement under the Trump administration’s second term. With SB 8 mandating sheriff participation in 287(g), daily ICE arrests jumping from 85 to 176, and mega-detention facilities under construction, the state is experiencing enforcement at a scale not seen in modern history. This hub tracks statewide developments and links to detailed regional pages.
   151→          </p>

      <article class="lg:col-span-2">

        <header class="mb-10">
          <div class="flex items-center gap-3 mb-4">
            <span class="inline-block bg-maroon/10 text-maroon text-xs font-semibold px-3 py-1 rounded-full uppercase tracking-wide">Community & Policy</span>
            <time datetime="2026-03-08" class="text-sm text-gray-500">March 8, 2026</time>
          </div>
          <h1 class="text-3xl md:text-4xl lg:text-5xl font-extrabold text-gray-900 leading-tight mb-6">
            ICE Activity in El Paso / West Texas
          </h1>
          <p class="text-lg md:text-xl text-gray-600 leading-relaxed">
            El Paso sits at the center of the federal government’s immigration enforcement infrastructure. Home to Camp East Montana—the nation’s largest ICE detention facility—the region has seen a detainee homicide ruled by the medical examiner, a measles outbreak, construction-site raids that collapsed a third of the local building industry, plans for a $123 million mega-facility, and a federal court system buckling under habeas corpus filings. This page tracks sourced incidents, community responses, and policy developments across El Paso and West Texas.
          </p>
        </header>

        <!-- Report callout -->
        <div class="bg-maroon/5 border-l-4 border-maroon rounded-r-lg p-5 mb-10">
          <div class="flex items-start gap-3">
            <svg class="w-5 h-5 text-maroon flex-shrink-0 mt-0.5" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-width="2" d="M12 9v2m0 4h.01m-6.938 4h13.856c1.54 0 2.502-1.667 1.732-2.5L13.732 4c-.77-.833-1.964-.833-2.732 0L4.082 16.5c-.77.833.192 2.5 1.732 2.5z"/></svg>
            <div>
              <p class="font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-1">Report ICE Activity in El Paso</p>
              <p class="text-sm text-gray-600">If you witness or experience ICE enforcement, <a href="/#contact-form" class="text-gold font-semibold hover:underline">submit an anonymous report</a>. See our <a href="/kyr-ice-training.html" class="text-gold font-semibold hover:underline">Know Your Rights guide</a>.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="content-area prose prose-lg max-w-none text-gray-800 leading-relaxed">

          <!-- Camp East Montana -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">1. Camp East Montana (Fort Bliss) — Nation’s Largest ICE Detention Facility</h2>

          <p>Camp East Montana, a soft-sided tent structure on the Fort Bliss military installation, holds an average of <strong>2,954 detainees per day</strong>, making it the largest ICE detention facility in the United States.<sup><a href="#fn1" id="fnref1" class="fn-ref">1</a></sup> The facility operates under a <strong>$1.2 billion contract</strong> with Acquisition Logistics and Technology (ALT), a company with <strong>no prior detention experience</strong>.<sup><a href="#fn4" id="fnref4" class="fn-ref">4</a></sup></p>

          <h3 class="text-xl font-semibold text-maroon mt-8 mb-3">Homicide of Geraldo Lunas Campos (January 3, 2026)</h3>

          <p>Geraldo Lunas Campos, a 55-year-old Cuban man, died in custody on January 3, 2026. DHS initially claimed the death was a suicide. On January 21, the El Paso County Medical Examiner ruled his death a <strong>homicide</strong>, finding the cause to be <strong>asphyxia due to compression of the neck and torso</strong>.<sup><a href="#fn1" id="fnref1b" class="fn-ref">1</a></sup></p>

          <p>A witness account reported that <strong>five guards held Lunas Campos down while one squeezed his neck</strong>. The autopsy documented abrasions, hemorrhage in the neck, and petechial hemorrhaging—physical findings inconsistent with self-inflicted injury.<sup><a href="#fn2" id="fnref2" class="fn-ref">2</a></sup></p>

          <p>Lunas Campos was the <strong>third person to die</strong> at Camp East Montana.</p>

          <h3 class="text-xl font-semibold text-maroon mt-8 mb-3">Measles Outbreak</h3>

          <p>On February 26, public health officials confirmed <strong>17 measles cases</strong>, with 13 occurring at Camp East Montana.<sup><a href="#fn3" id="fnref3" class="fn-ref">3</a></sup> By March 3, the count had reached <strong>14 confirmed cases with 112 individuals in isolation</strong>. The facility had previously documented <strong>two tuberculosis cases</strong> and a <strong>COVID outbreak in January</strong>.<sup><a href="#fn3" id="fnref3b" class="fn-ref">3</a></sup></p>

          <h3 class="text-xl font-semibold text-maroon mt-8 mb-3">911 Calls Reveal Conditions (March 6–7)</h3>

          <p>911 call records from March 6–7 documented <strong>attempted suicides, fights, and medical distress</strong> inside Camp East Montana. Detainees reported <strong>weight loss and food shortages</strong>. The facility relies on a <strong>private security force</strong> rather than federal staff.<sup><a href="#fn5" id="fnref5" class="fn-ref">5</a></sup></p>

          <h3 class="text-xl font-semibold text-maroon mt-8 mb-3">Possible Closure (March 4)</h3>

          <p>On March 4, the Texas Tribune reported that <strong>ICE is drafting a termination of the $1.2 billion contract</strong> with ALT, potentially closing Camp East Montana.<sup><a href="#fn4" id="fnref4b" class="fn-ref">4</a></sup></p>

          <hr>

          <!-- Construction Raids -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">2. Construction-Site Raids</h2>

          <p>Between January 10 and 19, ICE conducted a <strong>nine-day enforcement operation</strong> targeting construction sites across the El Paso region. A total of <strong>38 workers were arrested at three sites</strong> in El Paso and Santa Teresa, New Mexico. Videos of the raids circulated widely on social media.<sup><a href="#fn6" id="fnref6" class="fn-ref">6</a></sup></p>

          <p>The construction industry in the region experienced an estimated <strong>30–40% drop in activity</strong> in the weeks following the raids, as workers stayed home out of fear of further enforcement actions.<sup><a href="#fn6" id="fnref6b" class="fn-ref">6</a></sup></p>

          <hr>

          <!-- $123M Mega-Facility -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">3. $123M Mega-Facility Near Clint</h2>

          <p>On January 17, DHS purchased warehouses in far East El Paso County for a planned <strong>8,500-capacity detention facility</strong>. The $123 million purchase includes <strong>three 296,000-square-foot concrete warehouses</strong> near Clint, Texas.<sup><a href="#fn7" id="fnref7" class="fn-ref">7</a></sup></p>

          <p>The facility is part of the administration’s <strong>$38 billion national detention expansion plan</strong>, which envisions converting industrial warehouses into immigration detention centers across the country.<sup><a href="#fn8" id="fnref8" class="fn-ref">8</a></sup></p>

          <p>El Paso City Council <strong>moved to reject the facility</strong> on February 2, and the city of <strong>Socorro has also moved to block it</strong>.<sup><a href="#fn14" id="fnref14" class="fn-ref">14</a></sup></p>

          <hr>

          <!-- National Shutdown -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">4. National Shutdown Protests (January 30)</h2>

          <p><strong>Hundreds of El Paso residents participated</strong> in the January 30 National Shutdown, a coordinated day of action against federal immigration enforcement. Student walkouts occurred at <strong>Eastwood High School and Bel Air High School</strong>, among others.<sup><a href="#fn9" id="fnref9" class="fn-ref">9</a></sup></p>

          <p>The protests were triggered in part by the <strong>shooting of Renee Nicole Good on January 7 in Minneapolis</strong> by ICE agents. The Texas Education Agency (TEA) <strong>threatened penalties</strong> against districts where students walked out.<sup><a href="#fn9" id="fnref9b" class="fn-ref">9</a></sup></p>

          <hr>

          <!-- City/County Cooperation -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">5. City & County Cooperation with ICE</h2>

          <p>The El Paso County Sheriff’s Office (EPCSO) adopted the <strong>Jail Enforcement Model</strong> under SB 8—the <strong>least aggressive</strong> of the three compliance options available to Texas sheriffs. This model limits cooperation to processing individuals already in county custody.<sup><a href="#fn10" id="fnref10" class="fn-ref">10</a></sup></p>

          <p>El Paso City Council has taken several steps to push back against federal enforcement expansion:</p>

          <ul>
            <li><strong>January 29:</strong> Considered a ban on new detention centers within city limits</li>
            <li><strong>February 2:</strong> Moved to reject the planned $123M detention facility near Clint<sup><a href="#fn14" id="fnref14b" class="fn-ref">14</a></sup></li>
            <li>Voted to <strong>hold ICE accountable</strong> for conditions at Camp East Montana</li>
          </ul>

          <hr>

          <!-- Federal Court Backlog -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">6. Federal Court Backlog</h2>

          <p>The Western District of Texas (El Paso Division) is experiencing an unprecedented surge in habeas corpus petitions from ICE detainees. Between 2021 and 2024, El Paso federal judges averaged <strong>26 habeas petitions per year</strong>. In 2025, that number jumped to <strong>300</strong>.<sup><a href="#fn11" id="fnref11" class="fn-ref">11</a></sup></p>

          <p>At the pace set in January 2026, filings would reach <strong>2,800 per year</strong>. Habeas corpus cases now represent <strong>75% of all new civil filings</strong> in the division. The court has <strong>only two full-time judges</strong> handling over <strong>1,300 petitions already filed</strong>.<sup><a href="#fn12" id="fnref12" class="fn-ref">12</a></sup></p>

          <hr>

          <!-- Cross-Border / Port of Entry -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">7. Cross-Border & Port of Entry Developments</h2>

          <p>The <strong>Bridge of the Americas (BOTA) modernization</strong> plan includes a <strong>permanent ban on commercial truck traffic</strong> at the crossing. A new <strong>Santa Teresa connector highway</strong> is intended to reroute commercial traffic through southern New Mexico.<sup><a href="#fn12" id="fnref12b" class="fn-ref">12</a></sup></p>

          <p>On March 24, El Paso Bishop <strong>Mark Seitz</strong> led a march to the border, calling federal enforcement a <strong>“war on the poor.”</strong><sup><a href="#fn13" id="fnref13" class="fn-ref">13</a></sup> Twenty border bishops had previously issued a <strong>joint statement on February 24</strong> condemning the administration’s enforcement posture.<sup><a href="#fn13" id="fnref13b" class="fn-ref">13</a></sup></p>

      <!-- SIDEBAR -->
      <aside class="lg:col-span-1">
        <div class="lg:sticky lg:top-[120px]">

          <!-- Report callout sidebar -->

This is a regional ICE activity page for El Paso / West Texas as part of the justNICE.us ICE Activity Tracker.

## Template Pattern (MUST follow exactly)
- Tailwind CDN 3.4.17 with colors maroon: '#8B1A2B', gold: '#F5A623', font Inter
- Sticky maroon header with justNICE logo, nav links: Immigrant Resources, Know Your Rights, Blog (active), Resource Map, NICE Gear, Contact, Español toggle, Donate button
- Breadcrumb: Home / Blog / ICE Activity Tracker / Texas / El Paso
- Article badge "Community & Policy", date March 8, 2026
- Content area class "content-area prose prose-lg max-w-none text-gray-800 leading-relaxed"
- h2 for sections, h3 for incidents (text-xl font-semibold text-maroon)
- Footnotes with numbered sources
- Standard justNICE footer with contact form (webhook: https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact)
- Mobile menu, contact form JS, translations.js
- Two-column: article lg:col-span-2, sidebar lg:col-span-1
- Report callout + KYR card in sidebar

## Content

**1. Camp East Montana (Fort Bliss) — Nation's Largest ICE Detention Facility**
Avg 2,954 detainees/day. Soft-sided tent structure. $1.2B contract with Acquisition Logistics (no prior detention experience).

Homicide of Geraldo Lunas Campos (Jan 3): 55-year-old Cuban. Medical examiner ruled homicide Jan 21: asphyxia compression of neck/torso. DHS claimed suicide. Witness: 5 guards held him down, one squeezed neck. Autopsy: abrasions, hemorrhage in neck, petechial hemorrhaging. Third death at facility.

Measles outbreak: Feb 26: 17 cases, 13 at camp. Mar 3: 14 confirmed, 112 isolated. Prior TB (2) and COVID in Jan.

911 calls (Mar 6-7): Attempted suicides, fights, medical distress. Weight loss, food shortages, private security force.

Possible closure (Mar 4): ICE drafting termination of $1.2B contract.

**2. Construction Raids**
Jan 10-19 nine-day operation: 38 arrested at 3 sites (El Paso + Santa Teresa NM). Videos circulating. 30-40% regional construction drop.

**3. $123M Mega-Facility near Clint**
Jan 17: DHS bought warehouses Far East El Paso County. 8,500-capacity planned. Three 296K sq-ft concrete warehouses. Part of $38B national plan. City Council moved to reject. Socorro blocking.

**4. National Shutdown (Jan 30)**
Hundreds participated. Student walkouts: Eastwood HS, Bel Air HS. Triggered by Renee Nicole Good shooting (Jan 7 Minneapolis). TEA threatened penalties.

**5. City/County Cooperation**
EPCSO: Jail Enforcement Model (least aggressive) under SB 8. City Council: considered ban on detention centers (Jan 29), moved to reject facility (Feb 2), voted to hold ICE accountable.

**6. Federal Court Backlog**
El Paso judges: 26 habeas/year avg 2021-24. 300 in 2025. Pace in Jan 2026 = 2,800/year. Habeas = 75% of new civil cases. Only 2 full-time judges. 1,300+ petitions filed.

**7. Cross-Border / Port of Entry**
BOTA modernization: permanent truck ban planned. Santa Teresa connector highway. Bishop Mark Seitz march Mar 24 "war on the poor." 20 border bishops joint statement Feb 24.

This is a regional ICE activity page for El Paso / West Texas as part of the justNICE.us ICE Activity Tracker.

## Template Pattern (MUST follow exactly)
- Tailwind CDN 3.4.17 with colors maroon: '#8B1A2B', gold: '#F5A623', font Inter
- Sticky maroon header with justNICE logo, nav links: Immigrant Resources, Know Your Rights, Blog (active), Resource Map, NICE Gear, Contact, Español toggle, Donate button
- Breadcrumb: Home / Blog / ICE Activity Tracker / Texas / El Paso
- Article badge "Community & Policy", date March 8, 2026
- Content area class "content-area prose prose-lg max-w-none text-gray-800 leading-relaxed"
- h2 for sections, h3 for incidents (text-xl font-semibold text-maroon)
- Footnotes with numbered sources
- Standard justNICE footer with contact form (webhook: https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact)
- Mobile menu, contact form JS, translations.js
- Two-column: article lg:col-span-2, sidebar lg:col-span-1
- Report callout + KYR card in sidebar

## Content

**1. Camp East Montana (Fort Bliss) — Nation's Largest ICE Detention Facility**
Avg 2,954 detainees/day. Soft-sided tent structure. $1.2B contract with Acquisition Logistics (no prior detention experience).

Homicide of Geraldo Lunas Campos (Jan 3): 55-year-old Cuban. Medical examiner ruled homicide Jan 21: asphyxia compression of neck/torso. DHS claimed suicide. Witness: 5 guards held him down, one squeezed neck. Autopsy: abrasions, hemorrhage in neck, petechial hemorrhaging. Third death at facility.

Measles outbreak: Feb 26: 17 cases, 13 at camp. Mar 3: 14 confirmed, 112 isolated. Prior TB (2) and COVID in Jan.

911 calls (Mar 6-7): Attempted suicides, fights, medical distress. Weight loss, food shortages, private security force.

Possible closure (Mar 4): ICE drafting termination of $1.2B contract.

**2. Construction Raids**
Jan 10-19 nine-day operation: 38 arrested at 3 sites (El Paso + Santa Teresa NM). Videos circulating. 30-40% regional construction drop.

**3. $123M Mega-Facility near Clint**
Jan 17: DHS bought warehouses Far East El Paso County. 8,500-capacity planned. Three 296K sq-ft concrete warehouses. Part of $38B national plan. City Council moved to reject. Socorro blocking.

**4. National Shutdown (Jan 30)**
Hundreds participated. Student walkouts: Eastwood HS, Bel Air HS. Triggered by Renee Nicole Good shooting (Jan 7 Minneapolis). TEA threatened penalties.

**5. City/County Cooperation**
EPCSO: Jail Enforcement Model (least aggressive) under SB 8. City Council: considered ban on detention centers (Jan 29), moved to reject facility (Feb 2), voted to hold ICE accountable.

**6. Federal Court Backlog**
El Paso judges: 26 habeas/year avg 2021-24. 300 in 2025. Pace in Jan 2026 = 2,800/year. Habeas = 75% of new civil cases. Only 2 full-time judges. 1,300+ petitions filed.

**7. Cross-Border / Port of Entry**
BOTA modernization: permanent truck ban planned. Santa Teresa connector highway. Bishop Mark Seitz march Mar 24 "war on the poor." 20 border bishops joint statement Feb 24.

### Sources:

   146→          <h1 class="text-3xl md:text-4xl lg:text-5xl font-extrabold text-gray-900 leading-tight mb-6">
   147→            ICE Activity in Texas
   148→          </h1>
   149→          <p class="text-lg md:text-xl text-gray-600 leading-relaxed">
   150→            Texas has become the epicenter of federal immigration enforcement under the Trump administration’s second term. With SB 8 mandating sheriff participation in 287(g), daily ICE arrests jumping from 85 to 176, and mega-detention facilities under construction, the state is experiencing enforcement at a scale not seen in modern history. This hub tracks statewide developments and links to detailed regional pages.
   151→          </p>

   158→            <div>
   159→              <p class="font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-1">Report ICE Activity in Texas</p>
   160→              <p class="text-sm text-gray-600">If you witness or experience ICE enforcement, <a href="/#contact-form" class="text-gold font-semibold hover:underline">submit an anonymous report</a>. See our <a href="/kyr-ice-training.html" class="text-gold font-semibold hover:underline">Know Your Rights guide</a>.</p>

   427→              <li class="flex items-start gap-2"><span class="text-maroon font-bold mt-0.5">•</span>You have the right to remain silent</li>
   428→              <li class="flex items-start gap-2"><span class="text-maroon font-bold mt-0.5">•</span>You do not have to open your door</li>
   429→              <li class="flex items-start gap-2"><span class="text-maroon font-bold mt-0.5">•</span>Ask if you are free to leave</li>
   430→              <li class="flex items-start gap-2"><span class="text-maroon font-bold mt-0.5">•</span>Do not sign anything you don’t understand</li>

  <!-- MAIN -->

      <article class="lg:col-span-2">

        <header class="mb-10">
          <div class="flex items-center gap-3 mb-4">
            <span class="inline-block bg-maroon/10 text-maroon text-xs font-semibold px-3 py-1 rounded-full uppercase tracking-wide">Community & Policy</span>
            <time datetime="2026-03-08" class="text-sm text-gray-500">March 8, 2026</time>
          </div>
          <h1 class="text-3xl md:text-4xl lg:text-5xl font-extrabold text-gray-900 leading-tight mb-6">
            ICE Activity in El Paso / West Texas
          </h1>
          <p class="text-lg md:text-xl text-gray-600 leading-relaxed">
            El Paso sits at the center of the federal government’s immigration enforcement infrastructure. Home to Camp East Montana—the nation’s largest ICE detention facility—the region has seen a detainee homicide ruled by the medical examiner, a measles outbreak, construction-site raids that collapsed a third of the local building industry, plans for a $123 million mega-facility, and a federal court system buckling under habeas corpus filings. This page tracks sourced incidents, community responses, and policy developments across El Paso and West Texas.
          </p>
        </header>

        <!-- Report callout -->
        <div class="bg-maroon/5 border-l-4 border-maroon rounded-r-lg p-5 mb-10">
          <div class="flex items-start gap-3">
            <svg class="w-5 h-5 text-maroon flex-shrink-0 mt-0.5" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-width="2" d="M12 9v2m0 4h.01m-6.938 4h13.856c1.54 0 2.502-1.667 1.732-2.5L13.732 4c-.77-.833-1.964-.833-2.732 0L4.082 16.5c-.77.833.192 2.5 1.732 2.5z"/></svg>
            <div>
              <p class="font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-1">Report ICE Activity in El Paso</p>
              <p class="text-sm text-gray-600">If you witness or experience ICE enforcement, <a href="/#contact-form" class="text-gold font-semibold hover:underline">submit an anonymous report</a>. See our <a href="/kyr-ice-training.html" class="text-gold font-semibold hover:underline">Know Your Rights guide</a>.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="content-area prose prose-lg max-w-none text-gray-800 leading-relaxed">

          <!-- Camp East Montana -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">1. Camp East Montana (Fort Bliss) — Nation’s Largest ICE Detention Facility</h2>

          <p>Camp East Montana, a soft-sided tent structure on the Fort Bliss military installation, holds an average of <strong>2,954 detainees per day</strong>, making it the largest ICE detention facility in the United States.<sup><a href="#fn1" id="fnref1" class="fn-ref">1</a></sup> The facility operates under a <strong>$1.2 billion contract</strong> with Acquisition Logistics and Technology (ALT), a company with <strong>no prior detention experience</strong>.<sup><a href="#fn4" id="fnref4" class="fn-ref">4</a></sup></p>

          <h3 class="text-xl font-semibold text-maroon mt-8 mb-3">Homicide of Geraldo Lunas Campos (January 3, 2026)</h3>

          <p>Geraldo Lunas Campos, a 55-year-old Cuban man, died in custody on January 3, 2026. DHS initially claimed the death was a suicide. On January 21, the El Paso County Medical Examiner ruled his death a <strong>homicide</strong>, finding the cause to be <strong>asphyxia due to compression of the neck and torso</strong>.<sup><a href="#fn1" id="fnref1b" class="fn-ref">1</a></sup></p>

          <p>A witness account reported that <strong>five guards held Lunas Campos down while one squeezed his neck</strong>. The autopsy documented abrasions, hemorrhage in the neck, and petechial hemorrhaging—physical findings inconsistent with self-inflicted injury.<sup><a href="#fn2" id="fnref2" class="fn-ref">2</a></sup></p>

          <p>Lunas Campos was the <strong>third person to die</strong> at Camp East Montana.</p>

          <h3 class="text-xl font-semibold text-maroon mt-8 mb-3">Measles Outbreak</h3>

          <p>On February 26, public health officials confirmed <strong>17 measles cases</strong>, with 13 occurring at Camp East Montana.<sup><a href="#fn3" id="fnref3" class="fn-ref">3</a></sup> By March 3, the count had reached <strong>14 confirmed cases with 112 individuals in isolation</strong>. The facility had previously documented <strong>two tuberculosis cases</strong> and a <strong>COVID outbreak in January</strong>.<sup><a href="#fn3" id="fnref3b" class="fn-ref">3</a></sup></p>

          <h3 class="text-xl font-semibold text-maroon mt-8 mb-3">911 Calls Reveal Conditions (March 6–7)</h3>

          <p>911 call records from March 6–7 documented <strong>attempted suicides, fights, and medical distress</strong> inside Camp East Montana. Detainees reported <strong>weight loss and food shortages</strong>. The facility relies on a <strong>private security force</strong> rather than federal staff.<sup><a href="#fn5" id="fnref5" class="fn-ref">5</a></sup></p>

          <h3 class="text-xl font-semibold text-maroon mt-8 mb-3">Possible Closure (March 4)</h3>

          <p>On March 4, the Texas Tribune reported that <strong>ICE is drafting a termination of the $1.2 billion contract</strong> with ALT, potentially closing Camp East Montana.<sup><a href="#fn4" id="fnref4b" class="fn-ref">4</a></sup></p>

          <hr>

          <!-- Construction Raids -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">2. Construction-Site Raids</h2>

          <p>Between January 10 and 19, ICE conducted a <strong>nine-day enforcement operation</strong> targeting construction sites across the El Paso region. A total of <strong>38 workers were arrested at three sites</strong> in El Paso and Santa Teresa, New Mexico. Videos of the raids circulated widely on social media.<sup><a href="#fn6" id="fnref6" class="fn-ref">6</a></sup></p>

          <p>The construction industry in the region experienced an estimated <strong>30–40% drop in activity</strong> in the weeks following the raids, as workers stayed home out of fear of further enforcement actions.<sup><a href="#fn6" id="fnref6b" class="fn-ref">6</a></sup></p>

          <hr>

          <!-- $123M Mega-Facility -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">3. $123M Mega-Facility Near Clint</h2>

          <p>On January 17, DHS purchased warehouses in far East El Paso County for a planned <strong>8,500-capacity detention facility</strong>. The $123 million purchase includes <strong>three 296,000-square-foot concrete warehouses</strong> near Clint, Texas.<sup><a href="#fn7" id="fnref7" class="fn-ref">7</a></sup></p>

          <p>The facility is part of the administration’s <strong>$38 billion national detention expansion plan</strong>, which envisions converting industrial warehouses into immigration detention centers across the country.<sup><a href="#fn8" id="fnref8" class="fn-ref">8</a></sup></p>

          <p>El Paso City Council <strong>moved to reject the facility</strong> on February 2, and the city of <strong>Socorro has also moved to block it</strong>.<sup><a href="#fn14" id="fnref14" class="fn-ref">14</a></sup></p>

          <hr>

          <!-- National Shutdown -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">4. National Shutdown Protests (January 30)</h2>

          <p><strong>Hundreds of El Paso residents participated</strong> in the January 30 National Shutdown, a coordinated day of action against federal immigration enforcement. Student walkouts occurred at <strong>Eastwood High School and Bel Air High School</strong>, among others.<sup><a href="#fn9" id="fnref9" class="fn-ref">9</a></sup></p>

          <p>The protests were triggered in part by the <strong>shooting of Renee Nicole Good on January 7 in Minneapolis</strong> by ICE agents. The Texas Education Agency (TEA) <strong>threatened penalties</strong> against districts where students walked out.<sup><a href="#fn9" id="fnref9b" class="fn-ref">9</a></sup></p>

          <hr>

          <!-- City/County Cooperation -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">5. City & County Cooperation with ICE</h2>

          <p>The El Paso County Sheriff’s Office (EPCSO) adopted the <strong>Jail Enforcement Model</strong> under SB 8—the <strong>least aggressive</strong> of the three compliance options available to Texas sheriffs. This model limits cooperation to processing individuals already in county custody.<sup><a href="#fn10" id="fnref10" class="fn-ref">10</a></sup></p>

          <p>El Paso City Council has taken several steps to push back against federal enforcement expansion:</p>

          <ul>
            <li><strong>January 29:</strong> Considered a ban on new detention centers within city limits</li>
            <li><strong>February 2:</strong> Moved to reject the planned $123M detention facility near Clint<sup><a href="#fn14" id="fnref14b" class="fn-ref">14</a></sup></li>
            <li>Voted to <strong>hold ICE accountable</strong> for conditions at Camp East Montana</li>
          </ul>

          <hr>

          <!-- Federal Court Backlog -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">6. Federal Court Backlog</h2>

          <p>The Western District of Texas (El Paso Division) is experiencing an unprecedented surge in habeas corpus petitions from ICE detainees. Between 2021 and 2024, El Paso federal judges averaged <strong>26 habeas petitions per year</strong>. In 2025, that number jumped to <strong>300</strong>.<sup><a href="#fn11" id="fnref11" class="fn-ref">11</a></sup></p>

          <p>At the pace set in January 2026, filings would reach <strong>2,800 per year</strong>. Habeas corpus cases now represent <strong>75% of all new civil filings</strong> in the division. The court has <strong>only two full-time judges</strong> handling over <strong>1,300 petitions already filed</strong>.<sup><a href="#fn12" id="fnref12" class="fn-ref">12</a></sup></p>

          <hr>

          <!-- Cross-Border / Port of Entry -->
          <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-10 mb-4">7. Cross-Border & Port of Entry Developments</h2>

          <p>The <strong>Bridge of the Americas (BOTA) modernization</strong> plan includes a <strong>permanent ban on commercial truck traffic</strong> at the crossing. A new <strong>Santa Teresa connector highway</strong> is intended to reroute commercial traffic through southern New Mexico.<sup><a href="#fn12" id="fnref12b" class="fn-ref">12</a></sup></p>

          <p>On March 24, El Paso Bishop <strong>Mark Seitz</strong> led a march to the border, calling federal enforcement a <strong>“war on the poor.”</strong><sup><a href="#fn13" id="fnref13" class="fn-ref">13</a></sup> Twenty border bishops had previously issued a <strong>joint statement on February 24</strong> condemning the administration’s enforcement posture.<sup><a href="#fn13" id="fnref13b" class="fn-ref">13</a></sup></p>

          <!-- Footnotes -->
          <section class="footnotes-section">
            <h2 class="text-lg font-bold text-gray-900 mb-4">Sources</h2>
            <ol>
              <li id="fn1"><a href="https://www.texastribune.org/2026/01/21/texas-el-paso-immigrant-death-ice-custody-homicide/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Texas Tribune: Homicide ruling — Immigrant in ICE custody at El Paso’s Camp East Montana died of homicide</a> <a href="#fnref1" class="fn-back">↑</a></li>
              <li id="fn2"><a href="https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/cuban-immigrant-in-ice-custody-died-of-homicide-due-to-asphyxia-autopsy-finds" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">PBS NewsHour: Cuban immigrant in ICE custody died of homicide due to asphyxia, autopsy finds</a> <a href="#fnref2" class="fn-back">↑</a></li>

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me go through the conversation chronologically to capture all important details.

1. **Session start**: The system instructions include CLAUDE.md with credential handling, git operations, Moltbook heartbeat requirement, and infrastructure details for commoncloud.cc/justNICE.us.

2. **User's initial request**: Implement a detailed plan for a 50-State ICE Activity Tracker for justNICE. The plan covers:
   - URL structure: `/blog/ice-activity/` hub, state pages, Texas regional pages
   - 5 phases: Texas statewide, TX regional pages, US hub + 49 states, resources sidebar, n8n automation
   - Template based on existing ATX/MN ICE activity posts
   - Deploy via SFTP to commoncloud.cc

3. **Moltbook heartbeat**: Launched in background, failed due to Bash permission denied. Not critical to main task.

4. **Codebase exploration**: Agent explored justNICE.us site structure:
   - Site root: `/Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/justNICE.us/`
   - Pure static HTML, Tailwind CDN 3.4.17, Inter font, maroon (#8B1A2B) + gold (#F5A623) colors
   - Existing posts: `blog/atx-ice-activity.html` (59KB) and `blog/mn-ice-activity.html` (56KB)
   - Blog listing at `blog.html` with card grid layout
   - No build step, vanilla JS, translations.js for i18n

5. **User correction**: "the website to deploy is incorrect. the repo is /Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/justNICE.us" - This was actually already the correct path I was using.

6. **User request about images**: "it would be good to have images generated or displayed with credit to photographers that you find for each area. unsplash, pexels api or are there other sites?" - I acknowledged and suggested Unsplash, Pexels, Wikimedia Commons, Flickr CC as options, deferred to follow-up pass.

7. **Frontend design skill**: Triggered but I chose to match existing justNICE design system rather than create new aesthetic.

8. **Tasks created** (6 total):
   - #1: Create directory structure and HTML template ✅
   - #2: Build Texas hub page (texas.html) ✅
   - #3: Build 6 Texas regional pages ✅
   - #4: Build US hub page and 49 state skeleton pages (pending - US hub done, 49 states NOT done)
   - #5: Update blog.html and write n8n workflow spec ✅
   - #6: Deploy via SFTP to justnice.us (pending)

9. **Files created**:
   - `/blog/ice-activity/index.html` - US hub, 66KB, all 50 states + DC + territories linked by region
   - `/blog/ice-activity/texas.html` - TX statewide hub, 48KB, SB 8, stats, facilities, economic impact
   - `/blog/ice-activity/texas/austin.html` - 37KB, links to existing ATX report + updates
   - `/blog/ice-activity/texas/dfw.html` - 49KB, 100/day arrests, Hutchins, CLEAR, probation arrests
   - `/blog/ice-activity/texas/houston.html` - 47KB, 646 arrests, HPD 1000% surge
   - `/blog/ice-activity/texas/el-paso.html` - 41KB, Camp East Montana, $123M facility
   - `/blog/ice-activity/texas/rio-grande-valley.html` - 47KB, Mariachi family, Dilley, LUPE
   - `/blog/ice-activity/texas/san-antonio.html` - 45KB, 7-hr council, $66M warehouse
   - `/docs/n8n-ice-tracker-workflow-spec.md` - n8n automation pipeline spec

10. **Files modified**:
    - `blog.html` - Added ICE Activity Tracker featured card at top of blog grid

11. **Research agents** completed for: TX statewide, DFW, Houston, San Antonio, RGV + El Paso. All returned comprehensive data with dates, numbers, and source URLs.

12. **What's NOT done yet**:
    - 49 state skeleton pages (task #4 partially complete - US hub done but not individual state pages)
    - SFTP deployment (task #6)
    - Images for pages (user requested)
    - Actual n8n workflow implementation (spec only)

13. **Template pattern** used across all pages:
    - Tailwind CDN 3.4.17
    - Maroon header, breadcrumb, two-column layout (article + sidebar)
    - Resources sidebar with tracking tools, maps, legal resources
    - KYR quick card
    - Footer with contact form (webhook: n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact)
    - Mobile menu, translations.js
    - Footnotes with fn-ref/fn-back pattern
    - JSON-LD structured data, OG tags, Twitter cards

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested implementation of a comprehensive 50-State ICE Activity Tracker for the justNICE.us static website. The detailed plan includes:
   - **Phase 1**: Texas statewide hub page with SB 8 context, enforcement stats, facility developments
   - **Phase 2**: 6 Texas regional pages (Austin, DFW, Houston, San Antonio, RGV, El Paso) with researched, sourced content
   - **Phase 3**: US hub/index page linking all 50 states + territories, organized by region; 49 state skeleton pages seeded with ICE field office info, 287(g) data, sanctuary status
   - **Phase 4**: Resources sidebar on all pages linking 19+ tracking tools/data sources
   - **Phase 5**: n8n automation workflow spec for daily content updates
   - **Deployment**: SFTP upload to justnice.us (commoncloud.cc hosting)
   - **Images**: User later requested images with photographer credit from Unsplash, Pexels, or other free sources for each area
   - The existing ATX and MN posts should NOT be moved (preserve URLs), but linked from the new hub pages

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Pure static HTML site (no build step, no bundler, no React)
   - Tailwind CSS via CDN 3.4.17 with custom config: maroon `#8B1A2B`, gold `#F5A623`
   - Google Fonts Inter (async loaded, weights 300-800)
   - Two-column layout pattern: `lg:grid-cols-3` with article `lg:col-span-2` + sidebar `lg:col-span-1`
   - Sticky maroon header + sticky breadcrumb at `top-[72px]`
   - Footnote system: `fn-ref` superscript links in text, `footnotes-section` at bottom with `fn-back` arrows
   - Contact form posting to webhook `https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact`
   - translations.js for i18n (Spanish) with `data-i18n` attributes
   - JSON-LD structured data for SEO
   - Blog card grid system in blog.html with `data-category` and `data-search` filter attributes
   - SFTP deployment via 1Password credential `tekzxxut6fft4yesjpcgruumnu` to commoncloud.cc `/var/www/my_webapp/www`

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`/blog/ice-activity/index.html`** (66KB, CREATED)
     - US hub page linking all 50 states + DC + 3 territories organized by region (South, West, Northeast, Midwest, Territories)
     - State cards with abbreviation, ICE field office info, 287(g) status
     - Texas and Minnesota highlighted as "Active" with pulse dots
     - "Detailed Coverage" section linking to TX hub, MN report, ATX report
     - Full resources sidebar with Structured Data, Maps & Alerts, Journalism, Legal & Policy sections
     - "Report ICE Activity" callout box, KYR quick card, "About This Tracker" section
   
   - **`/blog/ice-activity/texas.html`** (48KB, CREATED)
     - Texas statewide hub with regional page links (6 cards)
     - Sections: SB 8 (287(g) mandate table), Enforcement Stats (table with daily arrests, field office numbers), Mega-Detention Facilities (Camp East Montana, SA warehouse, Clint, Hutchins), Economic Impact (construction data, survey stats), Community Response (walkouts, National Shutdown, faith-based, Judge Sakai), Key Incidents Timeline (14-row chronological table)
     - 14 footnoted sources with URLs
     - Texas-specific sidebar resources (Texas Tribune, KERA, Texas Observer, El Paso Matters)
   
   - **`/blog/ice-activity/texas/austin.html`** (37KB, CREATED)
     - Links to existing `/blog/atx-ice-activity.html` detailed report
     - Summarizes key incidents (Gutiérrez-Castellanos, APD policy change, wrongful deportation, walkouts)
     - Policy context (APD general orders, Travis/Williamson/Hays county SB 8 status)
     - 7 footnoted sources
   
   - **`/blog/ice-activity/texas/dfw.html`** (49KB, CREATED)
     - 100/day arrest average (2nd nationally), 84-arrest single-day sweep, 108% YoY increase
     - Probation office arrests, courthouse arrests, construction raids
     - Hutchins mega-facility blocked, Prairieland/Leqaa Kordia
     - Dallas PD rejected $25M 287(g), Tarrant expanded, Arlington rejected
     - CLEAR faith network, student walkouts, AG Paxton investigation
     - 14 footnoted sources
   
   - **`/blog/ice-activity/texas/houston.html`** (47KB, CREATED)
     - 646 arrests Feb 23-Mar 2 operation breakdown
     - HPD ICE calls surged 1,000% (154 calls, 12 officers responsible for 1/3)
     - East End raids, Greenspoint court arrests, Curtis Wright case
     - Project Homecoming self-deportation flights, Avelo Airlines
     - Student walkouts (14 campuses), Rice/Kinder survey (1 in 7)
     - 14 footnoted sources
   
   - **`/blog/ice-activity/texas/san-antonio.html`** (45KB, CREATED)
     - 7-hour City Council meeting (180 speakers), 8-2 moratorium vote
     - $66M warehouse (542 SE Loop 410, 1,500 beds), Lackland AFB
     - Vehicle ramming (Walmart Blanco Rd), viral home raid (3M views), food truck raid
     - SAPD policy (111/51,000 detainers), Bexar County 287(g) WSO
     - Judge Sakai internment comparison, Rodeo/Fiesta chilling effect
     - 14 footnoted sources
   
   - **`/blog/ice-activity/texas/rio-grande-valley.html`** (47KB, CREATED)
     - Mariachi family (Gamez-Cuellar) detained at check-in
     - Worksite raids (25 + 12 arrested, 30-40% construction drop)
     - LUPE organizing (Valle Fuerte campaign, butterfly/barbed wire signs)
     - Dilley detention (2,400 cap, CoreCivic $160M/yr, RAICES 700+ complaints, ProPublica investigation)
     - BP vs ICE operations, school/church impacts
     - 14 footnoted sources
   
   - **`/blog/ice-activity/texas/el-paso.html`** (41KB, CREATED)
     - Camp East Montana (2,954 avg, Lunas Campos homicide, measles 14 cases, 911 calls, possible closure)
     - Construction raids (38 arrested, 9-day operation)
     - $123M Clint mega-facility (8,500 capacity), $38B national plan
     - National Shutdown, EPCSO Jail Enforcement Model
     - Federal court backlog (26/yr to 2,800/yr pace habeas filings)
     - Bishop Seitz march, BOTA modernization
     - 14 footnoted sources
   
   - **`/blog.html`** (MODIFIED)
     - Added featured ICE Activity Tracker card at top of blog grid
     - Full-width card (`md:col-span-2`) with maroon gradient hero, "Live" badge, ring-2 border
     - Card includes category "Community & Policy", date March 8 2026, tracker description
   
   - **`/docs/n8n-ice-tracker-workflow-spec.md`** (CREATED)
     - 7-stage pipeline: RSS collection (10+ feeds), API checks (CourtListener, TRAC, ICE.gov), page scraping, AI classification (Claude), HTML formatting, digest assembly, notification
     - Schedule trigger daily 6am CT
     - RSS feeds listed: Drop Site News, ProPublica, Texas Tribune, KERA, El Paso Matters, Houston Public Media, Border Report, ICE.gov, DOJ WDTX, ACLU
     - Deduplication via Postgres, error handling per feed
   
   - **Existing files read for template reference**:
     - `/blog/atx-ice-activity.html` (lines 1-350, 600-892) - Full template pattern extracted
     - `/blog.html` (lines 1-80, 160-175) - Blog card grid pattern

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Moltbook heartbeat agent failed**: Bash permission was denied for curl commands. Not a blocking issue - the heartbeat is a session-start routine, not related to the main task.
   - **No other significant errors**: All file writes and agent tasks completed successfully.
   - **User correction on deploy target**: User said "the website to deploy is incorrect. the repo is /Users/kristinesocall/.../justNICE.us" — however this was already the path being used. Acknowledged and confirmed.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Parallelization strategy**: Used background agents extensively — 5 research agents (TX statewide, DFW, Houston, San Antonio, RGV+El Paso) ran in parallel, then 6 page-building agents ran in parallel. This was highly efficient for the large volume of content.
   - **Template consistency**: Extracted the exact HTML template pattern from the existing `atx-ice-activity.html` (header, nav, breadcrumb, content-area styles, footnotes, footer, scripts) and provided it to all building agents to ensure consistency.
   - **Existing content preservation**: Austin page links to existing `/blog/atx-ice-activity.html` rather than duplicating it. Minnesota links to existing `/blog/mn-ice-activity.html` from the US hub.

6. All user messages:

   - "it would be good to have images generated or displayed with credit to photographers that you find for each area. unsplash, pexels api or are there other sites?"

7. Pending Tasks:

   - **n8n workflow implementation** — Only the spec document was created; actual n8n workflow has not been built (plan noted this would be spec-only for this session)

8. Current Work:

The final file inventory was run showing 8 HTML files totaling 396KB in `/blog/ice-activity/`. The blog.html card was added. The n8n spec was written. Tasks #1, #2, #3, #5 are marked complete. Tasks #4 (49 state skeleton pages) and #6 (SFTP deploy) remain pending.

   The last assistant message was a file inventory check confirming all Texas pages exist, followed by receipt of the El Paso agent completion notification.

9. Optional Next Step:

The plan stated: "State page generation — may span into next session if context runs low" and "Every state page gets researched and seeded with: State's ICE field office(s) and AOR, 287(g) agreements count, sanctuary/anti-sanctuary laws, known incidents from Jan-Mar 2026, key local news sources, governor/AG stance." Community & Policy March 8, 2026

ICE Activity in Austin / Central Texas

Austin and the surrounding Central Texas counties—Travis, Williamson, and Hays—have experienced a sharp escalation in ICE enforcement since January 2026. From APD changing its general orders on ICE cooperation to student walkouts and thousands rallying in East Austin, the region is navigating a new landscape of federal-local enforcement dynamics.

Full Detailed Report Available

For the full Jan 10–Feb 10 timeline with 34+ sourced incidents, see our original report: ICE Activity Within a 2-Hour Radius of Austin, TX.

Report ICE Activity in Central Texas

If you witness or experience ICE enforcement, submit an anonymous report. See our Know Your Rights guide.

Overview

function generateStatePage(state) {
  const isMinnesota = state.abbr === 'MN';
  const mnExistingLink = isMinnesota ? `
          <!-- Full report callout -->
          <div class="bg-gold/10 border-l-4 border-gold rounded-r-lg p-5 mb-10">
            <div class="flex items-start gap-3">
              <svg class="w-6 h-6 text-gold flex-shrink-0 mt-0.5" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-width="2" d="M9 12h6m-6 4h6m2 5H7a2 2 0 01-2-2V5a2 2 0 012-2h5.586a1 1 0 01.707.293l5.414 5.414a1 1 0 01.293.707V19a2 2 0 01-2 2z"/></svg>
              <div>
                <p class="font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-1">Full Detailed Report Available</p>
                <p class="text-sm text-gray-600">For our comprehensive Minnesota ICE activity report with sourced incidents and community response, see: <a href="/blog/mn-ice-activity.html" class="text-gold font-semibold hover:underline">ICE Activity in Minnesota</a>.</p>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>` : '';

  const sanctuaryColor = state.sanctuary.startsWith('Anti-sanctuary') ? 'red' :
    state.sanctuary.startsWith('Sanctuary') ? 'green' : 'yellow';
  const sanctuaryLabel = state.sanctuary.startsWith('Anti-sanctuary') ? 'Anti-Sanctuary' :
    state.sanctuary.startsWith('Sanctuary') ? 'Sanctuary' : 'Mixed / No Statewide Policy';
  const sanctuaryBg = sanctuaryColor === 'red' ? 'bg-red-50 border-red-200 text-red-800' :
    sanctuaryColor === 'green' ? 'bg-green-50 border-green-200 text-green-800' : 'bg-yellow-50 border-yellow-200 text-yellow-800';

  const newsLinks = state.news.map(([name, url]) =>
    `              <a href="${url}" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="resource-link block px-3 py-2 rounded-lg text-sm"><span class="font-medium text-gray-900">${name}</span></a>`
  ).join('\n');

  const iceOfficesList = Array.isArray(state.iceFieldOffices) ? state.iceFieldOffices.join(', ') : state.iceFieldOffices;

  return `<!DOCTYPE html>

  <script>
    const menuBtn = document.getElementById('mobile-menu-btn');
    const mobileMenu = document.getElementById('mobile-menu');
    const hamburgerIcon = document.getElementById('hamburger-icon');
    const closeIcon = document.getElementById('close-icon');
    menuBtn.addEventListener('click', function () {
      const isOpen = !mobileMenu.classList.contains('hidden');
      mobileMenu.classList.toggle('hidden');
      hamburgerIcon.classList.toggle('hidden');
      closeIcon.classList.toggle('hidden');
      menuBtn.setAttribute('aria-expanded', !isOpen);
    });
  </script>
  <script>
    function submitContactForm(event) {
      event.preventDefault();
      var messageEl = document.getElementById('contact-message');
      var nameEl = document.getElementById('contact-name');
      var emailEl = document.getElementById('contact-email');
      var urlEl = document.getElementById('contact-url');
      var categoryEl = document.getElementById('contact-category');
      var statusEl = document.getElementById('form-status');
      var message = messageEl.value.trim();
      if (!message) return;
      statusEl.textContent = 'Sending...';
      statusEl.className = 'mt-3 text-sm text-gray-400';
      fetch('https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ message: message, name: nameEl.value.trim(), email: emailEl.value.trim(), url: urlEl.value.trim(), category: categoryEl.value, source: 'justnice.us/blog/ice-activity/${state.slug}', page: window.location.pathname, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() })
      }).then(function (r) { if (r.ok) { statusEl.textContent = 'Thank you! Your message has been sent.'; statusEl.className = 'mt-3 text-sm text-green-400'; messageEl.value = ''; nameEl.value = ''; emailEl.value = ''; urlEl.value = ''; categoryEl.value = ''; } else { throw new Error(); } }).catch(function () { statusEl.textContent = 'Something went wrong. Please try again.'; statusEl.className = 'mt-3 text-sm text-red-400'; });
    }
  </script>
  <script src="/translations.js"></script>
  <script>document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', initTranslation);</script>

// Generate all state pages
let created = 0;
let skipped = 0;

for (const state of states) {
  // Skip Texas (already has detailed page)
  if (state.abbr === 'TX') {
    skipped++;
    continue;
  }

  const filePath = path.join(BASE_DIR, `${state.slug}.html`);
  const html = generateStatePage(state);
  fs.writeFileSync(filePath, html, 'utf8');
  created++;
  console.log(`Created: ${state.slug}.html (${state.name})`);
}

console.log(`\nDone! Created ${created} state pages, skipped ${skipped} (Texas already exists).`);

function generateStatePage(state) {
  const isMinnesota = state.abbr === 'MN';
  const mnExistingLink = isMinnesota ? `
          <!-- Full report callout -->
          <div class="bg-gold/10 border-l-4 border-gold rounded-r-lg p-5 mb-10">
            <div class="flex items-start gap-3">
              <svg class="w-6 h-6 text-gold flex-shrink-0 mt-0.5" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-width="2" d="M9 12h6m-6 4h6m2 5H7a2 2 0 01-2-2V5a2 2 0 012-2h5.586a1 1 0 01.707.293l5.414 5.414a1 1 0 01.293.707V19a2 2 0 01-2 2z"/></svg>
              <div>
                <p class="font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-1">Full Detailed Report Available</p>
                <p class="text-sm text-gray-600">For our comprehensive Minnesota ICE activity report with sourced incidents and community response, see: <a href="/blog/mn-ice-activity.html" class="text-gold font-semibold hover:underline">ICE Activity in Minnesota</a>.</p>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>` : '';

  const sanctuaryColor = state.sanctuary.startsWith('Anti-sanctuary') ? 'red' :
    state.sanctuary.startsWith('Sanctuary') ? 'green' : 'yellow';
  const sanctuaryLabel = state.sanctuary.startsWith('Anti-sanctuary') ? 'Anti-Sanctuary' :
    state.sanctuary.startsWith('Sanctuary') ? 'Sanctuary' : 'Mixed / No Statewide Policy';
  const sanctuaryBg = sanctuaryColor === 'red' ? 'bg-red-50 border-red-200 text-red-800' :
    sanctuaryColor === 'green' ? 'bg-green-50 border-green-200 text-green-800' : 'bg-yellow-50 border-yellow-200 text-yellow-800';

  const newsLinks = state.news.map(([name, url]) =>
    `              <a href="${url}" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="resource-link block px-3 py-2 rounded-lg text-sm"><span class="font-medium text-gray-900">${name}</span></a>`
  ).join('\n');

  const iceOfficesList = Array.isArray(state.iceFieldOffices) ? state.iceFieldOffices.join(', ') : state.iceFieldOffices;

  return `<!DOCTYPE html>

  <script>
    const menuBtn = document.getElementById('mobile-menu-btn');
    const mobileMenu = document.getElementById('mobile-menu');
    const hamburgerIcon = document.getElementById('hamburger-icon');
    const closeIcon = document.getElementById('close-icon');
    menuBtn.addEventListener('click', function () {
      const isOpen = !mobileMenu.classList.contains('hidden');
      mobileMenu.classList.toggle('hidden');
      hamburgerIcon.classList.toggle('hidden');
      closeIcon.classList.toggle('hidden');
      menuBtn.setAttribute('aria-expanded', !isOpen);
    });
  </script>
  <script>
    function submitContactForm(event) {
      event.preventDefault();
      var messageEl = document.getElementById('contact-message');
      var nameEl = document.getElementById('contact-name');
      var emailEl = document.getElementById('contact-email');
      var urlEl = document.getElementById('contact-url');
      var categoryEl = document.getElementById('contact-category');
      var statusEl = document.getElementById('form-status');
      var message = messageEl.value.trim();
      if (!message) return;
      statusEl.textContent = 'Sending...';
      statusEl.className = 'mt-3 text-sm text-gray-400';
      fetch('https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ message: message, name: nameEl.value.trim(), email: emailEl.value.trim(), url: urlEl.value.trim(), category: categoryEl.value, source: 'justnice.us/blog/ice-activity/${state.slug}', page: window.location.pathname, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() })
      }).then(function (r) { if (r.ok) { statusEl.textContent = 'Thank you! Your message has been sent.'; statusEl.className = 'mt-3 text-sm text-green-400'; messageEl.value = ''; nameEl.value = ''; emailEl.value = ''; urlEl.value = ''; categoryEl.value = ''; } else { throw new Error(); } }).catch(function () { statusEl.textContent = 'Something went wrong. Please try again.'; statusEl.className = 'mt-3 text-sm text-red-400'; });
    }
  </script>
  <script src="/translations.js"></script>
  <script>document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', initTranslation);</script>
</body>
</html>`;
}

// Generate all state pages
let created = 0;
let skipped = 0;

for (const state of states) {
  // Skip Texas (already has detailed page)
  if (state.abbr === 'TX') {
    skipped++;
    continue;
  }

  const filePath = path.join(BASE_DIR, `${state.slug}.html`);
  const html = generateStatePage(state);
  fs.writeFileSync(filePath, html, 'utf8');
  created++;
  console.log(`Created: ${state.slug}.html (${state.name})`);
}

console.log(`\nDone! Created ${created} state pages, skipped ${skipped} (Texas already exists).`);

content.text:
All tasks complete. Here's the summary:

**Pushed to GitHub** (`ef7efba`): 58 files, 21,819 lines added.

### What was built:
- **US Hub** (`/blog/ice-activity/index.html`) — links all 50 states by region
- **Texas Hub** (`texas.html`) — SB 8 context, enforcement stats, 6 regional links
- **6 Texas Regional Pages** — Austin, DFW, Houston, San Antonio, RGV, El Paso (all with researched, sourced content and footnotes)
- **49 State Skeleton Pages** — each seeded with:
  - ICE field office(s) and area of responsibility
  - 287(g) agreement count and participating counties
  - Sanctuary/anti-sanctuary status with specific legislation
  - Governor and AG stance on ICE cooperation
  - Estimated undocumented population
  - Local news sources in sidebar
  - Tracking tools (ResistMap, ICE in My Area, TRAC, Deportation Data Project)
  - KYR quick card and contact form
  - "Coverage In Progress" callout inviting community reports
- **Minnesota** links to existing `/blog/mn-ice-activity.html`
- **blog.html** — featured ICE Activity Tracker card at top of blog grid
- **n8n workflow spec** — saved in `/docs/` (gitignored, planning doc)

### Still deferred:
- Images with photographer credit (Unsplash/Pexels) — follow-up session
- n8n workflow implementation (spec is written, needs n8n UI setup)

   100→
   101→## When to Add Credentials to n8n vs Use Claude CLI
   102→
   103→> **Decision Rule:** Only add to n8n if the workflow runs WITHOUT Claude (scheduled, webhook-triggered, or multi-service automation).
   104→>
   105→> **Source of truth for credentials:** `docs/reference/MASTER-CREDENTIALS-INVENTORY.md` → n8n Credential IDs section
   106→> **Source of truth for pending tasks:** `docs/TODO.md`
   107→
   108→### n8n Credentials for Workflows

   110→| Service | Why n8n | Use Case | Credential ID |

   112→| **Airtable** | Webhook → database workflows | Form submissions | `bSAwT7gNO9ESeqNc` |
   113→| **Givebutter** | Donation webhook processing | Automated notifications | `WjXA5PIoZEQ0p18L` |
   114→| **Blotato** | Social media scheduling | Scheduled posts | `Bubd9ZIEfpHAYJkp` |
   115→| **Slack** | Workflow notifications | Automated alerts | `a4QIJNM9Kn6yHeca` |
   116→| **Google AI Studio** | AI content generation | Social Media Pipeline | `yOHUvSOQrNAHHHdQ` ✅ |
   117→| **Pexels** | Stock photos/videos | Image automation | `9pdG7cHvotzdS0hK` ✅ |
   118→
   119→### NOT Needed in n8n (Claude CLI Has Direct Access)
   120→
   121→| Service | Claude CLI Access | Why Not n8n |
   122→|---------|-------------------|-------------|
   123→| Pexels | Direct API call | Interactive, not automated |
   124→| OpenAI | Direct API call | Claude handles AI tasks |

   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→## How to Add Credentials in n8n
   143→
   144→### Method 1: Native n8n Credentials (Recommended)
   145→
   146→For services with built-in n8n support:
   147→
   148→1. Go to **Settings** (gear icon) → **Credentials**
   149→2. Click **+ Add Credential**
   150→3. Search for the service name (e.g., "OpenAI", "Slack", "Airtable")
   151→4. Fill in the required fields from 1Password
   152→
   153→**Services with Native Support:**
   154→- OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), Perplexity
   155→- Slack, Notion, Airtable, Linear
   156→- AWS, Google Cloud, Azure
   157→- GitLab, GitHub
   158→
   159→### Method 2: Header Auth for REST APIs
   160→
   161→For APIs that use header-based authentication (Blotato, Givebutter, Pexels, Google AI Studio):
   162→
   163→1. Go to **Settings** → **Credentials**
   164→2. Click **+ Add Credential**
   165→3. Search for **"Header Auth"** (the credential type is called "Header Auth")
   166→4. Configure:
   167→   - **Name**: Descriptive name (e.g., "Blotato API")
   168→   - **Name** (header field): The header key (e.g., `blotato-api-key`, `Authorization`)
   169→   - **Value**: The API key from 1Password
   170→
   171→**Important:** The credential type is called **"Header Auth"** in n8n, not "HTTP Header Auth".
   172→
   173→### Method 3: Query Auth for URL Parameters
   174→
   175→For APIs that need the key in the URL:
   176→
   177→1. Search for **"Query Auth"** in credentials
   178→2. Configure:
   179→   - **Name**: Descriptive name
   180→   - **Name** (query field): Usually `key` or `api_key`
   181→   - **Value**: The API key
   182→
   183→---
   184→

   344→
   345→## Using Credentials in HTTP Request Node
   346→
   347→When you add an HTTP Request node:
   348→
   349→1. Set **Authentication** to **Generic Credential Type**
   350→2. Set **Generic Auth Type** to **Header Auth** (or other type)
   351→3. Select your credential from the dropdown
   352→
   353→**Example: Calling Blotato API**
   354→```
   355→Method: GET
   356→URL: https://backend.blotato.com/v2/users/me/accounts
   357→Authentication: Generic Credential Type
   358→Generic Auth Type: Header Auth
   359→Credential: Blotato API
   360→```

   362→---
   363→
   364→## OAuth vs API Key Comparison
   365→
   366→### When to Use OAuth
   367→
   368→| Use Case | Why OAuth |
   369→|----------|-----------|
   370→| Gmail, Sheets, Drive nodes | Required by Google |
   371→| Any "Google" branded n8n node | These nodes expect OAuth |
   372→| User-delegated access | When acting on behalf of a user |
   373→
   374→### When to Use API Key (Header Auth)
   375→
   376→| Use Case | Why API Key |
   377→|----------|-------------|
   378→| Gemini API via HTTP Request | Simpler, no token refresh |
   379→| Blotato, Givebutter, Pexels | These APIs use header-based auth |
   380→| Claude API (via HTTP Request) | API key authentication |
   381→| OpenAI API (via HTTP Request) | API key authentication |
   382→

   461→
   462→## Troubleshooting
   463→
   464→### "Credential not found" when searching
   465→
   466→- **"HTTP Header Auth" not found:** Search for **"Header Auth"** instead
   467→- **"Query Auth" not found:** Search for **"Query String Auth"**
   468→- For OAuth: Search for the service name (e.g., "Google OAuth2 API")
   469→
   470→### "401 Unauthorized"
   471→
   472→- **OAuth:** Token may have expired → re-authenticate
   473→- **API Key:** Key may be incorrect → verify in 1Password
   474→- **Header name:** Check exact spelling (case-sensitive)
   475→- **Bearer prefix:** Some APIs need `Bearer ` prefix, some don't
   476→
   477→### "OAuth error: invalid_client"
   478→
   479→1. Using wrong OAuth credential (wrong GCP project)
   480→2. OAuth consent screen not configured
   481→3. Authorized redirect URIs don't include n8n URL
   482→
   483→**Fix:** Verify you're using `GCP OAuth - n8n Gmail Integration` from 1Password.
   484→
   485→---
   486→
   487→## Quick Reference: n8n Credential Type Names
   488→
   489→| What You Need | Search For | n8n Credential Type |
   490→|---------------|------------|---------------------|
   491→| API key in header | "Header Auth" | Header Auth |
   492→| API key in URL query | "Query String" | Query String Auth |
   493→| Username/password | "Basic Auth" | Basic Auth |
   494→| OAuth 2.0 | Service name or "OAuth2" | Various OAuth2 types |
   495→| Google services | "Google" | Google OAuth2 API |
   496→| AWS services | "AWS" | AWS Credentials |
   497→
   498→---
   499→

   507→| `n8n Givebutter API Credentials` | Header Auth | `Givebutter API` |

   509→| `Pexels API Key` | Header Auth | `Pexels API` |

# n8n Credentials Guide

> **Instance:** https://n8n.cloudpublica.org
> **Total Credentials:** 29 configured (as of Dec 30, 2025)

---

## Access Methods

| Method | URL | 1Password Item | Use Case |
|--------|-----|----------------|----------|
| REST API | `https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/api/v1/` | `n8n REST API Key (Claudecode)` | Programmatic workflow management |
| Instance MCP | `https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/mcp-server/http` | `n8n Instance MCP Bearer Key (Claudecode)` | AI client connections (Claude Desktop, etc.) |
| Webhooks | `https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/<path>` | None (public) | Trigger workflows from external sources |

### Instance MCP Configuration

For Claude Desktop or other MCP clients, add to config:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "n8n-instance": {
      "command": "/bin/sh",
      "args": ["-c", "npx -y mcp-remote https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/mcp-server/http --header \"Authorization: Bearer $(op read 'op://Engineering/luooictav42nw563febxtes7my/credential')\""]
    }
  }
}
```

---

## When to Add Credentials to n8n vs Use Claude CLI

> **Decision Rule:** Only add to n8n if the workflow runs WITHOUT Claude (scheduled, webhook-triggered, or multi-service automation).
>
> **Source of truth for credentials:** `docs/reference/MASTER-CREDENTIALS-INVENTORY.md` → n8n Credential IDs section
> **Source of truth for pending tasks:** `docs/TODO.md`

| **Givebutter** | Donation webhook processing | Automated notifications | `WjXA5PIoZEQ0p18L` |
| **Blotato** | Social media scheduling | Scheduled posts | `Bubd9ZIEfpHAYJkp` |
| **Slack** | Workflow notifications | Automated alerts | `a4QIJNM9Kn6yHeca` |
| **Google AI Studio** | AI content generation | Social Media Pipeline | `yOHUvSOQrNAHHHdQ` ✅ |
| **Pexels** | Stock photos/videos | Image automation | `9pdG7cHvotzdS0hK` ✅ |

### NOT Needed in n8n (Claude CLI Has Direct Access)

| Service | Claude CLI Access | Why Not n8n |
|---------|-------------------|-------------|
| Pexels | Direct API call | Interactive, not automated |
| OpenAI | Direct API call | Claude handles AI tasks |
| AWS | AWS CLI | CLI more powerful |
| Cloudflare DNS | Direct API | One-off DNS changes |
| GitLab | SSH + API + MCP | Full access already |
| GitHub | SSH + API + MCP + n8n | n8n credential `UbOXTgq7uci489Hj` now available |

| Analytics, BigQuery, Cloud Storage | ✅ OAuth connected |

---

## How to Add Credentials in n8n

### Method 1: Native n8n Credentials (Recommended)

For services with built-in n8n support:

1. Go to **Settings** (gear icon) → **Credentials**
2. Click **+ Add Credential**
3. Search for the service name (e.g., "OpenAI", "Slack", "Airtable")
4. Fill in the required fields from 1Password

**Services with Native Support:**
- OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), Perplexity
- Slack, Notion, Airtable, Linear
- AWS, Google Cloud, Azure
- GitLab, GitHub

### Method 2: Header Auth for REST APIs

For APIs that use header-based authentication (Blotato, Givebutter, Pexels, Google AI Studio):

1. Go to **Settings** → **Credentials**
2. Click **+ Add Credential**
3. Search for **"Header Auth"** (the credential type is called "Header Auth")
4. Configure:
   - **Name**: Descriptive name (e.g., "Blotato API")
   - **Name** (header field): The header key (e.g., `blotato-api-key`, `Authorization`)
   - **Value**: The API key from 1Password

**Important:** The credential type is called **"Header Auth"** in n8n, not "HTTP Header Auth".

### Method 3: Query Auth for URL Parameters

For APIs that need the key in the URL:

1. Search for **"Query Auth"** in credentials
2. Configure:
   - **Name**: Descriptive name
   - **Name** (query field): Usually `key` or `api_key`
   - **Value**: The API key

## Using Credentials in HTTP Request Node

When you add an HTTP Request node:

1. Set **Authentication** to **Generic Credential Type**
2. Set **Generic Auth Type** to **Header Auth** (or other type)
3. Select your credential from the dropdown

**Example: Calling Blotato API**
```
Method: GET
URL: https://backend.blotato.com/v2/users/me/accounts
Authentication: Generic Credential Type
Generic Auth Type: Header Auth
Credential: Blotato API
```

---

## OAuth vs API Key Comparison

### When to Use OAuth

| Use Case | Why OAuth |
|----------|-----------|
| Gmail, Sheets, Drive nodes | Required by Google |
| Any "Google" branded n8n node | These nodes expect OAuth |
| User-delegated access | When acting on behalf of a user |

### When to Use API Key (Header Auth)

| Use Case | Why API Key |
|----------|-------------|
| Gemini API via HTTP Request | Simpler, no token refresh |
| Blotato, Givebutter, Pexels | These APIs use header-based auth |
| Claude API (via HTTP Request) | API key authentication |
| OpenAI API (via HTTP Request) | API key authentication |

---
