Senate Bill 8: Mandatory ICE Cooperation

203→ 204→

Texas Senate Bill 8 took effect January 1, 2026, requiring every county sheriff in a county with a population over 100,000 to apply for a 287(g) agreement with ICE by December 1, 2026. Under 287(g), trained local officers can perform federal immigration enforcement functions — from checking immigration status of jail inmates to assisting with field arrests, depending on the agreement type.[1]

205→ 206→

SB 8 provides three models of cooperation:

207→
    208→
  • Jail Enforcement Model (least aggressive) — Deputies question inmates about immigration status inside the jail
    • 209→
  • Warrant Service Officer — Deputies serve ICE warrants on inmates already in custody
    • 210→
  • Task Force Model (most aggressive) — Officers assist with street-level immigration enforcement
    • 211→
212→ 213→

Counties that fail to apply face potential lawsuits from the Texas Attorney General. The law effectively ends the ability of Texas counties to function as sanctuary jurisdictions.[2]

Enforcement by the Numbers

242→ 243→

Texas accounts for roughly one quarter of all ICE arrests nationwide. Key statistics from January 2025 through March 2026:[3][4]

244→ 245→
246→ 247→ 248→ 249→ 250→ 251→ 252→ 253→ 254→ 255→ 256→ 257→ 258→ 259→ 260→ 261→ 262→ 263→ 264→

Daily ICE arrests in Texas176/dayUp from 85/day under Biden
Dallas field office arrests (Jan–Oct 2025)12,100+108% increase year-over-year
Houston field office arrests (Jan–Oct 2025)17,500+Southeast TX to Waco
Houston week-long operation (Feb 23–Mar 2)646 arrested543 criminal, 7 gang members
Dallas arrestees without criminal convictions62%Up from 44% in 2024
Western District of TX immigration cases12,000+ in 2025240+ new cases first week of Feb 2026 alone
El Paso habeas filings~300 in 2025Up from avg. 26/year (2021–24)

265→
266→ 267→
268→ 269→ 270→

Mega-Detention Facilities

271→ 272→

Texas is at the center of a $38 billion national plan for expanded immigration detention.[5] Three major facility developments are underway:

273→ 274→

Camp East Montana (El Paso)

275→

The nation’s largest ICE detention facility, a tent structure at Fort Bliss holding ~2,954 detainees per day. A detainee died in January 2026 — the El Paso medical examiner ruled it a homicide (asphyxia due to compression). A measles outbreak infected 14+ detainees. As of March 4, ICE is reportedly drafting termination of the $1.2 billion contract.[6]

276→ 277→

San Antonio East Side Warehouse

278→

ICE purchased a 640,000-sq-ft warehouse at 542 SE Loop 410 for $66 million (appraised at $37M). Planned as a 1,500-bed processing facility, operational by November 2026. City Council voted 8–2 to explore moratorium on detention facilities but acknowledged ICE doesn’t need local approval.[7]

279→ 280→

El Paso / Clint Mega-Facility

281→

DHS purchased warehouses near Clint for $123 million, planning an 8,500-capacity facility. El Paso City Council moved to reject it, and Socorro is also blocking. The three reinforced concrete warehouses total ~888,000 sq ft.[8]

282→ 283→

Hutchins (Dallas) — Blocked

284→

DHS identified a 1-million-sq-ft warehouse in Hutchins (pop. 6,000) for 9,500–10,000 beds. After intense community opposition, property owner Majestic Realty Co. rejected the sale on February 16.[9]

285→ 286→
287→ 288→ 289→

Economic Impact

290→ 291→

ICE enforcement is having measurable economic consequences across Texas, particularly in the construction industry:[10]

292→ 293→
    294→
  • 38.6% of Houston-area construction workers are foreign-born noncitizens (Census Bureau)
    • 295→
  • Construction loans in affected regions down ~30% year-over-year
    • 296→
  • 92% of construction businesses struggling to fill positions (AGC survey)
    • 297→
  • 28% of construction firms directly affected by ICE raids
    • 298→
  • Supply chain companies reporting sharp revenue declines; some filing bankruptcy
    • 299→
  • Arbor Realty Trust reports ICE activity directly impacting apartment occupancy in Houston, SA, and DFW
    • 300→
  • 1 in 7 Houston-area residents personally know someone detained or deported (Rice/Kinder Institute survey)
    • 301→
  • Among Hispanic Houstonians: 25% know someone detained
    • 302→
303→ 304→
305→ “There has to be some sort of warrant that needs to be presented when these federal agents show up to construction sites. And there’s no warrants being presented.” — Mario Guerrero, South Texas Builders Association 306→

file.content:
name: Deploy to AWS Lightsail

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: deploy-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Deploy static files
        uses: appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.AWS_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.AWS_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SSH_KEY }}

content.text: Good, I now have a clear picture of all the variations. Let me design the plan.

input.prompt:
Design a build system for justNICE.us that extracts nav and footer into shared partials, with a build step that injects them into each page before deploy.

## Current state
- 78 static HTML pages, no build tools, Tailwind CDN, deployed via SCP from GitHub Actions
- Nav and footer are copy-pasted inline in every page with many inconsistencies
- Each page currently has its own slightly different nav/footer

## Key requirements
1. **Zero client-side JS for nav/footer** — pure static HTML output for maximum speed
2. **Single source of truth** for nav and footer markup
3. **Per-page active link highlighting** — pages need to indicate which nav item is "active" (currently done via different CSS classes: `border-b-2 border-gold pb-0.5`, `underline underline-offset-4 decoration-2`, etc.)
4. **Contact form category dropdown varies** — some pages have "ICE Activity Report" as first option, some don't
5. **data-i18n attributes** present on some pages but not others (translations.js handles i18n client-side)
6. **Breadcrumbs** are per-page (NOT part of the shared nav — they stay in each page's content)
7. **Must work with existing GitHub Actions deploy** (SCP to AWS Lightsail)
8. **No heavy frameworks** — keep it simple (Node.js script, not 11ty/Hugo/etc.)

## Nav variations to handle
- **Active link**: Each page highlights its own section (Blog, KYR, Resources, Downloads, ICE Tracker). The homepage highlights nothing.
- **Nav items are consistent now**: Immigrant Resources, Know Your Rights, ICE Tracker, Blog, Downloads, NICE Gear, Contact, Language toggle, Donate
- **data-i18n**: Should be on ALL nav items (standardize)

## Footer variations
- **Contact form**: Present on most pages but not all (downloads.html, kyr-ice-training.html may not have it)
- **Footer nav links**: Should be standardized
- **Form dropdown categories**: Can be standardized (include ICE Activity Report on all)
- **Copyright bar**: Consistent across all pages

## Design a plan that covers:
1. Directory structure for partials
2. The build script (Node.js) — how it reads partials, finds HTML files, injects content
3. How pages signal their active nav item (e.g., a data attribute or HTML comment)
4. How to handle the contact form presence/absence per page
5. The migration process (strip old nav/footer, add placeholders)
6. GitHub Actions workflow update
7. How to handle future new pages (what a new page template looks like)

Keep it practical and minimal. The build script should be <100 lines.

content.text:
I now have a comprehensive picture. Let me summarize my findings and deliver the plan. --- ## Build System Plan for justNICE.us: Shared Nav/Footer Partials ### Current State Summary After examining the codebase, here is what I found: **78 HTML files** across the repo (50 state ICE tracker pages, ~15 blog posts, plus top-level pages). The nav/footer are copy-pasted with these inconsistencies: **Nav inconsistencies found:** - **3 different active-link styles**: `underline underline-offset-4` (blog.html, downloads.html, anti-surveillance), `border-b-2 border-gold pb-0.5` (most blog posts, state pages), and `text-white font-semibold underline underline-offset-4 decoration-2` (immigrant-resources.html, kyr-ice-training.html) - **Missing nav items**: blog.html nav lacks "ICE Tracker" and "Resource Map"; some state pages are missing "Downloads" link - **data-i18n missing**: State pages (texas.html, nebraska.html, etc.) have zero `data-i18n` attributes on nav items - **Logo image varies**: index.html uses `h-16` with `BEE-ICON-128.webp`, other pages use `h-12` with `BEE-ICON-64.webp` - **Contact link**: index.html uses `#contact-form` (same-page), other pages use `/#contact-form` (cross-page) **Footer variations found:** - **Full footer** (index.html, blog.html, state pages): donate CTA + contact form + about + nav links + copyright bar - **Slim footer** (downloads.html): about section + nav links + copyright bar only (no contact form, no donate CTA) - **Contact form category dropdown differs**: index.html has 6 options (ICE Activity Report, General, Immigrant Resources, KYR, Blog, Other), blog.html has 6 (General, Resources, KYR, Blog, Resource Map, Other), texas.html has only 3 (ICE Activity Report, General, Other) - **Footer nav links differ**: Some include ICE Tracker, some don't; texas.html has ICE Tracker duplicated - **Mobile menu toggle script**: duplicated in every page (sometimes `const`, sometimes `var`) - **Contact form submission script**: duplicated in every page with same webhook URL --- ### 1. Directory Structure ``` justNICE.us/ _partials/ # Source partials (NOT deployed) nav.html # Full header/nav markup footer-full.html # Donate CTA + contact form + about + copyright footer-slim.html # About + nav links + copyright (no form) scripts.html # Mobile menu toggle + contact form JS + translations _build/ # NOT deployed build.js # Node.js build script (<100 lines) src/ # Source HTML pages with placeholders (authoring dir) index.html blog.html downloads.html ... dist/ # Build output (this is what gets deployed) index.html blog.html ... ``` **Alternative (simpler, recommended):** Skip the `src/` directory entirely. Keep source HTML files in place, use HTML comment markers as placeholders, and write output to `dist/`. This avoids moving 78 files. ``` justNICE.us/ _partials/ nav.html footer-full.html footer-slim.html scripts.html build.js # At project root dist/ # Generated; .gitignore this ``` ### 2. How Pages Signal Configuration (the frontmatter-like approach) Each source HTML page gets a single HTML comment at the top of `` that declares its build config: ```html ``` The `nav` value tells the build script which nav item is active. The `footer` value picks which footer partial to inject. Valid `nav` values: | Value | Highlights | |-------|-----------| | `"resources"` | Immigrant Resources | | `"kyr"` | Know Your Rights | | `"tracker"` | ICE Tracker | | `"blog"` | Blog | | `"downloads"` | Downloads | | `"home"` or `""` | Nothing highlighted | Valid `footer` values: `"full"` (with contact form) or `"slim"` (no contact form, used by downloads.html). **Why HTML comments over data attributes on ``?** Because the build script needs to parse this before the nav/footer exist. A comment is invisible, survives any linting, and is trivially regex-matched. ### 3. Placeholder Markers in Source Pages After stripping the current inline nav/footer, each source page gets: ```html
...
``` Three markers: ``, ``, ``. The build script replaces each marker line with the corresponding partial content. ### 4. The Partials **`_partials/nav.html`** -- The canonical nav. Uses a placeholder class `{{ACTIVE:key}}` that the build script replaces: ```html
justNICE
Immigrant Resources Know Your Rights ICE Tracker Blog Downloads NICE Gear Contact Donate
Immigrant Resources
``` The standardized active style is `underline underline-offset-4 decoration-2` -- already the most common pattern across the site. The build script replaces `{{ACTIVE:blog}}` with that class string when the page's config `nav` value is `"blog"`, and replaces all other `{{ACTIVE:*}}` tokens with empty string. **`_partials/footer-full.html`** -- The full footer with donate CTA, contact form (standardized dropdown with all 6 categories + ICE Activity Report), about section, nav links, and copyright bar. Contact form has the standardized `data-i18n` attributes on all elements. **`_partials/footer-slim.html`** -- About section + nav links + copyright bar only. Used for pages that don't need the contact form (downloads.html). **`_partials/scripts.html`** -- The shared scripts block: ```html ``` ### 5. The Build Script (`build.js`) Under 100 lines of zero-dependency Node.js: ```javascript #!/usr/bin/env node const fs = require('fs'); const path = require('path'); const ROOT = __dirname; const PARTIALS_DIR = path.join(ROOT, '_partials'); const DIST_DIR = path.join(ROOT, 'dist'); const ACTIVE_CLASS = 'underline underline-offset-4 decoration-2'; // Load partials const partials = { nav: fs.readFileSync(path.join(PARTIALS_DIR, 'nav.html'), 'utf8'), 'footer-full': fs.readFileSync(path.join(PARTIALS_DIR, 'footer-full.html'), 'utf8'), 'footer-slim': fs.readFileSync(path.join(PARTIALS_DIR, 'footer-slim.html'), 'utf8'), scripts: fs.readFileSync(path.join(PARTIALS_DIR, 'scripts.html'), 'utf8'), }; // Find all HTML files (excluding _partials, dist, node_modules, BACKUP) function findHtmlFiles(dir) { let results = []; for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true })) { const full = path.join(dir, entry.name); if (entry.name.startsWith('_') || entry.name === 'dist' || entry.name === 'node_modules') continue; if (entry.name.startsWith('BACKUP')) continue; if (entry.isDirectory()) results = results.concat(findHtmlFiles(full)); else if (entry.name.endsWith('.html')) results.push(full); } return results; } // Parse config from function parseConfig(html) { const m = html.match(//); if (!m) return null; try { return JSON.parse(m[1]); } catch { return null; } } // Process active nav: replace {{ACTIVE:key}} tokens function applyActiveNav(navHtml, activeKey) { return navHtml .replace(new RegExp(`\\{\\{ACTIVE:${activeKey}\\}\\}`, 'g'), ACTIVE_CLASS) .replace(/\{\{ACTIVE:\w+\}\}/g, ''); } // Main const htmlFiles = findHtmlFiles(ROOT); let processed = 0; for (const file of htmlFiles) { let html = fs.readFileSync(file, 'utf8'); const config = parseConfig(html); if (!config) { // No build markers -- copy as-is const rel = path.relative(ROOT, file); const dest = path.join(DIST_DIR, rel); fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(dest), { recursive: true }); fs.copyFileSync(file, dest); continue; } // Build nav with active highlighting const nav = applyActiveNav(partials.nav, config.nav || ''); // Pick footer variant const footerKey = config.footer === 'slim' ? 'footer-slim' : 'footer-full'; const footer = partials[footerKey]; // Replace markers html = html .replace(//, nav) .replace(//, footer) .replace(//, partials.scripts); // Remove the config comment from output html = html.replace(/\n?/, ''); // Write to dist const rel = path.relative(ROOT, file); const dest = path.join(DIST_DIR, rel); fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(dest), { recursive: true }); fs.writeFileSync(dest, html); processed++; } console.log(`Built ${processed} pages -> dist/`); ``` That is ~70 lines. Zero dependencies. Uses only `fs` and `path`. **Key behavior:** - Files without `` are copied unchanged to `dist/` (safe fallback for incremental migration) - The config comment is stripped from the final output - Active nav class is applied by simple string replacement - Footer variant selected by config key ### 6. Migration Process This is the most labor-intensive part. The recommended approach is incremental: **Phase 1: Set up infrastructure (1 step)** - Create `_partials/` directory with the 4 partial files - Create `build.js` at project root - Add `dist/` to `.gitignore` - Update GitHub Actions (see section 7) **Phase 2: Migrate top-level pages first (5 pages)** - Start with `index.html`, `blog.html`, `downloads.html`, `immigrant-resources.html`, `kyr-ice-training.html` - For each page: 1. Add `` after `` 2. Replace everything from `
` through `
` (end of mobile menu div) with `` 3. Replace everything from `
` through the closing `` tags with `` and `` 4. Remove the duplicated mobile menu toggle and contact form scripts 5. Keep breadcrumbs and all page-specific content intact - Test locally with `node build.js && npx serve dist` **Phase 3: Migrate state ICE tracker pages (50 pages)** - These are highly uniform. A scripted find-and-replace can handle most of them since the nav/footer structure is consistent across state pages. - All get `{"nav": "tracker", "footer": "full"}` **Phase 4: Migrate blog posts (15 pages)** - All get `{"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"}` - Some older posts have slightly different nav structures but the replacement approach is the same **Scripted migration helper** (not the build script -- a one-time migration tool): The migration for state pages can be semi-automated: read each HTML file, find the `
` opening tag and the corresponding `
`, replace that block. Find `
` and everything after it up to ``, replace with markers. This could be a second Node.js script (not deployed) that does the bulk stripping. ### 7. GitHub Actions Workflow Update The deploy workflow changes to add a build step before SCP: ```yaml name: Deploy to AWS Lightsail on: push: branches: [main] workflow_dispatch: concurrency: group: deploy-${{ github.ref }} cancel-in-progress: true jobs: deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest timeout-minutes: 5 steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - name: Setup Node.js uses: actions/setup-node@v4 with: node-version: '20' - name: Build static site run: node build.js - name: Copy non-HTML assets to dist run: | # Copy assets that don't go through the build cp -r assets dist/ cp -r downloads dist/ 2>/dev/null || true cp resources.json translations.js robots.txt sitemap.xml nginx.conf env.example.js CONTRIBUTING.md dist/ 2>/dev/null || true - name: Deploy static files uses: appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7 with: host: ${{ secrets.AWS_HOST }} username: ${{ secrets.AWS_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SSH_KEY }} source: "dist/*" target: "/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us" strip_components: 1 ``` Key changes: - Added `setup-node` step - Added `node build.js` step - Added a step to copy non-HTML assets into `dist/` - Changed SCP source to `dist/*` with `strip_components: 1` so the deploy target path stays the same - The `strip_components: 1` removes the `dist/` prefix so files land at `/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us/` directly ### 8. New Page Template When creating a new page, the author creates an HTML file with this structure: ```html Page Title -- justNICE
  1. Home
  2. /
  3. Page Title

Page Title

``` That is the complete template. The author only writes the `` and `
` content; nav, footer, and scripts are injected at build time. ### 9. Local Development Add a convenience script to `package.json` (create one if it doesn't exist): ```json { "scripts": { "build": "node build.js", "dev": "node build.js && npx serve dist" } } ``` No `npm install` needed -- `serve` via `npx` is fine for local preview, and `build.js` has zero dependencies. ### Potential Challenges 1. **index.html Contact link**: The homepage currently uses `#contact-form` (same-page anchor) while other pages use `/#contact-form`. The nav partial uses `/#contact-form` which works everywhere including the homepage (it just adds a slash). This is fine. 2. **index.html logo size**: Currently uses `h-16` while other pages use `h-12`. The partial standardizes to `h-12`. toolUseResult.prompt:
Design a build system for justNICE.us that extracts nav and footer into shared partials, with a build step that injects them into each page before deploy.

## Current state
- 78 static HTML pages, no build tools, Tailwind CDN, deployed via SCP from GitHub Actions
- Nav and footer are copy-pasted inline in every page with many inconsistencies
- Each page currently has its own slightly different nav/footer

## Key requirements
1. **Zero client-side JS for nav/footer** — pure static HTML output for maximum speed
2. **Single source of truth** for nav and footer markup
3. **Per-page active link highlighting** — pages need to indicate which nav item is "active" (currently done via different CSS classes: `border-b-2 border-gold pb-0.5`, `underline underline-offset-4 decoration-2`, etc.)
4. **Contact form category dropdown varies** — some pages have "ICE Activity Report" as first option, some don't
5. **data-i18n attributes** present on some pages but not others (translations.js handles i18n client-side)
6. **Breadcrumbs** are per-page (NOT part of the shared nav — they stay in each page's content)
7. **Must work with existing GitHub Actions deploy** (SCP to AWS Lightsail)
8. **No heavy frameworks** — keep it simple (Node.js script, not 11ty/Hugo/etc.)

## Nav variations to handle
- **Active link**: Each page highlights its own section (Blog, KYR, Resources, Downloads, ICE Tracker). The homepage highlights nothing.
- **Nav items are consistent now**: Immigrant Resources, Know Your Rights, ICE Tracker, Blog, Downloads, NICE Gear, Contact, Language toggle, Donate
- **data-i18n**: Should be on ALL nav items (standardize)

## Footer variations
- **Contact form**: Present on most pages but not all (downloads.html, kyr-ice-training.html may not have it)
- **Footer nav links**: Should be standardized
- **Form dropdown categories**: Can be standardized (include ICE Activity Report on all)
- **Copyright bar**: Consistent across all pages

## Design a plan that covers:
1. Directory structure for partials
2. The build script (Node.js) — how it reads partials, finds HTML files, injects content
3. How pages signal their active nav item (e.g., a data attribute or HTML comment)
4. How to handle the contact form presence/absence per page
5. The migration process (strip old nav/footer, add placeholders)
6. GitHub Actions workflow update
7. How to handle future new pages (what a new page template looks like)

Keep it practical and minimal. The build script should be <100 lines.

content.text:
I now have a comprehensive picture. Let me summarize my findings and deliver the plan. --- ## Build System Plan for justNICE.us: Shared Nav/Footer Partials ### Current State Summary After examining the codebase, here is what I found: **78 HTML files** across the repo (50 state ICE tracker pages, ~15 blog posts, plus top-level pages). The nav/footer are copy-pasted with these inconsistencies: **Nav inconsistencies found:** - **3 different active-link styles**: `underline underline-offset-4` (blog.html, downloads.html, anti-surveillance), `border-b-2 border-gold pb-0.5` (most blog posts, state pages), and `text-white font-semibold underline underline-offset-4 decoration-2` (immigrant-resources.html, kyr-ice-training.html) - **Missing nav items**: blog.html nav lacks "ICE Tracker" and "Resource Map"; some state pages are missing "Downloads" link - **data-i18n missing**: State pages (texas.html, nebraska.html, etc.) have zero `data-i18n` attributes on nav items - **Logo image varies**: index.html uses `h-16` with `BEE-ICON-128.webp`, other pages use `h-12` with `BEE-ICON-64.webp` - **Contact link**: index.html uses `#contact-form` (same-page), other pages use `/#contact-form` (cross-page) **Footer variations found:** - **Full footer** (index.html, blog.html, state pages): donate CTA + contact form + about + nav links + copyright bar - **Slim footer** (downloads.html): about section + nav links + copyright bar only (no contact form, no donate CTA) - **Contact form category dropdown differs**: index.html has 6 options (ICE Activity Report, General, Immigrant Resources, KYR, Blog, Other), blog.html has 6 (General, Resources, KYR, Blog, Resource Map, Other), texas.html has only 3 (ICE Activity Report, General, Other) - **Footer nav links differ**: Some include ICE Tracker, some don't; texas.html has ICE Tracker duplicated - **Mobile menu toggle script**: duplicated in every page (sometimes `const`, sometimes `var`) - **Contact form submission script**: duplicated in every page with same webhook URL --- ### 1. Directory Structure ``` justNICE.us/ _partials/ # Source partials (NOT deployed) nav.html # Full header/nav markup footer-full.html # Donate CTA + contact form + about + copyright footer-slim.html # About + nav links + copyright (no form) scripts.html # Mobile menu toggle + contact form JS + translations _build/ # NOT deployed build.js # Node.js build script (<100 lines) src/ # Source HTML pages with placeholders (authoring dir) index.html blog.html downloads.html ... dist/ # Build output (this is what gets deployed) index.html blog.html ... ``` **Alternative (simpler, recommended):** Skip the `src/` directory entirely. Keep source HTML files in place, use HTML comment markers as placeholders, and write output to `dist/`. This avoids moving 78 files. ``` justNICE.us/ _partials/ nav.html footer-full.html footer-slim.html scripts.html build.js # At project root dist/ # Generated; .gitignore this ``` ### 2. How Pages Signal Configuration (the frontmatter-like approach) Each source HTML page gets a single HTML comment at the top of `` that declares its build config: ```html ``` The `nav` value tells the build script which nav item is active. The `footer` value picks which footer partial to inject. Valid `nav` values: | Value | Highlights | |-------|-----------| | `"resources"` | Immigrant Resources | | `"kyr"` | Know Your Rights | | `"tracker"` | ICE Tracker | | `"blog"` | Blog | | `"downloads"` | Downloads | | `"home"` or `""` | Nothing highlighted | Valid `footer` values: `"full"` (with contact form) or `"slim"` (no contact form, used by downloads.html). **Why HTML comments over data attributes on ``?** Because the build script needs to parse this before the nav/footer exist. A comment is invisible, survives any linting, and is trivially regex-matched. ### 3. Placeholder Markers in Source Pages After stripping the current inline nav/footer, each source page gets: ```html
...
``` Three markers: ``, ``, ``. The build script replaces each marker line with the corresponding partial content. ### 4. The Partials **`_partials/nav.html`** -- The canonical nav. Uses a placeholder class `{{ACTIVE:key}}` that the build script replaces: ```html
justNICE
Immigrant Resources Know Your Rights ICE Tracker Blog Downloads NICE Gear Contact Donate
Immigrant Resources
``` The standardized active style is `underline underline-offset-4 decoration-2` -- already the most common pattern across the site. The build script replaces `{{ACTIVE:blog}}` with that class string when the page's config `nav` value is `"blog"`, and replaces all other `{{ACTIVE:*}}` tokens with empty string. **`_partials/footer-full.html`** -- The full footer with donate CTA, contact form (standardized dropdown with all 6 categories + ICE Activity Report), about section, nav links, and copyright bar. Contact form has the standardized `data-i18n` attributes on all elements. **`_partials/footer-slim.html`** -- About section + nav links + copyright bar only. Used for pages that don't need the contact form (downloads.html). **`_partials/scripts.html`** -- The shared scripts block: ```html ``` ### 5. The Build Script (`build.js`) Under 100 lines of zero-dependency Node.js: ```javascript #!/usr/bin/env node const fs = require('fs'); const path = require('path'); const ROOT = __dirname; const PARTIALS_DIR = path.join(ROOT, '_partials'); const DIST_DIR = path.join(ROOT, 'dist'); const ACTIVE_CLASS = 'underline underline-offset-4 decoration-2'; // Load partials const partials = { nav: fs.readFileSync(path.join(PARTIALS_DIR, 'nav.html'), 'utf8'), 'footer-full': fs.readFileSync(path.join(PARTIALS_DIR, 'footer-full.html'), 'utf8'), 'footer-slim': fs.readFileSync(path.join(PARTIALS_DIR, 'footer-slim.html'), 'utf8'), scripts: fs.readFileSync(path.join(PARTIALS_DIR, 'scripts.html'), 'utf8'), }; // Find all HTML files (excluding _partials, dist, node_modules, BACKUP) function findHtmlFiles(dir) { let results = []; for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true })) { const full = path.join(dir, entry.name); if (entry.name.startsWith('_') || entry.name === 'dist' || entry.name === 'node_modules') continue; if (entry.name.startsWith('BACKUP')) continue; if (entry.isDirectory()) results = results.concat(findHtmlFiles(full)); else if (entry.name.endsWith('.html')) results.push(full); } return results; } // Parse config from function parseConfig(html) { const m = html.match(//); if (!m) return null; try { return JSON.parse(m[1]); } catch { return null; } } // Process active nav: replace {{ACTIVE:key}} tokens function applyActiveNav(navHtml, activeKey) { return navHtml .replace(new RegExp(`\\{\\{ACTIVE:${activeKey}\\}\\}`, 'g'), ACTIVE_CLASS) .replace(/\{\{ACTIVE:\w+\}\}/g, ''); } // Main const htmlFiles = findHtmlFiles(ROOT); let processed = 0; for (const file of htmlFiles) { let html = fs.readFileSync(file, 'utf8'); const config = parseConfig(html); if (!config) { // No build markers -- copy as-is const rel = path.relative(ROOT, file); const dest = path.join(DIST_DIR, rel); fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(dest), { recursive: true }); fs.copyFileSync(file, dest); continue; } // Build nav with active highlighting const nav = applyActiveNav(partials.nav, config.nav || ''); // Pick footer variant const footerKey = config.footer === 'slim' ? 'footer-slim' : 'footer-full'; const footer = partials[footerKey]; // Replace markers html = html .replace(//, nav) .replace(//, footer) .replace(//, partials.scripts); // Remove the config comment from output html = html.replace(/\n?/, ''); // Write to dist const rel = path.relative(ROOT, file); const dest = path.join(DIST_DIR, rel); fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(dest), { recursive: true }); fs.writeFileSync(dest, html); processed++; } console.log(`Built ${processed} pages -> dist/`); ``` That is ~70 lines. Zero dependencies. Uses only `fs` and `path`. **Key behavior:** - Files without `` are copied unchanged to `dist/` (safe fallback for incremental migration) - The config comment is stripped from the final output - Active nav class is applied by simple string replacement - Footer variant selected by config key ### 6. Migration Process This is the most labor-intensive part. The recommended approach is incremental: **Phase 1: Set up infrastructure (1 step)** - Create `_partials/` directory with the 4 partial files - Create `build.js` at project root - Add `dist/` to `.gitignore` - Update GitHub Actions (see section 7) **Phase 2: Migrate top-level pages first (5 pages)** - Start with `index.html`, `blog.html`, `downloads.html`, `immigrant-resources.html`, `kyr-ice-training.html` - For each page: 1. Add `` after `` 2. Replace everything from `
` through `
` (end of mobile menu div) with `` 3. Replace everything from `
` through the closing `` tags with `` and `` 4. Remove the duplicated mobile menu toggle and contact form scripts 5. Keep breadcrumbs and all page-specific content intact - Test locally with `node build.js && npx serve dist` **Phase 3: Migrate state ICE tracker pages (50 pages)** - These are highly uniform. A scripted find-and-replace can handle most of them since the nav/footer structure is consistent across state pages. - All get `{"nav": "tracker", "footer": "full"}` **Phase 4: Migrate blog posts (15 pages)** - All get `{"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"}` - Some older posts have slightly different nav structures but the replacement approach is the same **Scripted migration helper** (not the build script -- a one-time migration tool): The migration for state pages can be semi-automated: read each HTML file, find the `
` opening tag and the corresponding `
`, replace that block. Find `
` and everything after it up to ``, replace with markers. This could be a second Node.js script (not deployed) that does the bulk stripping. ### 7. GitHub Actions Workflow Update The deploy workflow changes to add a build step before SCP: ```yaml name: Deploy to AWS Lightsail on: push: branches: [main] workflow_dispatch: concurrency: group: deploy-${{ github.ref }} cancel-in-progress: true jobs: deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest timeout-minutes: 5 steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - name: Setup Node.js uses: actions/setup-node@v4 with: node-version: '20' - name: Build static site run: node build.js - name: Copy non-HTML assets to dist run: | # Copy assets that don't go through the build cp -r assets dist/ cp -r downloads dist/ 2>/dev/null || true cp resources.json translations.js robots.txt sitemap.xml nginx.conf env.example.js CONTRIBUTING.md dist/ 2>/dev/null || true - name: Deploy static files uses: appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7 with: host: ${{ secrets.AWS_HOST }} username: ${{ secrets.AWS_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SSH_KEY }} source: "dist/*" target: "/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us" strip_components: 1 ``` Key changes: - Added `setup-node` step - Added `node build.js` step - Added a step to copy non-HTML assets into `dist/` - Changed SCP source to `dist/*` with `strip_components: 1` so the deploy target path stays the same - The `strip_components: 1` removes the `dist/` prefix so files land at `/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us/` directly ### 8. New Page Template When creating a new page, the author creates an HTML file with this structure: ```html Page Title -- justNICE
  1. Home
  2. /
  3. Page Title

Page Title

``` That is the complete template. The author only writes the `` and `
` content; nav, footer, and scripts are injected at build time. ### 9. Local Development Add a convenience script to `package.json` (create one if it doesn't exist): ```json { "scripts": { "build": "node build.js", "dev": "node build.js && npx serve dist" } } ``` No `npm install` needed -- `serve` via `npx` is fine for local preview, and `build.js` has zero dependencies. ### Potential Challenges 1. **index.html Contact link**: The homepage currently uses `#contact-form` (same-page anchor) while other pages use `/#contact-form`. The nav partial uses `/#contact-form` which works everywhere including the homepage (it just adds a slash). This is fine. 2. **index.html logo size**: Currently uses `h-16` while other pages use `h-12`. The partial standardizes to `h-12`. If the homepage needs a bigger logo, that could be handled by adding a `logoSize` config option, but I would recommend just standardizing to `h-12` to keep it simple. 3. **Per-page `

input.content:
# Plan: Shared Nav/Footer Build System for justNICE.us

## Context

justNICE.us has 78 static HTML pages with nav and footer copy-pasted inline. Every time a nav link is added (like ICE Tracker), all 73+ files must be edited individually. The nav/footer markup is inconsistent across pages (different active-link styles, missing `data-i18n` attributes, different footer link sets, varying contact form dropdowns).

**Goal**: Extract nav and footer into shared partials with a zero-dependency Node.js build script that injects them at deploy time. Zero client-side JS overhead — pure static HTML output.

## Architecture

### How it works
1. `_partials/` directory holds 4 shared HTML files (nav, footer-full, footer-slim, scripts)
2. Each source page has `<!-- build:config -->`, `<!-- build:nav -->`, `<!-- build:footer -->`, `<!-- build:scripts -->` markers
3. `build.js` (root, ~70 lines, zero deps) reads partials, finds all HTML files, replaces markers, writes to `dist/`
4. GitHub Actions runs `node build.js` before SCP deploy
5. Pages without markers are copied as-is (safe incremental migration)

### Per-page configuration

Each page declares its config in an HTML comment after `<body>`:
```html
<body class="font-sans text-gray-900 bg-white">
<!-- build:config {"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"} -->

<!-- build:nav -->

  <!-- breadcrumbs and page content stay here -->

<!-- build:footer -->
<!-- build:scripts -->
</body>
```

**`nav` values**: `"home"`, `"resources"`, `"kyr"`, `"tracker"`, `"blog"`, `"downloads"` — controls which nav item gets the active underline style
**`footer` values**: `"full"` (donate CTA + contact form + about + copyright) or `"slim"` (about + copyright only)

### Active link handling

The nav partial uses `{{ACTIVE:key}}` tokens in each link's class attribute. The build script replaces the matching token with `underline underline-offset-4 decoration-2` and strips all non-matching tokens. This standardizes the active style across all pages.

### Directory structure
```
justNICE.us/
  _partials/
    nav.html              # Full <header> with desktop + mobile nav
    footer-full.html      # Donate CTA + contact form + about + nav links + copyright
    footer-slim.html      # About + nav links + copyright (no form)
    scripts.html          # Mobile menu toggle + contact form JS + translations init
  build.js                # Node.js build script (zero deps)
  dist/                   # Build output (deployed, .gitignored)
  .github/workflows/deploy.yml  # Updated with build step
```

## Implementation Steps

### Phase 1: Create build infrastructure
1. Create `_partials/nav.html` — extract canonical nav from `index.html`, standardize:
   - All 7 nav items with `data-i18n` attributes
   - `{{ACTIVE:key}}` tokens on each link
   - `h-12` logo (standardized)
   - `/#contact-form` for contact link (works everywhere)
   - Both desktop and mobile navs in one file
2. Create `_partials/footer-full.html` — extract from `index.html`, standardize:
   - Donate CTA section
   - Contact form with all categories (ICE Activity Report, General, Immigrant Resources, KYR, Blog, Other)
   - About section with standardized footer nav links (Resources, KYR, ICE Tracker, Blog, Downloads, NICE Gear, Contact, Donate)
   - Copyright bar with justnice.us, email, GitHub, GD org credit
   - All elements get `data-i18n` attributes
3. Create `_partials/footer-slim.html` — footer without contact form or donate CTA
4. Create `_partials/scripts.html` — mobile menu toggle, contact form handler, translations init
5. Create `build.js` at project root
6. Add `dist/` to `.gitignore`

### Phase 2: Update GitHub Actions
- Add Node.js setup step (`actions/setup-node@v4`, node 20)
- Add `node build.js` step
- Add asset copy step (copy non-HTML files to `dist/`)
- Change SCP source to `dist/*` with `strip_components: 1`

### Phase 3: Migrate top-level pages (5 pages)
For each of: `index.html`, `blog.html`, `downloads.html`, `immigrant-resources.html`, `kyr-ice-training.html`:
1. Add `<!-- build:config {"nav": "...", "footer": "..."} -->` after `<body>`
2. Replace `<header>...</header>` (including mobile menu div) with `<!-- build:nav -->`
3. Replace `<footer>...</footer>` and trailing scripts with `<!-- build:footer -->` + `<!-- build:scripts -->`
4. Keep breadcrumbs and page content intact
5. Remove duplicated inline scripts (menu toggle, form handler, translation init)

Config per page:
- `index.html`: `{"nav": "home", "footer": "full"}`
- `blog.html`: `{"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"}`
- `downloads.html`: `{"nav": "downloads", "footer": "slim"}`
- `immigrant-resources.html`: `{"nav": "resources", "footer": "full"}`
- `kyr-ice-training.html`: `{"nav": "kyr", "footer": "full"}`

### Phase 4: Migrate ICE tracker pages (56 pages)
Write a one-time migration script that:
1. Finds all `blog/ice-activity/*.html` and `blog/ice-activity/texas/*.html`
2. Strips `<header>...</header>` block and replaces with `<!-- build:nav -->`
3. Strips `<footer>...</footer>` and trailing scripts, replaces with `<!-- build:footer -->` + `<!-- build:scripts -->`
4. Inserts `<!-- build:config {"nav": "tracker", "footer": "full"} -->` after `<body>`

All get `{"nav": "tracker", "footer": "full"}`.

### Phase 5: Migrate blog posts (~15 pages)
Same migration approach. All get `{"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"}`.

### Phase 6: Verify and deploy
1. Run `node build.js` locally
2. Verify `dist/` output: spot-check 5 pages (index, a state page, a blog post, downloads, kyr)
3. Check all pages have correct nav, footer, active links, `data-i18n` attributes
4. Commit and push — GitHub Actions builds and deploys
5. Purge Cloudflare cache
6. Verify live pages

## Key files to modify
- `index.html` — reference for extracting canonical nav/footer partials; then strip inline nav/footer
- `downloads.html` — reference for slim footer; then strip
- `blog/ice-activity/texas.html` — representative state page for bulk migration script
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — add build step
- `blog/*.html` (15 files) — strip inline nav/footer
- `blog/ice-activity/*.html` + `blog/ice-activity/texas/*.html` (56 files) — strip inline nav/footer

## New files to create
- `_partials/nav.html`
- `_partials/footer-full.html`
- `_partials/footer-slim.html`
- `_partials/scripts.html`
- `build.js`
- `.gitignore` (add `dist/`)

## New page template

Future pages only need `<head>` + page content:
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en" class="scroll-smooth">
<head>
  <!-- meta, title, Tailwind CDN, fonts -->
</head>
<body class="font-sans text-gray-900 bg-white">
<!-- build:config {"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"} -->
<!-- build:nav -->

  <main>
    <!-- your content -->
  </main>

<!-- build:footer -->
<!-- build:scripts -->
</body>
</html>
```

## Verification
- `node build.js` produces `dist/` with all 78 pages
- Every page in `dist/` has the canonical nav and footer HTML (no `{{ACTIVE:...}}` tokens remaining)
- Active links are correctly highlighted per page
- `data-i18n` attributes present on all nav/footer elements
- Contact form submits to `https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact`
- Mobile menu toggle works
- Language switcher works
- No broken links in nav or footer
- GitHub Actions deploys successfully
- Live site renders correctly on desktop and mobile

- `kyr-ice-training.html`: `{"nav": "kyr", "footer": "full"}`

### Phase 4: Migrate ICE tracker pages (56 pages)
Write a one-time migration script that:

Future pages only need `<head>` + page content:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>

<head>

</head>
<body class="font-sans text-gray-900 bg-white">
<!-- build:config {"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"} -->
<!-- build:nav -->

  <main>
    <!-- your content -->
  </main>

<!-- build:footer -->
<!-- build:scripts -->
</body>
</html>
```

- Mobile menu toggle works
- Language switcher works

- GitHub Actions deploys successfully
- Live site renders correctly on desktop and mobile

# Plan: 50-State ICE Activity Tracker for justNICE ## Context justNICE already has two ICE activity blog posts (ATX and MN). The user wants to: 1. Expand Austin → full Texas statewide coverage (hub + regional pages) 2. Create skeleton pages for all 50 states 3. Build an n8n automation pipeline for daily updates 4. Compile a comprehensive resources sidebar linking 19+ tracking tools/sources ## Architecture ### URL Structure ``` /blog/ice-activity/ ← US hub/index page (links to all 50 states) /blog/ice-activity/texas.html ← Texas hub (links to regional pages) /blog/ice-activity/texas/austin.html ← Austin regional (existing ATX post, moved) /blog/ice-activity/texas/dfw.html ← Dallas-Fort Worth /blog/ice-activity/texas/houston.html ← Houston / Harris County /blog/ice-activity/texas/san-antonio.html /blog/ice-activity/texas/rio-grande-valley.html /blog/ice-activity/texas/el-paso.html /blog/ice-activity/texas/other.html ← Lubbock, Corpus, Tyler, Amarillo, etc. /blog/ice-activity/minnesota.html ← MN (existing, moved) /blog/ice-activity/california.html ← Skeleton /blog/ice-activity/florida.html ← Skeleton ... (all 50 states) ``` ### What gets built TODAY (this session) #### Phase 1: Texas statewide page (`texas.html`) - **Hub page** with statewide context (SB 8, Abbott's actions, DPS 287(g), enforcement stats, school/church impacts, construction industry impact, deportation flights, legal challenges) - Links to regional sub-pages - "Last updated" timestamp and reverse-chronological entry format - Resources sidebar with all 19+ tracking tools #### Phase 2: Texas regional pages (6 pages) Using research from the TX agent, create pages for: 1. **Austin** — move existing ATX content, add Feb-Mar 2026 updates 2. **DFW** — 84 arrests/day, probation office arrests, 100 arrests/day field office 3. **Houston** — 646 arrests Feb-Mar, Harris County jail detainers, HPD policy changes 4. **San Antonio** — 7-hour city council meeting, $66M warehouse purchase, vehicle ramming, home raid 5. **Rio Grande Valley** — Mariachi family detention, worksite raids, LUPE signs, Dilley 6. **El Paso** — Camp East Montana (homicide ruling, measles, possible closure), construction raids, National Shutdown #### Phase 3: US hub + 49 state pages (seeded with data) - **US index page** with list linking to all 50 states, organized by region - **Every state page** gets researched and seeded with: - State's ICE field office(s) and AOR (area of responsibility) - 287(g) agreements count and key participating counties - State-level sanctuary/anti-sanctuary laws - Known incidents from Jan-Mar 2026 (web search per state) - Key local news sources for that state - Governor/AG stance on ICE cooperation - This requires ~50 web searches (one per state cluster) — will batch via agents - Pages with no known 2026 activity still get the policy context + "Submit a report" callout #### Phase 4: Resources sidebar (shared across all pages) A standardized sidebar/section on every page linking to: **Structured Data Sources:** - Deportation Data Project (deportationdata.org) — Excel/ZIP downloads - TRAC Immigration (tracreports.org) — queryable court data - CourtListener (courtlistener.com) — federal court API - FlightAware ICE fleet (fleet code RPN) — live deportation flights **Tracking Maps & Tools:** - DetentionReports.com — facility-level detention data - ICE List (icelist.is) — wiki of incidents, agents, facilities - ResistMap (resistmap.com) — crowdsourced ICE sighting map - DeFlock.me — ALPR camera map - HaveIBeenFlocked.com — plate reader lookup - ICE in My Area (iceinmyarea.org) — SMS alerts - Immigrant Defense Project ICEwatch — raid tactics map - Deportation Tracker Live (deportationtracker.live) — real-time tracker **Journalism:** - Drop Site News (dropsitenews.com) — Substack, RSS available - ProPublica Immigration (propublica.org/topics/immigration) - TRAC Reports (tracreports.org) - Witness at the Border → Human Rights First ICE Flight Monitor **Legal & Policy:** - Immigration Policy Tracking Project (immpolicytracking.org) — Stanford/Yale - National Immigration Litigation Alliance — Litigation Navigator - ACLU ICE Tracking — FOIA documents - American Immigration Council — Map the Impact tool #### Phase 5: n8n automation workflow Design an n8n workflow that: 1. **Monitors RSS feeds** — Drop Site News, ProPublica, state-specific news sources 2. **Checks structured APIs** — CourtListener (for new immigration rulings), TRAC (for new data releases) 3. **Scrapes key pages** — DetentionReports.com (biweekly ICE data), ICE.gov press releases 4. **Generates daily digest** — summarized by state, with source URLs 5. **Creates draft entries** — formatted HTML snippets ready to paste into state pages 6. **Sends notification** — Slack/email to human for review before publishing n8n workflow nodes: - Schedule Trigger (daily at 6am CT) - HTTP Request nodes for RSS feeds (10+ feeds) - HTML Extract nodes for scraping - AI Agent node (Claude) for summarization and state classification - Code node for HTML formatting - Webhook/notification for review queue ### Template Structure Each state page follows the same HTML template based on the existing ATX/MN pattern: - justNICE header/nav (copy from existing) - Breadcrumb: Home / Blog / ICE Activity / [State] - Article header with state name, last-updated date - "How to submit a report" callout box - Content area: entries in reverse chronological order (newest first) - Each entry: `

` with date + location, `

` with sourced narrative, footnote refs - Resources sidebar - Footnotes section - justNICE footer ### Key Files to Modify - `/blog.html` — add ICE Activity Tracker section/card linking to hub - Existing ATX post stays at current URL (don't break links) but also linked from TX hub - Existing MN post stays at current URL but also linked from US hub ### What I will NOT do - Move/rename existing ATX or MN files (would break existing links) - Generate hero images (placeholder or no-image layout) - Build the full n8n workflow (will design the spec, user needs to create in n8n UI or via API) - Research X.com accounts (deferred to follow-up session) ### Session scope reality check Building 50+ seeded state pages is a LOT of content. Realistic plan for this session: 1. **Template + Texas hub + 6 TX regionals** — full content, today 2. **US hub page** — today 3. **State research** — batch via parallel agents (5-10 states per agent) 4. **State page generation** — may span into next session if context runs low 5. **n8n workflow spec** — document, not implementation 6. **SFTP deploy** — whatever is ready ## Execution Order 1. Create directory structure: `/blog/ice-activity/` and `/blog/ice-activity/texas/` 2. Create the shared HTML template (header, nav, sidebar, footer as reusable blocks) 3. Build Texas hub page with statewide content 4. Build 6 Texas regional pages with researched content 5. Build US hub/index page 6. Create 49 state skeleton pages (batch — template + state-specific data) 7. Update `blog.html` to feature the tracker 8. Write n8n workflow spec document 9. Deploy via SFTP to commoncloud.cc ## Verification - Open each page locally to check rendering - Verify all internal links work - Check footnote anchors - Test mobile responsiveness (Tailwind is responsive by default) - SFTP upload and verify on https://justnice.us/blog/ice-activity/

input.plan:
# Plan: Shared Nav/Footer Build System for justNICE.us

## Context

justNICE.us has 78 static HTML pages with nav and footer copy-pasted inline. Every time a nav link is added (like ICE Tracker), all 73+ files must be edited individually. The nav/footer markup is inconsistent across pages (different active-link styles, missing `data-i18n` attributes, different footer link sets, varying contact form dropdowns).

**Goal**: Extract nav and footer into shared partials with a zero-dependency Node.js build script that injects them at deploy time. Zero client-side JS overhead — pure static HTML output.

## Architecture

### How it works
1. `_partials/` directory holds 4 shared HTML files (nav, footer-full, footer-slim, scripts)
2. Each source page has `<!-- build:config -->`, `<!-- build:nav -->`, `<!-- build:footer -->`, `<!-- build:scripts -->` markers
3. `build.js` (root, ~70 lines, zero deps) reads partials, finds all HTML files, replaces markers, writes to `dist/`
4. GitHub Actions runs `node build.js` before SCP deploy
5. Pages without markers are copied as-is (safe incremental migration)

### Per-page configuration

Each page declares its config in an HTML comment after `<body>`:
```html
<body class="font-sans text-gray-900 bg-white">
<!-- build:config {"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"} -->

<!-- build:nav -->

  <!-- breadcrumbs and page content stay here -->

<!-- build:footer -->
<!-- build:scripts -->
</body>
```

**`nav` values**: `"home"`, `"resources"`, `"kyr"`, `"tracker"`, `"blog"`, `"downloads"` — controls which nav item gets the active underline style
**`footer` values**: `"full"` (donate CTA + contact form + about + copyright) or `"slim"` (about + copyright only)

### Active link handling

The nav partial uses `{{ACTIVE:key}}` tokens in each link's class attribute. The build script replaces the matching token with `underline underline-offset-4 decoration-2` and strips all non-matching tokens. This standardizes the active style across all pages.

### Directory structure
```
justNICE.us/
  _partials/
    nav.html              # Full <header> with desktop + mobile nav
    footer-full.html      # Donate CTA + contact form + about + nav links + copyright
    footer-slim.html      # About + nav links + copyright (no form)
    scripts.html          # Mobile menu toggle + contact form JS + translations init
  build.js                # Node.js build script (zero deps)
  dist/                   # Build output (deployed, .gitignored)
  .github/workflows/deploy.yml  # Updated with build step
```

## Implementation Steps

### Phase 1: Create build infrastructure
1. Create `_partials/nav.html` — extract canonical nav from `index.html`, standardize:
   - All 7 nav items with `data-i18n` attributes
   - `{{ACTIVE:key}}` tokens on each link
   - `h-12` logo (standardized)
   - `/#contact-form` for contact link (works everywhere)
   - Both desktop and mobile navs in one file
2. Create `_partials/footer-full.html` — extract from `index.html`, standardize:
   - Donate CTA section
   - Contact form with all categories (ICE Activity Report, General, Immigrant Resources, KYR, Blog, Other)
   - About section with standardized footer nav links (Resources, KYR, ICE Tracker, Blog, Downloads, NICE Gear, Contact, Donate)
   - Copyright bar with justnice.us, email, GitHub, GD org credit
   - All elements get `data-i18n` attributes
3. Create `_partials/footer-slim.html` — footer without contact form or donate CTA
4. Create `_partials/scripts.html` — mobile menu toggle, contact form handler, translations init
5. Create `build.js` at project root
6. Add `dist/` to `.gitignore`

### Phase 2: Update GitHub Actions
- Add Node.js setup step (`actions/setup-node@v4`, node 20)
- Add `node build.js` step
- Add asset copy step (copy non-HTML files to `dist/`)
- Change SCP source to `dist/*` with `strip_components: 1`

### Phase 3: Migrate top-level pages (5 pages)
For each of: `index.html`, `blog.html`, `downloads.html`, `immigrant-resources.html`, `kyr-ice-training.html`:
1. Add `<!-- build:config {"nav": "...", "footer": "..."} -->` after `<body>`
2. Replace `<header>...</header>` (including mobile menu div) with `<!-- build:nav -->`
3. Replace `<footer>...</footer>` and trailing scripts with `<!-- build:footer -->` + `<!-- build:scripts -->`
4. Keep breadcrumbs and page content intact
5. Remove duplicated inline scripts (menu toggle, form handler, translation init)

Config per page:
- `index.html`: `{"nav": "home", "footer": "full"}`
- `blog.html`: `{"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"}`
- `downloads.html`: `{"nav": "downloads", "footer": "slim"}`
- `immigrant-resources.html`: `{"nav": "resources", "footer": "full"}`
- `kyr-ice-training.html`: `{"nav": "kyr", "footer": "full"}`

### Phase 4: Migrate ICE tracker pages (56 pages)
Write a one-time migration script that:
1. Finds all `blog/ice-activity/*.html` and `blog/ice-activity/texas/*.html`
2. Strips `<header>...</header>` block and replaces with `<!-- build:nav -->`
3. Strips `<footer>...</footer>` and trailing scripts, replaces with `<!-- build:footer -->` + `<!-- build:scripts -->`
4. Inserts `<!-- build:config {"nav": "tracker", "footer": "full"} -->` after `<body>`

All get `{"nav": "tracker", "footer": "full"}`.

### Phase 5: Migrate blog posts (~15 pages)
Same migration approach. All get `{"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"}`.

### Phase 6: Verify and deploy
1. Run `node build.js` locally
2. Verify `dist/` output: spot-check 5 pages (index, a state page, a blog post, downloads, kyr)
3. Check all pages have correct nav, footer, active links, `data-i18n` attributes
4. Commit and push — GitHub Actions builds and deploys
5. Purge Cloudflare cache
6. Verify live pages

## Key files to modify
- `index.html` — reference for extracting canonical nav/footer partials; then strip inline nav/footer
- `downloads.html` — reference for slim footer; then strip
- `blog/ice-activity/texas.html` — representative state page for bulk migration script
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — add build step
- `blog/*.html` (15 files) — strip inline nav/footer
- `blog/ice-activity/*.html` + `blog/ice-activity/texas/*.html` (56 files) — strip inline nav/footer

## New files to create
- `_partials/nav.html`
- `_partials/footer-full.html`
- `_partials/footer-slim.html`
- `_partials/scripts.html`
- `build.js`
- `.gitignore` (add `dist/`)

## New page template

Future pages only need `<head>` + page content:
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en" class="scroll-smooth">
<head>
  <!-- meta, title, Tailwind CDN, fonts -->
</head>
<body class="font-sans text-gray-900 bg-white">
<!-- build:config {"nav": "blog", "footer": "full"} -->
<!-- build:nav -->

  <main>
    <!-- your content -->
  </main>

<!-- build:footer -->
<!-- build:scripts -->
</body>
</html>
```

## Verification
- `node build.js` produces `dist/` with all 78 pages
- Every page in `dist/` has the canonical nav and footer HTML (no `{{ACTIVE:...}}` tokens remaining)
- Active links are correctly highlighted per page
- `data-i18n` attributes present on all nav/footer elements
- Contact form submits to `https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/justnice-contact`
- Mobile menu toggle works
- Language switcher works
- No broken links in nav or footer
- GitHub Actions deploys successfully
- Live site renders correctly on desktop and mobile
